Going beyond single-origin, they champion single varietal estate coffees, inviting a profound exploration into how a specific varietal, cultivated on a single farm, shapes the final taste in your cup.

The café's minimalist, "laboratory" aesthetic, with its pristine white walls, creates a focused environment for tasting. Here, attention to detail is "OCD to the extreme," even extending to vacuuming grinders between each single-origin brew to prevent any flavor contamination. To ensure the purest expression of their meticulously sourced, fully traceable coffees, a strict no-milk policy is observed for alternative brewing methods. This dedication to purity, precision, and transparency defines the soul of this iconic Cape Town destination, offering an unparalleled journey into the world of specialty coffee.
address: 8 De Smidt St, Green Point, Cape Town, 8005, South Africa

name: Bean There Coffee Company - Cape Town (by appointment only)
excerpt_short: SA's pioneer of Direct Fair Trade African coffee, ethically sourced & community-focused.
excerpt_long: Crafting the soul of African coffee. South Africa's first Direct Fair Trade roaster, dedicated to ethical sourcing & community.
summary: South Africa's pioneer of Direct Fair Trade, this roastery exclusively sources premium single-origin African coffees, building deep producer relationships and fostering community with every ethically crafted cup.
flavour_profile: African single-origin, Direct Fair Trade, Olga's Reserve, community.
about:
The Heart of African Coffee: Pioneering Direct Fair Trade

Founded in 2005 by the visionary brother-and-sister duo Jonathan and Sarah Robinson, this specialty coffee roaster began in Jonathan's garage, fueled by an inspiring 27,000 km road trip. Jonathan's discovery of Fair Trade coffee sparked a mission to pioneer the Direct Fair Trade model in South Africa. This commitment ensures personal relationships with African producers, paying premiums that foster community development.

As South Africa's first roaster of Certified Fairtrade coffee, their dedication to ethical sourcing and quality shines through.Exclusively sourcing and roasting single-origin coffees from the rich landscapes of Ethiopia, Kenya, Rwanda, Burundi, Tanzania, and the DRC, they bring the true soul of African coffee to every cup. A standout is 'Olga's Reserve,' a rare micro-lot dedicated to the founders' grandmother, who first ignited their passion.

Their cafes feature a central coffee bar island, creating a dramatic stage where baristas craft each drink, embodying a vibrant, community-focused atmosphere. This ethos extends to their team, with a strong commitment to paying a living wage and fostering a positive work environment.
address: W002, 23 Fairway Cl, Parow, Cape Town, 7500, South Africa

name: Deluxe Coffeeworks
excerpt_short: Punk rock soul, pure coffee, anti-frills, no Wi-Fi, genuine community.
excerpt_long: Deluxe Coffeeworks: Where punk rock spirit fuels pure coffee passion, crafting exceptional beans in anti-frills, community-focused spaces.
summary: Deluxe Coffeeworks: Where punk rock spirit fuels anti-frills cafes, fostering genuine connection over meticulously roasted, pure coffee. No Wi-Fi, just passion.
flavour_profile: Punk spirit, purist coffee, community focus, global reach.
about:
Deluxe Coffeeworks: Where Punk Rock Meets Pure Coffee Passion

Deluxe Coffeeworks was born in a backyard in 2009, founded by Carl Wessel, a former film production co-owner, and New Zealander Judd Nicolay, who brought extensive coffee experience from Havana Coffeeworks. Their journey began with a second-hand roaster, quickly cultivating a unique, anti-frills philosophy. Dedicated roaster Garreth van Eyssen, a punk rock musician with a green mohawk, embodies their unconventional spirit. Their minimalist cafes, often just a counter and a couch, famously eschew Wi-Fi to foster genuine conversation.

The original spot even became a hub for the "Vesparados" Vespa club, adding to its distinct, community-driven character.Deluxe Coffeeworks has grown organically, building a reputation for quality without a single salesperson. Their commitment extends to direct sourcing, importing green beans directly from places like Guatemala. Beyond their cafes, Carl and Judd established a global consulting arm, setting up roasteries and training baristas for luxury hotels worldwide.

This dedication to coffee integrity is perhaps best exemplified by their no-nonsense milk policy: only full-fat milk is offered, with a strong recommendation to experience their coffee black, ensuring the pure essence of their meticulously roasted beans shines through.
address: 171A Buitenkant St, Gardens, Cape Town, 8001, South Africa

name: Rosetta Roastery Cafe (Bree Street)
excerpt_short: Pioneering single-origin excellence, meticulously roasted, globally celebrated.
excerpt_long: Rosetta Roastery: Globally celebrated for pioneering single-origin excellence, meticulously curating rare, distinct coffees for unparalleled flavor.
summary: Rosetta Roastery pioneers single-origin excellence, meticulously sourcing and roasting rare, exceptional beans. Globally celebrated for their "Diversity in Singularity," they offer an unparalleled coffee journey.
flavour_profile: Direct-trade, single-origin, curated, award-winning, educational.
about:
Rosetta Roastery: Pioneering Single-Origin Excellence, Globally Celebrated

Rosetta Roastery, founded by Rob and Jono, embodies a unique soul in the specialty coffee world. Their journey began counter-intuitively with wholesale roasting, allowing them to deeply master their craft before opening a café. Driven by necessity, they pioneered their own direct-trade supply chain, sourcing rare and high-quality single-origin beans previously unavailable in South Africa.

This commitment to "Diversity in Singularity" guides their intensive curation process, where they rigorously test over 150 coffees annually, selecting only the most exceptional lots to showcase the vast spectrum of coffee's inherent flavors.As purists, Rosetta Roastery exclusively focuses on single-origin coffees, meticulously roasting each small batch to accentuate its distinct profile, never blending. Their dedication extends to freshness, employing a unique quality control method that ensures consistent flavor week after week.

This unwavering pursuit of excellence has earned them "Best Roastery in South Africa" three times, and their flagship café was recognized by the Financial Times as one of the world's best. Their café menu, a matrix of brewing methods and single-origin beans, invites customers on an educational exploration, deepening their appreciation for every perfectly crafted cup.
address: 101 Bree St, CBD, Cape Town, 8001, South Africa

name: Truth Coffee Roasting
excerpt_short: Steampunk roastery, pioneering mythical Racemosa coffee & vintage Colossus.
excerpt_long: Truth Coffee: A steampunk odyssey where rare Racemosa coffee meets vintage roasters and pioneering craft in Cape Town.
summary: Unearth rarity at Truth Coffee: a steampunk fantasy where the world's rarest Racemosa coffee (only 5kg secured!) is meticulously roasted on a vintage 1940s Colossus. A unique journey into pioneering coffee craft.
flavour_profile: Rare Racemosa, steampunk, vintage roaster, meticulous craft.
about:
Unearthing Rarity: Truth Coffee's Steampunk Odyssey

Step into Truth Coffee Roasting and be transported to a fully realized steampunk fantasy, where the romantic, steam-powered technology of coffee roasters comes alive. At its heart stands "Colossus," a vintage 1940s cast-iron roaster, meticulously rebuilt by founder David Donde, a pioneer credited with introducing specialty coffee roasting to Cape Town and co-founding SCASA.

This immersive world is home to the almost mythical Racemosa, a rediscovered coffee species so rare that in 2024, only 14 kilograms were harvested globally, with Truth securing a mere five. For such precious beans, master roasters employ stethoscopes to precisely pinpoint the "first crack," a testament to their delicate craft.Beyond the extraordinary Racemosa, Truth offers a unique "Black Honey" processed espresso, a vibrant blend of Ethiopian and El Salvadorian beans, showcasing their commitment to innovative flavors.

Their ethical sourcing ensures farmers receive premium prices, well above Fair Trade standards. The experience is further enriched by newspaper-style menus, featuring headlines about their homemade bread, and baristas in eccentric steampunk uniforms complete with leather waistcoats and goggles. Founder David Donde's flair for unique hospitality extends to "The Art of Duplicity," a secret speakeasy, cementing Truth Coffee's reputation as a truly distinctive destination in the specialty coffee world.
address: 36 Buitenkant St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town, 8000, South Africa

name: Origin Coffee Roasting
excerpt_short: SA's pioneering specialty roaster: historic roots, innovative, diverse, industry-shaping.
excerpt_long: Origin Coffee Roasters: South Africa's specialty coffee pioneer. Historic roastery, innovative processing, and industry-shaping Barista Academy.
summary: Origin Coffee Roasters: South Africa's specialty coffee genesis. The "Godfather" of SA coffee, pioneering Cape Town's scene with innovative, farm-traceable roasts, experimental processing, and a legacy of excellence.
flavour_profile: Pioneering, historic, experimental, diverse, academy, community.
about:
Origin Coffee Roasters: South Africa's Specialty Coffee Genesis

Origin Coffee Roasters, revered as the "Godfather of Specialty Coffee in South Africa," pioneered Cape Town's vibrant coffee scene. Founder Joel Singer, frustrated by the lack of quality coffee in the late '90s, established one of the country's first artisan roasters. Their historic Cape Town roastery, once a tobacco factory, embodies their deep roots.

Origin's Barista Academy has trained over 4,000 professionals, profoundly shaping the industry and producing South Africa's first-ever barista champion, Willem Pienaar.Origin's passion for exceptional coffee is evident in their diverse portfolio, sourcing and roasting approximately 50 different farm-traceable, specialty-grade coffees annually. They embrace innovation with experimental processing methods, offering intriguing selections like a whisky barrel-aged Colombian and a Rwandan coffee with 72-hour anaerobic cherry fermentation.

Their unique decaf, a single origin Red Bourbon from Colombia, uses a natural sugarcane process. This dedication to variety and quality, overseen by Head of Coffee Quality Jorge Alberto, solidifies Origin's legendary status.
address: 28 Hudson St, De Waterkant, Cape Town, 8001, South Africa

name: Solberg & Hansen Concept Store
excerpt_short: Oslo's pioneering roaster since 1879. Unique culture, sustainable, exceptional coffee.
excerpt_long: Norway's oldest roastery, Solberg & Hansen pioneers unique coffee, from Black Metal-inspired blends to climate-neutral goals.
summary: Norway's pioneering specialty roaster since 1879, Solberg & Hansen blends tradition with bold innovation. From direct trade to "Black Coffee" inspired by Norwegian Black Metal, discover unique, sustainable flavors.
flavour_profile: Pioneering, direct trade, unique varietals, metal culture, sustainable.
about:
From Oslo's Roots to Global Renown: Solberg & Hansen's Pioneering Coffee Journey

Solberg & Hansen, Norway's oldest and largest specialty coffee roastery since 1879, has profoundly shaped the nation's coffee culture, including the rise of coffee shops in the 1990s. This pioneering spirit extends to unique cultural collaborations, like their "Black Coffee" concept inspired by Norwegian Black Metal, even featuring an Ethiopia Natural coffee linked to the town of Hell.

Their unwavering commitment to direct trade ensures quality and sustainability, exemplified by their recent event hosting 21 partner farmers globally, fostering deeper connections. They also roast fossil-free, aiming for climate neutrality by 2030. A lifetime member of the prestigious Cup of Excellence, Solberg & Hansen consistently sources exceptional beans, including rare Wolisho and Kurume varietals from Ethiopia's 2400m altitudes.

Their award-winning seasonal packaging, recognized by the Sprudge Design Awards, reflects their attention to detail, while Managing Director Andreas Hertzberg's "Outstanding Contribution to the European Coffee Industry" further cements their fame. Their Mathallen Oslo concept store, opened in 2012, champions hand-brewed coffee, and their categorized offerings—SANS, TRACK, ESPRESSO, and CONCEPT—guide enthusiasts through a world of unique flavors and stories.

name: Havens kafe
excerpt_short: Fagerborg's Nordic coffee haven: multi-roaster excellence, calm vibe, local treats.
excerpt_long: Fagerborg's Nordic coffee haven: a multi-roaster gem featuring acclaimed Fuglen & Tim Wendelboe, local pastries, and a sister grocery.
summary: Oslo's Fagerborg offers a Nordic coffee haven. Enjoy acclaimed roasters like Tim Wendelboe & Fuglen in a calm, brick-clad setting, complemented by local pastries and a sister specialty grocery across the street.
flavour_profile: Fuglen, Wendelboe, Oslo's best pastries, Fagerborg.
about:
A Nordic Coffee Haven in Fagerborg

Nestled in Oslo's idyllic Fagerborg neighborhood, this specialty coffee destination exudes a calm, intimate atmosphere, marked by its distinctive brick facade adorned with climbing plants. It's a multi-roaster haven, proudly serving a curated selection from internationally acclaimed Norwegian roasters: Fuglen and Tim Wendelboe. Tim Wendelboe, a titan in the coffee world, is a former World Barista Champion (2004) and World Cup Tasting Champion (2005), with his roastery repeatedly winning the Nordic Roaster Championship.

Fuglen, co-founded by Norwegian Barista Champion Einar Kleppe Holthe, brings a unique blend of Norwegian and Japanese coffee culture, recognized for its exceptional espresso and roasting.Beyond the exceptional brews, the café offers beans from both roasters for home brewing.

While not baking in-house, a thoughtful selection of pastries from Oslo's finest bakeries, including Mendel's, Gladbakst, Åpent Bakeri, and Lyds Gluten Free, perfectly complements the coffee. Adding to its unique character, a sister business, Havens Kolonial, a specialty grocery store brimming with fresh produce and delicacies, is conveniently located directly across the street. This establishment is more than a café; it's a vibrant hub celebrating the pinnacle of Nordic coffee and local craftsmanship.

name: Erlik Kaffe (=KAFFE)
excerpt_short: Erlik Kaffe: Specialty coffee, empowering lives, brewing hope.
excerpt_long: Erlik Kaffe: Brewing hope & exceptional specialty coffee. A social enterprise empowering individuals through barista training.
summary: Erlik Kaffe: a unique social enterprise brewing hope and exceptional specialty coffee. We empower individuals with substance abuse backgrounds through barista training and quality collaboration.
flavour_profile: Social impact, expert baristas, community, award-winning coffee.
about:
Erlik Kaffe: Brewing Hope, One Cup at a Time

Erlik Kaffe isn't just a coffee shop; it's a vibrant social enterprise with a profound mission. Owned by the Stiftelsen Erlik (=Oslo Foundation), the force behind the renowned "=Oslo" street magazine, this café provides vital employment and expert barista training to individuals with backgrounds of substance abuse. Since its 2017 inception, Erlik Kaffe has generated over 30,000 hours of paid work for more than 50 baristas, fostering an inclusive environment where diverse life experiences converge.

Their commitment to quality is evident in their collaboration with the historic Norwegian specialty coffee roastery, Solberg & Hansen, ensuring every cup is crafted with expertise and care.

The unique soul of Erlik Kaffe has garnered significant attention, earning recognition from Lonely Planet as one of Oslo's finest coffee bars. Its welcoming atmosphere has even hosted Norway's Prime Minister and the Crown Prince and Princess. Beyond their public cafés, including a mobile coffee bike gifted by the Kavli Foundation, Erlik Kaffe also operates internal coffee bars for major corporate law firms. The journey of its creation and the training of its first baristas were famously documented in a television series by Petter Nyquist, further cementing its place as a beacon of hope and exceptional coffee.

name: Papegøye
excerpt_short: Oslo's inclusive plant-based coffee, award-winning craft, and best cinnamon rolls.
excerpt_long: Oslo's plant-based coffee haven: expert baristas, inclusive vibe, and home to the city's best cinnamon rolls. Experience craft & community.
summary: Oslo's plant-based coffee haven: curated Norwegian roasts, national team craft, and the city's best cinnamon rolls. An inclusive space blending specialty coffee with minimalist Scandinavian style.
flavour_profile: Oat milk, Norwegian craft, best rolls, inclusive, Scandinavian retail
about:
Craft, Community, and Oslo's Best Cinnamon Rolls

Nestled in Oslo's vibrant Grønland neighborhood, this specialty coffee haven redefines the café experience. Committed to a fully plant-based ethos, every exquisite coffee drink is exclusively crafted with oat milk. Far from a typical roastery, the café prides itself on a meticulously curated, rotating selection of hand-picked coffees from esteemed Norwegian roasters like Solberg & Hansen, with offerings changing every few days.

This dedication to sourcing the finest beans is matched by unparalleled expertise; three employees proudly competed on the Norwegian national coffee team in 2022, a testament to their high level of craft.Beyond the exceptional coffee, this establishment cultivates a truly unique soul and character. It's an openly inclusive space, proudly LGBTQ+ and transgender-friendly, featuring gender-neutral toilets and full wheelchair accessibility, fostering a welcoming environment for all.

While coffee is a draw, many locals and visitors flock here for what's widely celebrated as the best cinnamon rolls in Oslo – a must-try house specialty. Complementing the coffee bar is a thoughtfully curated retail concept, offering clothing and accessories that embody minimalist Scandinavian design, blending specialty coffee with a distinctive lifestyle.

name: Lille Oslo Kaffebrenneri
excerpt_short: Lille Oslo: Hygge, hand-roasted small-batch coffee, roasting theater, quiet oasis.
excerpt_long: Lille Oslo Kaffebrenneri: A hygge haven where hand-roasted, small-batch Scandinavian light roasts and unique cold brews create a serene escape.
summary: Lille Oslo Kaffebrenneri: A hygge haven where hand-roasted, small-batch specialty coffee is crafted before your eyes. Savor Scandinavian light roasts and personalized service in our cozy, rustic escape. Pure coffee magic.
flavour_profile: Hand-roasted, light Scandinavian, hygge, backyard oasis.
about:
Lille Oslo Kaffebrenneri: A Hygge Haven of Hand-Roasted Excellence

Lille Oslo Kaffebrenneri, once an extension of the beloved "My Ugly Baby" donut bakery, has blossomed into an independent café and roastery, offering a truly unique coffee experience. Here, the aroma of freshly roasted beans mingles with the cozy "hygge" ambiance, characterized by dim lighting and a rustic charm. Guests are invited into an intimate setting where the roasting machine shares the seating area, allowing for an up-close view of the meticulous, hand-roasted, small-batch process.

This dedication to craft embodies the Scandinavian light roasting style, designed to highlight the subtle, nuanced flavors inherent in each bean.Beyond the captivating roasting theater, Lille Oslo Kaffebrenneri cultivates a serene escape with its quiet backyard oasis, shared with the neighboring wine bar, "Territoriet." Their commitment to quality extends to a unique summer-special cold brew with lemon, celebrated for its subtle, layered, and almost tea-like profile.

Emphasizing a personalized touch, they offer a weekly coffee subscription and prepare coffee specifically for your machine, whether whole or ground, at the same price. The café also champions the distinct quality of Norwegian water, believing it enhances the clean, vibrant flavors of every cup.

name: Rum Baba
excerpt_short: Amsterdam roastery: education, 'Liquid Diamond' rarities & legendary marble cake.
excerpt_long: East Amsterdam's roastery: coffee education, direct trade, and rare 'Liquid Diamond' experimental lots. Don't miss the legendary cake!
summary: Unearth 'Liquid Diamonds' at our specialty roastery. We blend coffee education with rare, experimental beans, meticulous roasting, and legendary marble cake. Demystify coffee & discover unique flavors!
flavour_profile: Liquid Diamond, experimental roasts, direct trade, workshops, bakery.
about:
Where Coffee Education Meets Liquid Diamonds

Step into a world where specialty coffee is an open book, not a secret society. This vibrant roaster champions coffee education, hosting free monthly workshops and open cuppings that demystify everything from latte art to bean sourcing. Their commitment extends to direct trade, ensuring fair prices for farmers, while their dedicated roastery meticulously refines each batch.

With playful, creative packaging, they invite everyone to explore the fun side of high-quality coffee, making the journey from farm to cup an approachable adventure, guided by passionate baristas eager to share their knowledge.Their innovative spirit shines through exclusive creations like "La Push," developed specifically for the Dutch AeroPress Championship, showcasing their competitive edge.

Dive deeper into their "Liquid Diamond Collection," a treasure trove of premium and rare coffees, including experimental lots boasting unconventional notes like "Skittle'icous." Beyond the brew, their East Amsterdam location delights with an in-house bakery, home to a legendary marble cake, perfectly complementing their passion for sharing exceptional flavors and fostering a deeper appreciation for the craft.

name: Talormade
excerpt_short: Precision roasts, playful flavors, and vibrant, Willy Wonka-style cafes.
excerpt_long: Serious coffee craft meets whimsical wonder. Expect expertly roasted, unique flavors in a vibrant, "Willy Wonka" world.
summary: Serious coffee craft meets playful wonder. Experience world-class roasting and "wacky" specialty flavors in a vibrant, unpretentious haven.
flavour_profile: Expertly roasted, experimental, fun, inclusive, global, unique.
about:
Talormade: Where World-Class Roasting Meets Whimsical Wonder

Step into the vibrant world of Talormade, a specialty coffee haven born from the vision of Talor Browne, a 20-year industry veteran and certified Q Grader. Her illustrious career includes shaping the coffee landscape as head roaster for Tim Wendelboe, crafting beans for World Barista Champions and Michelin-starred restaurants like Noma.

This unparalleled expertise is now poured into Talormade's high-volume operation, where a state-of-the-art Loring S15 Falcon roaster meticulously coaxes nuanced flavors from over 700kgs of coffee weekly, ensuring precision and consistency in every roast.Talormade's soul is a delightful blend of serious coffee craft and playful experimentation.

Known for "wacky" offerings like the "Cheese and Crackers" Uganda, a unique natural/semi-anaerobic coffee with a 12-hour fermentation, they challenge perceptions while maintaining an approachable, fun ethos. Their "Willy Wonka-style acid trip" cafes, bursting with kaleidoscopic colors, perfectly complement their gourmet doughnut pairings. This unique character, coupled with rapid international growth, has cultivated a welcoming community around exceptional coffee, free from pretension.

name: Java Espressobar
excerpt_short: Norway's pioneering roastery, founded by a World Barista Champion, setting global standards.
excerpt_long: From Norway's 1st World Barista Champion, a pioneering roastery defining specialty coffee, bringing exceptional, directly sourced beans to all.
summary: Founded by 2000 World Barista Champion Robert William Kallhovd Thoresen, this pioneering Norwegian roastery set the standard for specialty coffee, bringing exceptional quality from farm to cup and to wider audiences.
flavour_profile: Pioneering, Champion-led, Direct Sourcing, Innovative Farms
about:
Norway's Coffee Revolution: A Champion's Enduring Legacy

At the heart of this pioneering roastery lies the vision of Robert William Kallhovd Thoresen, the first-ever World Barista Champion in 2000. His journey, sparked by the burgeoning American third-wave coffee culture during his time as an architect in San Francisco, laid the groundwork for what would become one of Norway's foundational specialty coffee roasteries.

Collaborating with influential coffee professional Trish Rothgeb, credited with coining "third wave coffee," they meticulously crafted a foundational flavor profile and roasting style that set a new standard, paving the way for internationally renowned Norwegian roasters.This commitment to excellence extends to their sourcing, driven by The Collaborative Coffee Source, their dedicated green coffee company.

Through this initiative, they forge direct, sustainable relationships with top-tier producers worldwide, fostering connections with innovative farms like Colombia's La Palma & El Tucan. Their dedication to quality and accessibility culminated in 2017 when they became the first small, independent "third-wave" roastery in Norway to feature their exceptional coffee in a major national supermarket chain, bringing specialty coffee to a wider audience while maintaining their unique soul and character.

name: Kuro
excerpt_short: Kuro: Oslo's serene blend of Japanese coffee, fashion, and day-to-night elegance.
excerpt_long: Kuro: Oslo's unique blend of Japanese coffee culture, Scandinavian design, and fashion. Specialty coffee by day, wine by night. Curated brews & bites.
summary: Kuro: Oslo's unique blend of Japanese coffee culture, Scandinavian design, and high-end fashion. A serene coffee haven by day, sophisticated wine bar by night, showcasing curated Norwegian roasters.
flavour_profile: Japanese-Nordic design, curated coffee, fashion, wine bar.
about:
Kuro: Oslo's Black Canvas of Coffee & Culture

Step into Kuro, where the Japanese word for "black" defines an experience inspired by founder Nicolas Jara Marthinsen's immersion in Japanese coffee culture. This unique café, an extension of the Norwegian fashion brand F5, seamlessly blends high-end fashion with specialty coffee. Its interior is a serene fusion of Scandinavian minimalism and Japanese elegance, featuring natural wood furniture and creamy white paper lamps, creating a tranquil escape.

By day, Kuro is a specialty coffee haven; by night, it transforms into a sophisticated wine bar, embodying a dual identity that caters to diverse tastes.Kuro distinguishes itself with a meticulously curated coffee program, foregoing in-house roasting to showcase esteemed Norwegian roasters like Kokko from Stavanger and Fuglen from Oslo.

Their creative seasonal menus are a highlight, famously featuring a non-alcoholic Irish coffee crafted with whisky barrel-aged beans. Beyond coffee, premium teas like Kagoshima Kabusecha are served with exquisite Kinto ware, while pastries and cakes from independent local bakers offer unique delights rarely found elsewhere in Oslo. Nicolas, cousin to the F5 brand owners, ensures a distinctive and memorable visit.

name: Fuglen
excerpt_short: Fuglen: Global Nordic coffee pioneer, vintage design, coffee & cocktails.
excerpt_long: Fuglen blends Nordic light-roast coffee, vintage design, and cocktails, pioneering a global "Japandi" experience from Oslo to Tokyo.
summary: Fuglen: Pioneer of Nordic light-roast coffee in Asia, blending a vibrant cafe with a cocktail bar. Discover vintage Scandinavian design (for sale!) and "Japandi" aesthetics globally.
flavour_profile: Nordic roast, vintage design, direct trade, Japandi fusion
about:
Fuglen: A Global Flight of Nordic Coffee & Vintage Design

Step into Fuglen, where a unique dual-concept experience seamlessly blends a vibrant specialty coffee shop by day with a classic cocktail bar by night. Each location is a meticulously curated haven of vintage Norwegian and Scandinavian design from the 1950s and 60s, with every exquisite piece available for purchase, embodying a "Japandi" fusion of Scandinavian aesthetics and Japanese minimalism.

True to its name, "Fuglen" (Norwegian for "bird"), and its tern logo, this brand symbolizes a migratory spirit, absorbing local cultures from its 1963 Oslo origins to its global outposts in Tokyo, Seoul, and Jakarta.Fuglen stands as a pioneer, instrumental in introducing the light-roast "Nordic style" coffee to Japan and wider Asia, celebrated for highlighting the inherent fruity and floral notes of the beans.

Their roastery, founded on direct-trade principles, has evolved to source directly from farms, building on a legacy that once saw their Oslo cafe uniquely serving top Norwegian roasters like Tim Wendelboe. For the Asian market, they offer enhanced 14g single-serve drip bags, ensuring a fuller-flavored cup, all rooted in Norway's rich 18th-century coffee history.

name: Supreme Roastworks
excerpt_short: Oslo's champion roasters: natural processed coffee, unique iced drinks & Porsche collab.
excerpt_long: Oslo's champion roasters, Supreme Roastworks, excel in unique natural coffees, legendary iced drinks, and a vibrant, soulful evening atmosphere.
summary: Oslo's champion roasters, Supreme Roastworks, offers unique natural processed coffees, a Porsche-partnered ocean-view café, and transforms into an evening spot with natural wine & vinyl. Experience their distinctive soul.
flavour_profile: World Champion coffee, Porsche, natural wines, vinyl, unique iced coffee.
about:
Supreme Roastworks: Oslo's Champion Roasters with a Unique Soul

Step into the world of Supreme Roastworks, where coffee excellence is championed by co-founder Odd-Steinar Tøllefsen, the 2015 World Brewers Cup Champion – Norway's sole victor of this prestigious title. Alongside co-founder Magnus Lindskog, who roasted Tøllefsen's winning naturally processed Ethiopian coffee, they've cultivated a deep passion for exceptional beans. Starting in 2007 from a garage in Oslo's Torshov, they've grown into a renowned name, celebrated for their strong preference for natural processed coffees, often featuring multiple distinct options simultaneously – a rarity in specialty coffee.

Their Grünerløkka café invites you to witness the craft firsthand, with a 12-kilo Diedrich roaster on display. Beyond the brew, discover their legendary "Satans god iskaffe," a specialty iced coffee crowned with rose lemonade. For a unique experience, their Aker Brygge location boasts an innovative Porsche collaboration, featuring a coffee bar within a sleek studio and showroom overlooking the ocean. As evening descends, the café transforms into a vibrant hangout, serving natural wine, microbrews, and setting the mood with vinyl music, embodying a truly distinctive character.

name: Tim Wendelboe
excerpt_short: Tim Wendelboe: Purity, precision, and pioneering light roasts from farm to cup.
excerpt_long: Tim Wendelboe: A beacon of purity, precision, and pioneering light roasts, shaping specialty coffee from farm to cup with transparency.
summary: Tim Wendelboe pioneers very light roasts, revealing each bean's inherent sweetness and bright complexities. A beacon of radical transparency and sustainability, shaping coffee's future from farm to cup.
flavour_profile: Experimental farm, light roasts, transparency, single-origin.
about:
Tim Wendelboe: A Beacon of Purity and Precision in Specialty Coffee

Step into the world of Tim Wendelboe, where coffee is not just a beverage, but a meticulously crafted experience. Founded by the eponymous World Barista Champion (2004) and World Cup Tasters Champion (2005), this Oslo-based roastery is renowned for its pioneering very light roast profile, designed to unveil the inherent sweetness and bright complexities of each bean.

Their commitment to single-origin coffees is unwavering, often showcasing multiple varieties from a single farm to highlight the diverse terroir. This dedication extends to long-term relationships with producers, some dating back to 2009, fostering collaborative innovation in farming and processing.The soul of Tim Wendelboe is rooted in radical transparency, evidenced by annual reports detailing producer payments, and a deep commitment to sustainability, including an experimental farm in Colombia, Finca el Suelo, where agrochemical-free cultivation is explored.

The minimalist espresso bar in Oslo offers a purist coffee journey, free from distractions, while their influence reaches global kitchens with custom roasts for Michelin-starred restaurants like Noma. Tim Wendelboe isn't just a roastery; it's a philosophy, shaping the future of specialty coffee from farm to cup.

name: Sango Specialty Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Historic Dutch micro-roastery, award-winning coffee, unique experimental brews.
excerpt_long: Sango: Historic Dutch building, award-winning roaster, screen-free haven for unique, meticulously crafted coffees & genuine connection.
summary: Sango: A screen-free micro-roastery in a 1670 Dutch building, where award-winning craft meets connection. Enjoy exclusive competition lots and experimental coffees, meticulously prepared, often with a greeting from Mocha.
flavour_profile: Competition-grade coffee, experimental roasts, historic ambiance, screen-free.
about:
Sango: Where Craft Meets Connection in Every Cup

Step into Sango, a unique specialty coffee haven nestled within a historic 1670 Dutch building, where the aroma of freshly roasted beans mingles with an atmosphere designed for genuine connection. Co-founder Mitchell La Grouw Onuki, a celebrated figure, secured second place in the 2025 Dutch Brewers Cup. This micro-roastery, with its 1.5 kg roaster visible through a glass floor panel, dedicates itself to small-batch perfection. Guests are encouraged to disconnect in this screen-free, smoke-free sanctuary, fostering a social environment, often greeted by Mocha, the resident dog by the La Marzocco.

Sango's commitment to exceptional coffee shines through its exclusive "competition lots" rarely found elsewhere. Mitchell's award-winning blend, featuring coffees from Rodrigo Sanchez, Edwin Noreña, and Wilder Lazo, exemplifies their meticulous approach. The menu features highly specific and experimental coffees, such as a Colombian anaerobic processed coffee with captivating strawberry notes.

Each pour-over is meticulously prepared using specialized Origami drippers, ensuring a sublime experience. Even the house rules, like refraining from excessive fragrances, contribute to an unadulterated appreciation of the coffee's nuanced flavors.

name: Five Ways Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Where heritage meets innovation: unique coffees, roasted fresh, in a jewelry shop.
excerpt_long: Discover rare Red Honey & Daterra coffees, roasted fresh weekly. Enjoy unique brunch in our former jewelry shop, where heritage meets innovation.
summary: Experience unique 'Red Honey' & 'tree dried Natural' coffees, roasted fresh weekly. Our distinctive café, a former jewelry shop, blends heritage and innovation for an unparalleled coffee sanctuary.
flavour_profile: Unique processes, fresh roasts, sustainable, distinctive brunch.
about:
Where Heritage Meets Innovation: A Coffee Sanctuary

Step into a world where every cup tells a story, from the unique "Red Honey" processed Ethiopian gem to the exquisite "tree dried Natural" from Brazil's Daterra estate. This specialty coffee haven prides itself on a meticulously curated portfolio, including a distinctive "Sugarcane" decaffeinated Colombian offering, ensuring a remarkable experience for every palate.

With a commitment to freshness, all coffee is roasted to order weekly, then delivered in innovative, climate-friendly packaging, reflecting a deep dedication to sustainability from bean to brew.Beyond the exceptional coffee, discover a café experience unlike any other, with one location nestled within a former jewelry shop, its architectural echoes adding a unique charm.

Indulge in a distinctive brunch menu featuring delights like Turkish eggs, or explore a curated selection of high-end coffee equipment, including La Marzocco espresso machines and Bentwood grinders, for the ultimate home brewing setup. This is more than just a coffee shop; it's a destination where passion for quality and a unique character converge.

name: Screaming Beans
excerpt_short: Artful design, slow coffee mastery, and a vibrant creative community.
excerpt_long: Screaming Beans: A micro-roastery where design, culinary art, and meticulously crafted slow coffee converge for creative and tech innovators.
summary: Screaming Beans: A micro-roastery blending design, culinary artistry, and expert "slow coffee" craft. We fuel creative and tech sectors with exceptional brews and a uniquely vibrant, design-forward culture.
flavour_profile: Michelin design, micro-roast, slow brew, creative hub.
about:
Screaming Beans: Where Craft Meets Creative Culture

Founded in 2010 by baristas Charlotte Bubeck and Dick van den Heuvel, Screaming Beans quickly became a beacon of exceptional design and culinary vision. Their pilot store, a groundbreaking collaboration with renowned Belgian architect Glenn Sestig and Michelin-starred chef Jacob Jan Boerma, set a high standard. This commitment to artistry extends to co-founder and creative director Perre, also of Foster & Kin, ensuring a distinct aesthetic.

Screaming Beans specifically targets and supplies coffee solutions to businesses in the creative and technological sectors, cultivating a unique, design-forward atmosphere.As a dedicated micro-roastery, Screaming Beans offers a diverse "slow coffee" experience, mastering methods from V60, Aeropress, and French Press to Clever and Chemex, ensuring a meticulously crafted brew.

The team's expertise is evident; store manager David, "Bayreesta" on Instagram, is a recognized coffee influencer. This vibrant community spirit is further embodied by a staff member who began as a daily customer and now works in the kitchen. Even their social media manager, a barista, doesn't drink coffee – a quirky detail adding to the unique soul of this creative coffee haven.

name: The Coffee District
excerpt_short: Oud Zuid's bright, pioneering coffee haven. Community, courses, curated excellence.
excerpt_long: Pioneering Oud Zuid's coffee scene, this bright Scandinavian haven offers curated roasts, expert courses, and a vibrant community.
summary: Pioneering Oud Zuid's coffee scene, this bright Scandinavian-inspired cafe is a dynamic hub for coffee exploration, education, and community. Discover rotating roasters, expert courses, and curated gear.
flavour_profile: Pioneering, guest roasters, courses, Scandinavian design, retail.
about:
Oud Zuid's Bright Beacon: Crafting Community and Coffee Excellence

Born from a humble hole-in-the-wall within a fashion store in 2017, Rosa and Adil Loukane's vision blossomed into a standalone specialty cafe in 2019, pioneering the vibrant coffee scene in Amsterdam's previously overlooked Oud Zuid neighborhood. This inviting space immediately captivates with its distinct, bright Scandinavian modern interior, creating an atmosphere of serene sophistication.

It's a testament to their dedication to elevating the daily coffee ritual, establishing a true haven for discerning palates and curious minds alike.Beyond its striking aesthetics, the cafe is a dynamic hub for coffee exploration. Featuring a rotating selection from esteemed guest roasters across Europe, alongside their main Amsterdam-based supplier, Five Ways Coffee Roasters, there's always something new to discover, expertly prepared on their classic white La Marzocco Linea PB.

The commitment to education shines through their popular coffee roasting and brewing courses, while regular guest barista shifts foster a strong community spirit. A meticulously curated retail shop, stocked with high-end equipment from brands like Fellow and Moccamaster, ensures the pursuit of coffee excellence continues long after you leave.

name: Monks Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Amsterdam's green heart: sustainable, ethically sourced, community-focused coffee.
excerpt_long: Amsterdam's pioneering roastery: ethically sourced, meticulously roasted, entirely plastic-free, and community-driven since 2016.
summary: Amsterdam's pioneering, plastic-free specialty coffee roastery, founded by Patrick Abbott, meticulously roasts ethically sourced beans and fosters community with sustainable practices.
flavour_profile: Zero-waste, ethical beans, unique roasts, coffee education.
about:
Amsterdam's Green Heart of Specialty Coffee

Founded in 2016 by Irishman Patrick Abbott, who brought his 25 years of Australian experience to Amsterdam, this specialty coffee haven embodies a pioneering spirit. From its inception, the roastery has been entirely plastic-free, years ahead of local regulations, showcasing a deep commitment to environmental stewardship.

Every detail, from compostable sugar cane cups to paper straws, reflects this commitment. They even offer used coffee grounds to customers for gardening or skincare, and deliver their ethically sourced beans via electric bicycle, minimizing their carbon footprint.

At the heart of their operation is a 12kg Probat roaster, where ethically sourced beans are meticulously roasted in-house to highlight their unique characteristics. The focus remains on quality and origin. Beyond exceptional coffee, they foster a vibrant community through fun, jargon-free, hands-on specialty coffee classes, welcoming everyone from curious beginners to seasoned home baristas. Sourcing fresh produce from local businesses that share their commitment to quality further cements their role as a truly conscious and community-driven establishment.

name: Scandinavian Embassy
excerpt_short: Amsterdam's Nordic coffee embassy: champion brews, innovative flavors, unique pairings.
excerpt_long: Amsterdam's award-winning Nordic coffee embassy. Champion-led, innovative brews, coffee-infused cuisine, and pioneering Scandinavian pastries.
summary: Amsterdam's Nordic coffee embassy, led by a Brewers Cup champ, offers award-winning Scandinavian roasts, innovative coffee cuisine (oyster & espresso!), unique kombucha, and authentic pastries.
flavour_profile: Nordic roasters, coffee cuisine, oyster pairing, Kascucha
about:
A Nordic Coffee Embassy: Where Innovation Brews

Step into Amsterdam's unique 'embassy' for Scandinavian specialty coffee, a place where Nordic excellence meets innovative spirit. Co-founded by Nicolas Castagno, a former Swedish Brewers Cup champion (2013), this café embodies unparalleled expertise and a commitment to quality. Its groundbreaking approach quickly garnered international acclaim, being named 'Best New Café' in the world by Sprudge in 2015.

The coffee program exclusively features a rotating selection of beans from top Nordic roasters like The Coffee Collective, Koppi, Drop Coffee, and Tim Wendelboe, meticulously brewed with advanced technology such as the Modbar under-counter espresso system, reflecting a sleek Scandinavian aesthetic.Beyond exceptional brews, the culinary experience is equally pioneering. Discover innovative coffee-infused cuisine, where coffee is a key ingredient in savory dishes, like pickled green coffee beans in salad dressing.

For the adventurous, a signature oyster and espresso pairing offers a curated taste experience. They also developed the unique 'Kascucha' cascara kombucha and serve a rare coffee flower tea. Complementing these are authentic, freshly handmade Scandinavian pastries, including cardamom and cinnamon buns, which they pioneered in Amsterdam, all thoughtfully paired with specific coffees to enhance every flavor.

name: Uncommon Cafe
excerpt_short: Uncommon stories, rare micro-lots, unique events, fresh roasts, deep connections.
excerpt_long: Discover rare micro-lots & uncommon stories. A vibrant hub where every cup is an adventure, celebrating producers & craft.
summary: A vibrant hub where curiosity brews. Discover uncommon stories in every cup of our rare, experimental micro-lots, freshly roasted Tuesdays. Experience unique culinary journeys and producer craft.
flavour_profile: Foraged events, rare varietals, producer stories, craft.
about:
Uncommon Stories, Exceptional Coffee

More than just a coffee destination, this establishment is a vibrant hub where curiosity thrives and every cup tells a story. They host unique "Foraged & Fermented Evenings," seamlessly blending coffee culture with other culinary explorations, fostering an atmosphere of discovery. Their ethos is deeply rooted in storytelling, transforming each brew into a homage to the producers and a window into their "uncommon stories" of creativity and resilience.

This commitment extends to collaborations, like the exquisite handmade ceramics with artist Julia Saldaña, underscoring a dedication to craft and aesthetics that transcends the coffee itself.The coffee selection is a testament to their adventurous spirit, featuring a seasonal rotation of rare and experimental micro-lots. Expect innovative processing methods and newly discovered varieties, such as a remarkable Hybrid Washed Geisha from Peruvian producer Bill Klinton.

Their dedication to freshness is paramount, with all coffee orders roasted and shipped exclusively on Tuesdays. This meticulous approach is underpinned by close, direct relationships with coffee producers, allowing them to witness and share the profound environmental and socio-economic challenges faced at origin, bringing deeper meaning to every sip.

name: LOT61 Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Lot61: Amsterdam's pioneering roaster, global roots, circularity, unique drinks.
excerpt_long: Lot61: Amsterdam's pioneering circular coffee roastery, globally inspired, crafting innovative drinks with a commitment to sustainable sourcing.
summary: Lot61: Amsterdam's pioneering specialty roastery, globally inspired and deeply committed to circularity, poly-farming, and innovative coffee experiences.
flavour_profile: Pioneer, circular coffee, poly-farming, seasonal drinks, global ethos.
about:
Lot61: A Global Journey of Coffee, Community, and Circularity

Adam Craig, a founder celebrated by the New York Times for establishing three successful coffee bars in NYC, brought his visionary spirit to Amsterdam, opening Lot61 in 2013. Alongside his childhood friend from Sydney, they pioneered the city's third-wave coffee scene, introducing the specialty espresso bar and roastery concept when it was still a novelty.

Their journey, deeply influenced by witnessing plastic pollution and dying coral reefs in Australia, ignited a passion to create a business with a profound positive impact. This ethos is perfectly encapsulated in their brand's soul: "Born in Sydney. Raised in Brooklyn. Roasting in Amsterdam."Lot61 is on a mission to become a "circular coffee brand," championing the responsible use of natural resources for future generations. This commitment extends to their sourcing, where they prioritize relationships with producers practicing "poly-farming" – a method that naturally regenerates soil by cultivating diverse crops.

Pushing beverage boundaries, Lot61 collaborates with a renowned mixologist to craft innovative 'Seasonal Drinks,' offering a unique twist on their specialty coffee experience. Their dedication to quality, innovation, and environmental stewardship defines their distinctive character in the global coffee landscape.

name: Back to Black
excerpt_short: Amsterdam's pioneering female-led roastery: experimental, sustainable, community-focused.
excerpt_long: Back to Black: Amsterdam's pioneering female-led roastery, crafting unique, sustainable coffees & a vibrant community.
summary: Pioneering Amsterdam's specialty coffee, Back to Black is a female-led roastery exploring unique processing & sustainability. Enjoy "no-nonsense" quality in our vibrant community hub.
flavour_profile: Female-led, experimental roasts, SCA training, sustainable, local.
about:
Back to Black: Amsterdam's Pioneering Spirit in Specialty Coffee

Step into Back to Black, a true gem in Amsterdam's specialty coffee scene, founded in 2014 by friends Noortje Vlutters and Inge Bulthuis. A rarity as a female-led venture in a male-dominated industry, they quickly became early adopters of in-house roasting in 2015, with Inge emerging as one of the few female roasters in the Dutch capital, mastering their Giesen W6.

Their "no-nonsense" philosophy champions unadulterated, high-quality coffee, a commitment further solidified by their status as an official Specialty Coffee Association (SCA) training academy, shaping the next generation of coffee professionals.Beyond their foundational ethos, Back to Black's roastery actively explores unique and experimental processing methods, offering intriguing selections like a Natural Anaerobic Arabica from India and a washed anaerobic from Burundi fermented with Oro yeast.

This adventurous spirit is matched by a profound dedication to sustainability, evident in everything from green energy to investments in permaculture on coffee farms. Noortje oversees their in-house bakery, crafting delicious treats with organic, local ingredients, complementing the café's distinctive, "hipster" ambiance, complete with a wooden swing. Back to Black isn't just a coffee shop; it's a community hub, passionately run by its founders.

name: Bocca Coffee
excerpt_short: Ethiopian roots, Amsterdam craft: award-winning, transparently sourced coffee.
excerpt_long: Bocca Coffee: Ethiopian roots, Amsterdam craft. Award-winning roaster using vintage Probats & custom La Marzocco for exceptional, ethically sourced coffee.
summary: Bocca Coffee: Ethiopian roots, Amsterdam craft. Ethically sourced beans, roasted in vintage 1963 Probats, served on a custom La Marzocco with local elm.
flavour_profile: Ethiopian roots, direct trade, vintage roast, award-winning.
about:
Bocca Coffee: Where Ethiopian Roots Meet Amsterdam Craft

Menno Simons, a visionary with roots in organic sesame seed trading in Ethiopia, brought his passion for ethical sourcing to the heart of specialty coffee with Bocca. His sister company, Trabocca, founded in 2003, acts as the primary conduit for an estimated 99% of Bocca's green beans, ensuring a direct and transparent supply chain—a principle Menno vocally champions against industry norms.

This commitment to quality and integrity earned Bocca the prestigious European Coffee Award for best artisan roaster in Europe in 2014.The soul of Bocca's coffee is forged in refurbished cast-iron Probat roasters from 1963, chosen for their ability to provide a uniquely constant roasting environment. This dedication to craft extends to their flagship café, where a custom-made four-group La Marzocco Strada espresso machine, featuring a captivating see-through rear panel revealing its intricate copper piping, takes center stage.

The bar and machine's side paneling, crafted from an elm tree felled in an Amsterdam storm, further ground Bocca in its local heritage. Founder Menno Simons' innovative spirit was recognized with the 'Amsterdamse Ondernemersprijs' in 2018.

name: White Label Coffee
excerpt_short: White Label: Unconventional Omni-Roast, celebrating bean diversity.
excerpt_long: White Label Coffee: Unconventional minds craft an "Omni-Roast" experience, celebrating each bean's true character beyond traditional labels.
summary: White Label Coffee: Unconventional founders craft award-winning "Omni-Roast" coffee, celebrating each bean's unique diversity without labels. A blank page for coffee excellence.
flavour_profile: Academic roots, omni-roast, community, award-winning, unique space.
about:
White Label Coffee: A Blank Page for Unconventional Excellence

At White Label Coffee, founders Francesca Grassotti (physics) and Elmer Ommkens (forensic psychology) bring an unconventional, analytical spirit to the world of specialty coffee. Their "Omni-Roast" philosophy boldly eliminates the traditional "filter" and "espresso" labels, roasting every bean on their premium Giesen roasters to celebrate its inherent diversity, regardless of brewing method.

This commitment to openness, symbolized by their "White Label" name, extends to fostering a vibrant local café culture built on mutual support and shared knowledge, rather than competition.This unique approach has garnered significant acclaim, including winning the Roast Masters competition at the Amsterdam Coffee Festival in 2019, and their barista securing the Dutch Aeropress Championship in 2015.

Experience their dedication firsthand with their exclusive single-origin espresso, meticulously pulled "double naked" for unparalleled clarity. Step into one of their Amsterdam cafés and discover a distinctive multi-level interior, featuring a striking "hump" that creates unique seating and an elevated counter, embodying their truly one-of-a-kind coffee experience.

name: DAK Showroom by Dak Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Amsterdam's Dak: funky, experimental roasts, ethically sourced, award-winning.
excerpt_long: Dak Coffee Roasters: Amsterdam's vibrant roastery crafting funky, experimental, and award-winning coffees with unique processing.
summary: Dak Coffee Roasters: Born from an Amsterdam epiphany, we craft funky, experimental coffees with innovative processing and playful names. Expect clean, bright, and sweet cups from ethically sourced, exotic varietals.
flavour_profile: Funky roasts, rare varietals, award-winning, eco-conscious.
about:
From Finance to Funky Roasts: Dak Coffee Roasters' Experimental Journey

Born from a rooftop epiphany in Amsterdam, Dak Coffee Roasters embodies a vibrant spirit, spearheaded by co-founder Louis-Philippe Boucher's pivot from finance to a passion for exceptional coffee. Their showroom and tasting room, a Barde vanVoltt design, was longlisted for the prestigious Dezeen Awards 2025, reflecting their commitment to an immersive experience.

Dak is renowned for its "funky and experimental coffees," featuring innovative processing like Mossto Carbonic Maceration, and playfully descriptive names such as "Candy Crush" and "Lychee Lassi" that hint at their unique taste profiles.Roasting on an IMF convection system, Dak achieves "clean, bright, and sweet" coffees with shorter development times, showcasing clarity in every cup.

Their dedication extends to ethical sourcing, paying premium prices for green beans, and utilizing 100% recyclable, CO2-neutral packaging. Home to rare and exotic varietals, including the highly-prized Gesha, Dak also boasts a "People's Choice" award from the Leaderboard Coffee game, a testament to their community-loved roasts.

name: Balue Café
excerpt_short: Stockholm's unique roastery: minimalist design, non-traditional coffee, Korean cakes.
excerpt_long: Stockholm's unique roastery: minimalist Japanese/Korean design, curated floral coffees, and artful Korean cakes.
summary: Stockholm's unique roastery, founded by David You, blends minimalist Japanese/Korean design with non-traditional coffee profiles (floral, tea-like) and "instagrammable" Korean cakes. A fusion of flavor and intentionality.
flavour_profile: Floral coffee, Korean fusion cakes, minimalist design, community.
about:
Stockholm's Curated Canvas: A Fusion of Flavor and Intentionality

Founded by David You, a coffee professional with over a decade of experience, this Stockholm gem is the culmination of a journey from the USA to establish a truly unique café. Evolving from a series of dynamic pop-up locations, the permanent space exudes a distinct soul and character.

Housed in an artsy, garage-like setting, its minimalist design is beautifully infused with Japanese and Korean influences, creating an inviting and visually striking environment that feels both modern and deeply rooted in cultural appreciation.Beyond its captivating aesthetic, the café offers a specialized beverage menu that extends beyond exceptional coffee to include high-quality hojicha and matcha. David's philosophy shines through in a curated selection of coffees featuring non-traditional flavor profiles, such as delicate floral, tea-like, and bright lemongrassy notes, challenging conventional palates.

Complementing these unique brews are visually appealing "cross-over Korean cakes" and other "instagrammable" sweet treats, embodying a fusion of culinary artistry and an intentional environment designed for discovery and delight.

name: Volca Coffee Roaster
excerpt_short: Volca: Latin American soul, direct trade, vibrant flavors, farm-to-cup journey.
excerpt_long: Volca Coffee Roaster: Experience the vibrant soul of Latin American coffee culture, ethically sourced and meticulously crafted.
summary: Volca Coffee Roaster: Experience the vibrant soul of Latin American coffee culture. Ethically sourced, direct-trade beans bring South & Central America's heart to every unique cup.
flavour_profile: Latin American focus, direct trade, espresso tonic, Argentine pastries, coffee education, workshops, sensorial experience.
about:
Volca Coffee Roaster: A Vibrant Tapestry of Latin American Coffee Culture

Volca Coffee Roaster offers more than just exceptional coffee; it's a vibrant celebration of South and Central American culture, music, and art. Founded by a team predominantly from these regions, Volca brings the heart of Latin America to every cup. Their commitment to direct farmer relationships ensures not only exceptional quality but also fair deals for the growers, fostering personal bonds that resonate in the rich flavors of their meticulously sourced beans.

This dedication to origin and community creates a unique soul and character that sets Volca apart, inviting guests to experience a genuine connection to the coffee's roots. Beyond their ethically sourced portfolio, Volca provides a remarkable sensorial experience. Indulge in their widely reviewed signature Espresso Tonic, crafted with organic tonic water and perfectly brewed espresso, or savor the authentic taste of Argentinean *facturas* like sweet *medialunas* and savory empanadas.

Each coffee served comes with a detailed card, inviting you to explore its specific origin, producer, and tasting notes. For those eager to deepen their appreciation, Volca hosts hands-on workshops, guiding enthusiasts through the intricate journey of coffee from farm to cup, embodying their meticulous technical approach to brewing and roasting.

name: Friedhats FUKU Cafe
excerpt_short: World-class roasts, sustainable jars, artistic labels, pure coffee passion.
excerpt_long: Friedhats: World-class coffee, sustainable soul, and artistic flair. Champion roasts, reusable jars, and vibrant art define our unique specialty experience.
summary: Friedhats: World Barista Champion Lex Wenneker's roastery. Sustainable, artistic, direct trade coffee in reusable jars. Exceptional, competition-grade brews at FUKU.
flavour_profile: Award-winning, sustainable, direct trade, unique art, FUKU.
about:
Friedhats: World-Class Coffee, Sustainable Soul, and Artistic Flair

Born from a chance meeting at "Headfirst" café, Friedhats is the brainchild of coffee luminaries Lex Wenneker and Dylan Sedgwick. Lex, a multiple-time Dutch Barista and Brewer Champion, famously clinched the World Barista Championship in 2016 and secured second place in 2018, bringing unparalleled expertise to every roast. Their journey began humbly in an old garage with a secondhand Giesen roaster, embodying their "Less marketing, more coffee" philosophy.

This commitment to quality and transparency, coupled with Lex's extensive background, including his early "De Espressobus" venture, defines their approach to specialty coffee.Friedhats stands out with its innovative spirit and dedication to sustainability. Eschewing traditional bags, they package their exceptional coffees in reusable and refillable plastic jars, a deliberate step towards environmental responsibility.

Each jar is adorned with unique, vibrant labels crafted by artist Ivo Yarns, adding an artistic dimension to their brand. Through platforms like Algrano, they champion direct trade, fostering strong relationships with producers. Their café, FUKU, offers not just their signature specialty brews but also "Super Special" competition-grade coffees, alongside a curated selection of natural wines and craft beers, creating a truly distinctive experience.
address: Bos en Lommerweg 136 HS, 1055 ED Amsterdam

name: Muttley & Jack's Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Muttley & Jack's: Stockholm archipelago's global coffee journey, direct trade, community.
excerpt_long: From Stockholm's archipelago, Muttley & Jack's hand-roasts global single-origin coffees, offering a world journey in every cup.
summary: From the Stockholm archipelago, Muttley & Jack's hand-roasts global single-origin coffees. Journey the world in your cup with direct-trade excellence, Nordic blends, and a vibrant community hub.
flavour_profile: Single-origin, global subscription, direct trade, community hub.
about:
Muttley & Jack's: A Global Coffee Journey from the Stockholm Archipelago

Muttley & Jack's, nestled in the serene Stockholm archipelago, embodies a passionate dedication to specialty coffee. This roastery meticulously sources and hand-roasts exceptional single-origin coffees from across the globe, inviting enthusiasts on a "travel the world in your coffee cup" journey through their monthly subscription service.

Their commitment extends beyond the bean, rooted in direct trade principles that foster fair and respectful partnerships with growers who share their passion for quality and environmental harmony.More than just a roastery, Muttley & Jack's cultivates a vibrant community hub with its in-house coffee bar, serving delightful breakfast, traditional Swedish fika, and lunch.

Here, coffee lovers gather to savor their diverse offerings, including the unique Nordic-style blends from their sustainability-focused Archipelago Coffee project, inspired by the beauty of Swedish nature. For those eager to explore, a curated Tasting Box showcases the roastery's favorite selections, while their coffee education and consulting services underscore a deep commitment to sharing expertise and fostering a greater appreciation for the craft.

name: Beck Kaffebar
excerpt_short: Beck: Stockholm's award-winning organic coffee, history, and artisanal soul.
excerpt_long: Beck: Stockholm's award-nominated specialty coffee haven. Organic brews, handmade cups, secret menu. History meets artisanal soul.
summary: Beck: Stockholm's specialty coffee haven. Organic brews, served in unique handmade clay cups, echo Tjärhovsgatan's history. Discover award-nominated quality and a secret menu. Where artisanal soul meets every sip.
flavour_profile: Gulddraken, organic, handmade clay, secret menu, heritage.
about:
Beck: Where History Brews with Artisanal Soul

Step into Beck, a specialty coffee haven in Stockholm where every cup tells a story. Named after the dark, tar-like residue that historically inspired its street, Tjärhovsgatan, and the rich hue of coffee itself, Beck offers an experience steeped in local character. Here, the commitment to quality shines through organic coffee, meticulously sourced from a local micro-roastery.

Each dine-in coffee is a tactile pleasure, served in unique, locally handmade clay cups that elevate the ritual, reflecting the café's deep appreciation for artisanal craftsmanship and community.Beck's distinctive charm extends beyond its exceptional brews and thoughtful presentation. Nominated for the prestigious Gulddraken 2020, a testament to its excellence in Stockholm's vibrant hospitality scene, this café invites discovery.

For those seeking an extra layer of intrigue, Beck maintains a "secret menu," a delightful invitation for customer interaction that promises off-menu delights upon request. It’s a place where the past meets the present, and every visit offers a taste of authentic, award-recognized specialty coffee culture.

name: Stora bageriet
excerpt_short: Historic Stockholm bakery: Gast's curated coffee, top roasters, artisanal pairings.
excerpt_long: Stockholm's oldest bakery meets Gast's curated coffee. Experience award-winning roasters, artisanal pairings, and historic charm.
summary: Experience Gast's curated specialty coffee in Stockholm's 300-year-old Kronobageriet, featuring a rotating selection of Sweden's top roasters alongside artisanal treats.
flavour_profile: Top Swedish roasters, historic bakery, artisanal pairings
about:
Gast's Curated Coffee Gem in Stockholm's Oldest Bakery

Step into a piece of Stockholm's history at Stora Bageriet, where the aroma of freshly baked goods mingles with the rich scent of specialty coffee. Housed within the venerable Kronobageriet, a 300-year-old structure and the city's oldest industrial building, this café offers a truly unique soul and character. The artisanal coffee program, meticulously curated by the acclaimed team behind Gast, ensures every cup is a testament to quality and expertise, drawing on their reputation for high-caliber coffee experiences.

Beyond its historic charm, Stora Bageriet is a dynamic showcase for Sweden's premier coffee talent. Eschewing in-house roasting, they feature a rotating selection from top-tier roasters, frequently highlighting internationally recognized names like Koppi and Morgon, celebrated for their award-winning beans. Whether you prefer a perfectly pulled espresso or a consistently well-extracted batch brew filter coffee, the diverse brewing options cater to every palate.

Complementing the exceptional coffee, the establishment prides itself on local and artisanal pairings, offering natural wines and craft beers from local producers alongside their exquisite baked goods and food menu, creating a holistic and authentic Swedish experience.

name: Komet Cafe
excerpt_short: Nordic charm meets French flair: homemade delights & bright specialty coffee.
excerpt_long: Nordic charm meets French flair. Handcrafted pastries & specialty coffee from local roasters in a cozy, connection-focused haven.
summary: Nordic charm meets French flair in a cozy haven. Savor meticulously crafted pastries, bright specialty coffee from local roasters, and vegetarian delights, all made with love and precision.
flavour_profile: French-Nordic baking, medium roast, house mocha, cozy, homemade.
about:
Nordic Charm, French Flair: A Coffee Haven of Homemade Delights

Step into a unique culinary sanctuary where French pastry artistry gracefully intertwines with the comforting traditions of Swedish classics and a broader Scandinavian creativity. This isn't just a café; it's a philosophy, where every single product, from the first bite to the last sip, is meticulously crafted "with love and precision." The ambiance is a testament to this ethos, offering a "minimalist yet cozy" space designed for genuine connection, underscored by a thoughtful no-laptop policy on weekends to foster a truly social atmosphere.

The coffee experience is equally thoughtful, featuring specialty beans sourced exclusively from a curated selection of local Swedish and Scandinavian roasters. Expect a medium roast profile that sings with bright, acidic notes, culminating in a truly distinctive cup. Don't miss their renowned signature mocha, a decadent creation elevated by house-made chocolate. Complement your brew with standout pastries like the highly recommended Pistachio Roll or the "perfectly spiced" Cardamom Buns. For a more substantial offering, the menu proudly features exclusively vegetarian-friendly main dishes, ensuring a delightful experience for all.

name: Sempre Espresso Bar
excerpt_short: Italian escape: robusta-rich espresso, Sicilian cannoli, Aperitivo Fridays.
excerpt_long: Your Italian escape: authentic robusta espresso, Sicilian cannoli, and vibrant Aperitivo Fridays. Coffee is our weapon!
summary: Escape to Italy at Coffeum Arma Nostra! Savor unique robusta-blend espresso, authentic Sicilian cannoli, and lively Aperitivo Fridays in a true Italian espresso bar setting.
flavour_profile: Italian Espresso Bar, Robusta Blend, Cannoli, Aperitivo, Spirits.
about:
Coffeum Arma Nostra: An Italian Escape in Every Cup

Step into a slice of Italy at this unique specialty coffee haven, where the motto "Coffeum arma nostra" – "Coffee is our weapon" – isn't just a phrase, but a philosophy. Founder Per, with a twinkle in his eye, admits his journey began with a humorous misconception of profitability, yet has cultivated a "Classical Italian espresso bar" atmosphere that transports patrons far from typical minimalist Scandinavian aesthetics.

Here, the house blend boldly defies the 100% Arabica standard, featuring robusta beans with a touch of arabica, sourced through their own import, offering a distinct and authentic Italian coffee experience.Beyond the exceptional coffee, this establishment is a culinary and social destination. Indulge in their particularly renowned, authentic Sicilian cannoli – a true signature pastry.

As evenings descend, "Aperitivo Fridays" come alive, celebrating Italian social culture with delectable appetizers, classic cocktails like Aperol Spritz, and a vibrant live house band. Unusually for a café of its type in Sweden, a curated selection of wine, spirits, and cocktails further enhances the experience, making every visit a journey to the heart of Italy.

name: Lykke Kaffegårdar
excerpt_short: Lykke Kaffegårdar: Farmer co-owned, seed-to-soul, sustainable, cultivating happiness.
excerpt_long: Lykke Kaffegårdar: Cultivating happiness from seed to soul. Farmers are co-owners, ensuring ethical, sustainable, and unique specialty coffee.
summary: Lykke Kaffegårdar cultivates happiness from seed to soul. With farmer co-ownership and direct, sustainable practices, they deliver exceptional specialty coffee, ensuring joy in every cup.
flavour_profile: Farmer co-owned, seed-to-cup, experimental, happiness-driven.
about:
Lykke Kaffegårdar: Cultivating Happiness, From Seed to Soul

Lykke Kaffegårdar, meaning "happiness" in Nordic, embodies a profound commitment to joy and ethical practices throughout the coffee journey. Co-founded by Johan Damgaard, formerly of the renowned Johan & Nyström, and with Benjamin Norman, ex-head roaster at Drop Coffee Roasters, leading their roasting, Lykke brings serious specialty coffee pedigree.

Their unique soul lies in a groundbreaking ownership model where coffee farmers are co-owners, eliminating middlemen and fostering true partnership. Lykke establishes "role model" farms in origins like Peru, El Salvador, Uganda, and Brazil, developing and sharing sustainable, organic farming methods with neighboring communities.

This vertically integrated company controls every step, from seed to roasted bean, ensuring their comprehensive sustainability standards are met, often surpassing official certifications. Their commitment extends to biodegradable packaging, produced in a solar-powered factory. Lykke actively experiments with diverse processing methods—washed, honey, natural, carbonic maceration—to unlock unique flavor profiles from their farms. Projects like Uganda's "Coffee Scout" and special "555" Can releases highlight their direct origin involvement, showcasing a dedication to quality and a truly happy coffee experience.

name: Café Pascal
excerpt_short: Stockholm's Café Pascal: Rare coffees, unique pastries, and a vibrant community hub.
excerpt_long: Café Pascal: Stockholm's award-winning roastery. Experience rare, competition-level coffees and unique pastries in a vibrant, modern community hub.
summary: Stockholm's Café Pascal: A modern roastery honoring an Armenian coffee legacy. Discover exclusive, competition-level beans and unique pastries in a vibrant, community-driven space. Experience coffee craft.
flavour_profile: Armenian heritage, rare beans, competition coffee, unique bakes
about:
Café Pascal: A Legacy of Coffee, Community, and Craft

Named after Pascal, an Armenian coffee pioneer who opened Paris's first coffee shop, Café Pascal in Stockholm carries a rich legacy. Founded by the Seropian siblings – Hosep, Jannet, and Arman – from a family steeped in gastronomy, this café quickly evolved from a multi-roaster haven to a distinguished in-house roaster.

Its sleek, modern interior, inspired by Tokyo and Bangkok's vibrant coffee scenes, creates an inviting atmosphere that has cemented its reputation as a vital hub for Stockholm's coffee community, frequently hosting local events.Café Pascal's dedication to exceptional coffee is undeniable, earning it recognition as one of the best coffee shops in the 2021 Svenska Gastronomipriset.

They consistently source and serve exclusive, rare, and competition-level lots, such as the elusive Ethiopia natural N2 Hachira from Ninety Plus or the red Gesha from Finca La Negrita, used in the Swedish Brewer's Cup. Beyond the extraordinary brews, their creative culinary offerings shine, featuring unique pastries like saffron brioche with black currant and raspberry filling, crafted in collaboration with Komet, ensuring a truly unforgettable experience.

name: Drop Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Stockholm's pioneer, championing clarity, transparency & rare varietals.
excerpt_long: Drop Coffee: Stockholm's pioneer, led by a champion roaster. We craft clear, bright cups with radical transparency and exceptional, organic beans.
summary: Drop Coffee: Led by a 3x champion roaster, we craft clear, bright cups. Experience radical transparency, exceptional organic beans, and small-batch perfection from Stockholm's specialty coffee pioneer.
flavour_profile: Award-winning roasts, transparent sourcing, bright flavors, rare varietals.
about:
Drop Coffee: Crafting Clarity, Championing Transparency

Step into the world of Drop Coffee, a true pioneer in Stockholm's specialty coffee scene since 2009, where the craft of coffee making is elevated to an art form. At its heart is co-owner and managing director, Joanna Alm, a three-time Swedish Coffee Roasting Champion and World Roasting Championship runner-up, whose expertise is literally penned in her book, "Manifest För Bättre Kaffe."

This dedication to excellence defines their unique soul, driving a singular vision for a "clear and bright cup" achieved through a consistent light-to-medium roast profile for all their exceptional coffees.Drop Coffee's character is built on radical transparency and an unwavering commitment to quality. They openly publish FOB prices for green coffee and release annual sustainability reports, fostering trust and ethical sourcing.

With a minimum SCA cupping score of 86-87 for all beans, and a fully organic certification since 2018, their portfolio features rare and experimental varietals like Anaerobic Natural SL28 and Washed Java. Roasting in small 8-16kg batches on a Diedrich IR-25, they ensure peak freshness, zero waste, and continue to nurture long-term relationships with producers, some for over 13 years.

name: Johan & Nyström - Swedenborgsgatan
excerpt_short: Swedish coffee revolutionaries: modern craft, direct trade, sustainable excellence.
excerpt_long: Sweden's coffee revolutionaries since 2004. Direct Trade, rare beans, and regenerative farming for unparalleled excellence.
summary: Sweden's pioneering "big little roastery" since 2004. Crafting excellence with strict Direct Trade, rare Prime Series, and sustainable FAF Future Farmers, they redefine specialty coffee.
flavour_profile: Swedish Third Wave, Direct Trade, Rare Prime Series, Future Farmers
about:
Sweden's Coffee Revolutionaries: Crafting Excellence with a Conscience

Born from a vision in 2004 to ignite a "coffee revolution" in Sweden, this pioneering roastery moved beyond dark-roasted commodity coffee, introducing the nuanced world of specialty beans. Operating with a "big little roastery" philosophy, they blend the passion and craft of a small operation with the consistency of one of Europe's most modern roasteries, opened in Länna in 2017.

Their commitment extends to strict, relational Direct Trade, fostering partnerships with producers for at least three consecutive years, ensuring mutual growth and radical transparency throughout the supply chain.

Their dedication to exceptional coffee shines through offerings like the exclusive "Prime Series," featuring rare, limited-quantity coffees sourced only a few times a year, showcasing unique varietals and innovative processing. Beyond the cup, they actively champion sustainability through the "FAF Future Farmers" project in Brazil, supporting producers in transitioning to regenerative agriculture with tangible, on-the-ground improvements. This holistic approach, coupled with thorough barista training ensuring every pour-over and latte art creation realizes the coffee's full potential, defines their unique soul and unwavering pursuit of coffee excellence.

name: Astronauta Café
excerpt_short: Interstellar vegan coffee, unique roasts, and out-of-this-world plant-based bites.
excerpt_long: Blast off to an intergalactic vegan coffee experience! Enjoy specialty brews in rocket cups & unique plant-based delights. Houston, we have coffee!
summary: Blast off to an intergalactic vegan coffee experience! Enjoy specialty brews in rocket cups, paired with innovative plant-based delights. Discover unique "Honey" processed coffee from a female-led farm. Houston, we have coffee!
flavour_profile: Intergalactic vegan coffee, unique brews, female-led sourcing.
about:
Blast Off to Flavor: An Interstellar Vegan Coffee Experience

Prepare for an "intergalactic" escape in the city, where a striking rocket ship façade sets the stage. Inside, "Houston, we have coffee!" welcomes you to a space-themed wonderland, complete with alien-adorned pillows and a central coffee plant. Your espresso arrives in custom rocket-shaped cups, adding a whimsical touch to your journey.

This unique destination is also a haven for plant-based enthusiasts, boasting a 100% vegan menu. Be sure to try their signature "Shakerato Il Blu," a stellar blend of espresso, sugar syrup, sage, and pink pepper.Beyond the captivating theme, this café is serious about its brew, offering diverse methods like Hario V60 and French Press.

They proudly feature "Honey" processed coffee from producer Marisa, whose dedication transformed her farm from commodity to specialty, earning UTZ and BSCA certifications. This female-led sourcing initiative brings a distinct body and flavor to your cup. Complement your coffee with innovative vegan food pairings, such as purple sweet potato bread with vegetable chèvre and red onion jam, or activated charcoal bread with cashew cheddar, ensuring every visit is a flavorful adventure.
address: R. Rio Grande, 139 - Vila Mariana, São Paulo

name: Sensory Coffee
excerpt_short: Brazilian coffee's true soul: expertly roasted, unique pairings, sweet notes.
excerpt_long: Experience Brazil's soul in every cup. This roastery exclusively sources high-quality Brazilian coffees, offering unique pairings and flavors.
summary: Experience Brazil's soul in every cup. This specialty roastery exclusively sources and roasts exceptional Brazilian coffees, offering unique coffee & cheese pairings and an in-house bakery. Redefining Brazilian coffee excellence.
flavour_profile: Brazilian single origin, sensory pairings, unique pastries, shakarato.
about:
Capturing Brazil's Soul, One Cup at a Time
