Dive into a unique sensory journey where Brazil's rich coffee heritage takes center stage. This specialty coffee haven is dedicated to showcasing the nation's diverse and high-quality terroir, exclusively sourcing and serving coffees from its home country. Under the expert guidance of founder Fernando Guerra and master roaster Luciano Salomao, 250kg of exceptional beans are roasted weekly on in-house machines.

The café's very name reflects its mission to craft unparalleled experiences, notably through curated coffee and cheese pairings, reminiscent of fine wine tastings, inviting guests to explore new dimensions of flavor.Committed to redefining global perceptions of Brazilian coffee, this brand champions distinctive, high-quality single origins known for their intense sweetness and notes of chocolate and caramel.

Their direct sourcing relationship with the Santa Cecilia farm in the Cerrado Mineiro region, part of a family with over 40 years of coffee-producing expertise, ensures unparalleled quality. Beyond coffee, an in-house bakery crafts all pastries, featuring a standout signature orange croissant. Don't miss their unique "shakarato" drink, completing an experience that celebrates the true soul and vibrant character of Brazilian specialty coffee.
address: R. Simão Álvares, 781 - Pinheiros, São Paulo

name: Crio Café
excerpt_short: Roastery: unique microlots, square pão de queijo, and coffee discovery.
excerpt_long: Discover a new era of specialty coffee with unique microlots, innovative subscriptions, and the world's first square pão de queijo.
summary: Discover Gabriel Magalhães' vision: unique microlots via subscription, transparent sourcing, and the world's first square pão de queijo. A journey into specialty coffee innovation and community.
flavour_profile: Exclusive microlots, unique pão de queijo, coffee courses.
about:
Beyond the Bean: Unveiling a New Era of Specialty Coffee

Step into a world where specialty coffee is not just a drink, but an accessible journey of discovery. Founded by Gabriel Magalhães, formerly a partner at Rio's esteemed Custo Café, this establishment redefines the coffee experience. Their innovative subscription service grants exclusive access to monthly "microlotes inéditos," addressing common consumer challenges of cost and transparency head-on.

Partnering with distinguished suppliers like Fazenda 7 Senhoras, they ensure a curated selection that promises both quality and intrigue, inviting enthusiasts to explore unique origins and profiles.Beyond the exceptional beans, the café offers delightful culinary surprises and educational opportunities. Indulge in what's proudly claimed as the "world's first square cheese bread" (pão de queijo quadrado), a playful twist on a beloved classic.

Their highly-praised Cold Brew stands as a testament to their craft, while the "Filtered Coffee Experiences Course" (Curso Filtrados - Experiências) empowers customers to deepen their appreciation and brewing skills. It's a vibrant hub where passion for coffee meets a commitment to innovation and community.
address: Alameda Santos, 1470, Loja 1478 - Jardim Paulista, São Paulo

name: Urbe Café Bar
excerpt_short: Urbe Coffee: Redefining field-to-cup with innovative brews & coffee mixology.
excerpt_long: Urbe Coffee: A 'field to cup' pioneer, crafting innovative brews from rare micro-lots to coffee cocktails, served in a soulful, intimate setting.
summary: Urbe Coffee: From field to cup, a soulful sanctuary where innovative brews, unique coffee cocktails, and adventurous flavors redefine your specialty coffee experience.
flavour_profile: Seed-to-cup, surprise microlots, inventive cocktails, dark ambiance.
about:
Urbe Coffee: A Journey from Field to Cup, Redefined

Step into Urbe Coffee, a true pioneer in the "from field to cup" movement, meticulously controlling every step of their coffee's journey from planting to your final pour. The ambiance, with its dim lighting and black walls adorned with chalked coffee quotes, sets a soulful, intimate stage for an extraordinary experience. Here, innovation is brewed into every cup, from the surprise "Urbe 03" micro-lot pour-over, showcasing rare and rotating specialty beans, to the highly inventive "Urbe cappuccino," a delightful blend of doce de leite crowned with delicate lime shavings.

Beyond the traditional, Urbe Coffee elevates the coffee experience with a dedicated mixologist, Marco de La Roche, crafting an exclusive menu of coffee-based cocktails like the refreshing "Mojito Coffee" and the intriguing "Urbe G&T&C."

Don't miss their signature "Santo Refresco" cold brew, a unique infusion of coffee, honey, lemon, and lemongrass. The culinary offerings are equally adventurous, spanning from breakfast to late-night snacks, featuring unconventional delights such as a cold apple soup with curry and cilantro, served in a mug, ensuring every visit is a discovery.
address: R. Antônio Carlos, 404 - Consolação, São Paulo

name: Takkø Café
excerpt_short: São Paulo's pioneering café, sharing unique Brazilian coffee stories.
excerpt_long: Takko Café, a São Paulo pioneer, brews passion and purpose, connecting guests to unique Brazilian single-origin stories with innovative batch brews.
summary: Takko Café: São Paulo's pioneer, blending graphic design with direct-trade Brazilian specialty coffee. Known for its unique urban vibe and early adoption of batch brew, it shares producer stories in every cup.
flavour_profile: Pioneer, direct trade, urban art, Brazilian single-origins.
about:
Takko Café: A São Paulo Pioneer Brewing Passion and Purpose

Born from a graphic designer's unexpected love for specialty coffee, Takko Café is more than just a coffee shop; it's a vibrant testament to passion and pioneering spirit. Co-founded in 2014 by Rodolfo Herrera and journalist Flávio Seixlack, who bravely invested their severance pay into a dream, Takko quickly became a cornerstone of Vila Buarque's creative renaissance in downtown São Paulo.

Its distinctive visual identity, a graphic homage to the city's fragmented urban scenes, perfectly encapsulates its metropolitan soul, reflecting a deep connection to São Paulo's dynamic character.Takko Café distinguishes itself by fostering direct, intimate relationships with coffee producers, choosing not to roast its own beans but instead to genuinely share the unique stories and origins behind every cup.

This commitment to transparency and connection is paired with an innovative approach to brewing; Takko was an early adopter of the "batch brew" method in São Paulo, offering expertly prepared, ready-to-serve filter coffee. Their curated menu features exceptional Brazilian single-origin coffees, such as an Acaia varietal from Cerrado Mineiro for espresso and a bright yellow Catuaí from Espírito Santo for pour-overs, inviting guests to explore the rich tapestry of Brazilian coffee.
address: R. Maj. Sertório, 553 - República, São Paulo

name: Pato Rei
excerpt_short: Where classical composition orchestrates unique coffee flavors & pastry harmony.
excerpt_long: Pato Rei: Classical composition orchestrates unique coffee experiences. Explore rare, fruity, and floral notes, paired with exquisite pastries.
summary: Pato Rei: A composer's precision orchestrates unique coffee experiences, revealing unadvertised notes like yuzu and magnolia. A symphony of flavor in every meticulously crafted cup.
flavour_profile: Music-crafted roasts, weird fruits, rare beans, Asian pastries.
about:
Pato Rei: Where Classical Composition Meets Coffee Craft

Pato Rei, meaning 'King Duck,' is the brainchild of Tiago de Mello, a former music composer who traded soundtracks for roasting beans. His background in classical composition profoundly shapes his meticulous and complex approach to coffee, transforming each roast into a carefully orchestrated experience. The name, an ironic nod to a teacher's advice against clumsy versatility, now celebrates their mastery across diverse coffee expressions.

What began as an exclusive 'Cold Brew Club,' delivering unique bottled brews, evolved into a café concept inspired by immersive coffee and pastry research trips through the vibrant culinary scenes of Tokyo, Taipei, and Seoul.This unique vision is a collaborative symphony with Tiago's wife, Larissa Taketa, a talented pastry chef, ensuring a harmonious pairing of exceptional coffee and delectable treats.

Pato Rei categorizes its exquisite offerings into whimsical profiles like 'Frutados & esquisitos' (Fruity & weird), 'Florais & delicados' (Floral & delicate), and 'Raros & premiados' (Rare & award-winning). They pride themselves on showcasing coffees with distinct, often unadvertised, flavor notes such as yuzu, magnolia, lavender, and fig, inviting guests to explore a truly unparalleled sensory journey in every cup and bite.
address: R. Ferreira de Araújo, 353 - Pinheiros, São Paulo

name: UM Coffee Co.
excerpt_short: Um Coffee Co.: World Champion's farm-to-cup legacy, 38+ unique varieties.
excerpt_long: World Barista Champion Boram Um's farm-to-cup legacy. Experience unique Brazilian specialty coffee from Fazenda UM.
summary: Um Coffee Co.: 2023 World Barista Champion Boram Um's Korean-Brazilian legacy. From Fazenda UM to your cup, a unique farm-to-cup specialty coffee experience.
flavour_profile: World Champion, farm-to-cup, expert-led, academy, heritage.
about:
Um Coffee Co.: A Champion's Legacy, From Farm to Cup

Step into the world of Um Coffee Co., a specialty coffee haven born from a rich Korean-Brazilian heritage and crowned by unparalleled expertise. Founded by Boram Um, the 2023 World Barista Champion – a historic first for Brazil – this establishment embodies a profound passion for coffee. Boram, a certified Q Grader, along with his brother Garam Um, a certified SCA instructor, lead a team dedicated to excellence.

Their journey began with their father's move from Korea to Brazil, cultivating a dream that blossomed into Fazenda UM, the family's own farm.Um Coffee Co. proudly sources directly from Fazenda UM, home to over 38 distinct coffee varieties, ensuring a true farm-to-cup experience. Beyond exceptional beans, they foster education through the Um Coffee Academy, founded by Garam Um, Brazil's first school offering all SCA Coffee Skills Program certifications.

Coffee enthusiasts can savor the "Roaster's Choice of the Month" subscription, personally curated by three-time Brazilian Barista Champion, Boram Um. This commitment to quality and passion quickly earned them recognition, being named the best coffee shop in São Paulo within their inaugural year in 2017.
address: R. Júlio Conceição, 553 - Bom Retiro, São Paulo

name: Sofá Café
excerpt_short: Sofá Café: Award-winning specialty coffee, meticulously roasted with social impact.
excerpt_long: Passion-driven Sofá Café roasts award-winning 80+ point specialty coffee, empowering youth and expanding globally.
summary: Sofá Café: Award-winning roastery, meticulously crafting 80+ point specialty coffee with direct producer ties. From Rio to Boston, we blend global excellence with social impact, empowering youth.
flavour_profile: Forestry roots, award-winning, social impact, global reach.
about:
Sofá Café: A Journey of Passion, Purpose, and Award-Winning Coffee

Born from a Forestry Engineer's nine-month planned career change, Sofá Café embodies a profound dedication to specialty coffee. This commitment is evident in their meticulous approach: roasting their own beans to ensure consistent, exceptional quality across all locations, and exclusively sourcing specialty coffee scoring over 80 points, emphasizing traceability and direct relationships with producers. Their unwavering pursuit of excellence has earned them prestigious accolades, including being named the best coffee shop in Rio de Janeiro and the second-best in São Paulo by the esteemed Veja Comer & Beber guide. With an international presence now extending to Boston, USA, Sofá Café's passion for quality resonates globally.Beyond the cup, Sofá Café cultivates a powerful social impact through "Fazedores de Café," a project launched in 2014 that empowers young people from vulnerable backgrounds with barista training, achieving an impressive 90% employment rate for its graduates. This blend of social responsibility and business acumen has also led to significant partnerships with corporate giants like Google, Cubo/Itaú, Santander, and Bradesco. Further cementing their expertise, Sofá Café offers comprehensive consulting services, guiding aspiring and established coffee shop owners from architectural design to staff training, truly shaping the specialty coffee landscape.
address: R. Ferreira de Araújo, 907 - Pinheiros, São Paulo

name: Coffee Lab
excerpt_short: Brazil's pioneering Coffee Lab: meticulous sourcing, precision roasting, pure Brazilian.
excerpt_long: Brazil's first Barista Champ, Isabela Raposeiras, crafts unique Brazilian micro-lots with precision at her pioneering Coffee Lab.
summary: Isabela Raposeiras' Coffee Lab pioneers Brazil's specialty coffee. Meticulously sourcing unique micro-lots, this roastery champions precision, education, and the rich diversity of Brazilian beans.
flavour_profile: Brazilian microlots, AeroPress, lab aesthetic, education
about:
Isabela Raposeiras' Coffee Lab: A Brazilian Coffee Revolution

Step into the world of Isabela Raposeiras, Brazil's first National Barista Champion, at her pioneering Coffee Lab. This isn't just a café; it's a meticulously crafted experience where staff, clad in mechanics' jumpsuits, embody a dedication to precision and quality. Isabela's rigorous process for selecting micro-lots involves deep relationships with Brazilian farmers and a "nice people" policy, ensuring every bean is sourced with integrity.

Her rare practice of storing green beans separately from their bags further preserves their exceptional quality, reflecting a profound respect for the coffee's journey from farm to cup.As a Brazilian specialty roaster, Coffee Lab champions the rich diversity of its homeland, focusing exclusively on the unique flavor profiles from various regions across the country.

The café, housed in a bright, charmingly renovated residence, became a key draw for its advocacy of the AeroPress brewing method. Beyond serving exquisite coffee, Coffee Lab is a hub for education, operating a barista school and offering consulting services. Isabela's global travels to showcase Brazilian coffees to international roasters underscore her unwavering commitment to elevating her country's specialty coffee scene.
address: R. Fradique Coutinho, 1340 - Vila Madalena, São Paulo

name: Futuro Refeitório
excerpt_short: Crafted coffee: direct-sourced, conceptually blended, fine-dining experience.
excerpt_long: A unique roastery celebrating the bean: direct sourcing, visible craft, conceptual blends, and chef-driven coffee cocktails.
summary: A specialty coffee haven where visible roasting meets deep bean respect. Savor direct farm ties, conceptual blends, and innovative coffee cocktails, all infused with a fine-dining sensibility.
flavour_profile: Visible roasting, farm-to-cup, conceptual blends, coffee cocktails
about:
A Culinary Journey Rooted in Respect for the Bean

Step into a unique specialty coffee destination where the craft is on full display. At its heart, an Atilla 5 Gold Plus roaster commands attention, making the roasting process a visible and aromatic part of your experience. This commitment to the bean runs deep, exemplified by head barista Natalia Braga's immersive journey to Minas Gerais and the team's extensive sourcing trip, which yielded 65 distinct coffee samples, fostering direct relationships with producers.

Founders Gabriela and Karina Barretto infuse their vision with "a seriousness in the field, a respect for the grain," ensuring every cup tells a coherent story.The café’s character shines through its thoughtful approach. Their house blend is described conceptually, evoking the feeling of "Pedro Almodóvar and Talking Heads" rather than just flavor notes.

Brewing is intentionally simplified to espresso and V60 for consistent quality, while brewed coffee is unconventionally served in a glass to "democratize the service" and highlight its visual beauty. Beyond traditional offerings, discover creative coffee-based cocktails like the "Gin Brew," a refreshing mix of gin, tonic, and house-made cold brew. Chef Gabriela Barretto, a Cordon Bleu-trained alumna of Asador Etxebarri, ensures a fine-dining sensibility permeates the entire experience.
address: R. Cônego Eugênio Leite, 808 - Pinheiros, São Paulo

name: Morrow Coffee
excerpt_short: Barcelona's micro-roastery: Russian passion, advanced processing, unique cascara.
excerpt_long: Barcelona's Morrow Coffee: Russian passion fuels a micro-roastery, meticulously crafting unique flavors with advanced processing and cascara.
summary: Barcelona's Morrow Coffee: A micro-roastery born from Russian passion. Andrey meticulously roasts unique beans, highlighting advanced processing and exquisite flavors, fostering a vibrant community.
flavour_profile: Russian roots, micro-roastery, unique processing, cascara, community.
about:
Morrow Coffee: From Russian Roots to Barcelona's Roasting Heart

Morrow Coffee, nestled on Barcelona's Gran Vía, is more than just a café; it's the culmination of a passionate journey led by Russian-born founders Andrey Rachnov and Daria Repina. Andrey's quest for exceptional coffee began with a simple question: why did coffee so often taste bitter?

This curiosity led him to Barcelona's vibrant coffee scene and studies at the Institute of Coffee, igniting a dedication to specialty coffee. This passion is evident in their in-house micro-roastery, where every bean is transformed with meticulous care.As head roaster, Andrey personally oversees the roasting process, ensuring each batch highlights unique flavor profiles, often featuring advanced processing methods like the "anaerobic natural" found in their Ethiopia Idido selection.

Beyond exquisite coffee, Morrow offers unique cascara infusions, made from the coffee bean husk, and a commitment to quality extends to offering unrefined Philippine sugar for those who prefer a touch of sweetness. This dedication to craft and quality has fostered a strong, loyal community, making Morrow Coffee a beloved hub for both Barcelona locals and the city's international expat scene.

name: Orval Studio
excerpt_short: Rebellious creative hub: curated coffee, indie books, and vibrant events.
excerpt_long: Orval Studio: A vibrant creative hub where rebellious spirits connect over meticulously curated specialty coffee and unique artistic events.
summary: Orval Studio: A vibrant, rebellious creative hub where meticulously curated specialty coffee fuels artistic minds, surrounded by independent magazines and dynamic events.
flavour_profile: Creative hub, rebellious spirit, esoteric, vegan, curated.
about:
Orval Studio: Where Rebellious Creativity Meets Curated Coffee

Orval Studio, the brainchild of young designer Lucía López, is more than just a café; it's a vibrant creative hub where "creators meet coffee." Conceived as the "rebellious sister" to its romantic predecessor, Espai Joliu, Orval embraces a distinct, esoteric personality. Its name, a nod to the stimulating and almost addictive nature of coffee, reflects a unique spirit.

Beyond its distinctive vintage aesthetic, abundant plant life, and light-filled interior, Orval functions as a multi-concept space, housing a curated bookstore of independent magazines and hosting dynamic creative events, from flea markets to workshops and pop-ups.While Orval Studio doesn't roast its own beans, it prides itself on a meticulously curated coffee program, sourcing exceptional single-origin coffees from respected local specialty roasters like Nomad and Three Marks Coffee.

Their baristas expertly highlight the nuanced notes of each bean through both traditional and innovative brewing methods, including Aeropress and pour-over. Complementing their exquisite coffee selection, Orval also offers a thoughtful array of vegan-friendly food options, ensuring a delightful experience for every visitor.

name: Brew Coffee
excerpt_short: Junzhi Liu's sanctuary: award-winning barista, expert manual brews, purist focus.
excerpt_long: Junzhi Liu's intimate sanctuary offers award-winning coffee, exceptional Cold Brew, and diverse manual brews. A purist's dream.
summary: Junzhi Liu's intimate coffee sanctuary is a purist's haven. Savor unparalleled specialty coffee, from signature Cold Brew to diverse manual brews, personally guided by a champion barista.
flavour_profile: Championship barista, cold brew, manual brews, purist, intimate
about:
A Purist's Paradise: Junzhi Liu's Coffee Sanctuary

Tucked away from the bustling tourist paths, this intimate coffee sanctuary is a true hidden gem for those who seek unparalleled coffee experiences. At its heart is founder and head barista, Junzhi Liu, a passionate coffee expert whose dedication shines through every cup. A formidable competitor, Junzhi showcased his mastery across Latte Art, Barista, and Brewing categories at the 2024 Spanish Specialty Coffee Association (SCA) championships, bringing a competitive edge and profound knowledge to his craft.

As the name subtly suggests, the expertly prepared Cold Brew stands as a house specialty, a testament to their meticulous approach. Beyond this, the café is a haven for manual brewing enthusiasts, offering an impressive array of methods including Chemex, Aeropress, Kalita, V60, and the distinctive Origami dripper.

Junzhi himself delights in personally explaining the intricate nuances of extraction and flavor profiles, fostering a deep connection with each customer. This isn't just a café; it's a purist's destination, committed solely to the highest quality coffee in a local, intimate setting.
address: Carrer de la Junta de Comerç, 24, 08001 Barcelona

name: Roast Club Cafe
excerpt_short: Roast Club: Barcelona's global coffee odyssey, innovative blends, and coffee education.
excerpt_long: Roast Club: Barcelona's roastery, where Iker Zago's global journey infuses every bean, crafting unique, innovative flavor experiences.
summary: Roast Club: Barcelona's global coffee odyssey. Iker Zago's journey infuses unique, unconventional blends like "Blueberry Fudge" and "Zesty Pavlova." Discover exceptional coffee and education.
flavour_profile: Creative blends, global expertise, coffee college, monthly exploration
about:
Roast Club: Barcelona's Global Coffee Odyssey

Nestled in Barcelona, Roast Club is more than just a roastery; it's the culmination of founder Iker Zago's extraordinary global coffee journey. From learning the art of roasting in Italy with "Pura Vida," to a tiny 8-square-meter shop in NYC's East Village, and a transformative trip to Costa Rica in 2014 that solidified his passion, Iker's diverse experiences across Italy, Australia, Formentera, London, and New York City infuse every bean.

This rich background, combined with his role as "Coffee Selector," defines Roast Club's unique character and commitment to the craft since its establishment in 2016.Roast Club stands out for its innovative and unconventional blends, offering a vibrant exploration of flavor with profiles like "Blueberry Fudge," "Zesty Pavlova," and "Choco Power."

Their dedication extends beyond the cup, with a "Coffee College" in Tamarit fostering education and a "Blend of the Month" inviting continuous discovery. Supported by a team of dedicated experts, including a "Barista & Latte Art Expert," Roast Club cultivates a dynamic and educational environment, ensuring every coffee experience is both exceptional and enlightening.

name: Dalston Coffee
excerpt_short: Chef-founded roastery, brewing Dalston's artistic soul into every unique cup.
excerpt_long: Dalston Coffee: East London's soul in every cup. Chef-founded, artistically branded, with unique origins and playfully named, exceptional roasts.
summary: Dalston Coffee: A chef's vision, brewing East London's vibrant soul. Experience artistic branding, unique origins like "Lord Voldemort," and a journey from kitchen to cup, crafted with passion.
flavour_profile: Chef-led, artistic, unique sourcing, educational, guest roasters.
about:
Dalston Coffee: A Culinary Journey Brewed with Artistic Soul

Founded in 2015 by Borja Roselló, a former chef, Dalston Coffee embodies a unique journey from kitchen to cup, deeply inspired by the vibrant East London neighborhood where Roselló first discovered specialty coffee. This inspiration is vividly reflected in their highly creative and artistic branding, with packaging designed in collaboration with Barcelona's Ingdid Picanyol and Porto's Lourenço Providência.

The distinctive, colorful box designs pay homage to Dalston's iconic red brick buildings and lively awnings, capturing the area's eclectic spirit and infusing every bag with a piece of its soul.Dalston's commitment to unique character extends to its sourcing, featuring exceptional origins like a washed Tanzanian Kent cultivar from the Tembo Tembo farm, nestled in the rich volcanic soil of the Ngorongoro crater's foothills.

This coffee is a testament to their dedication to quality and nature's symbiosis. Their portfolio boasts creatively named offerings such as the "WHAT THE FUNK" espresso blend and the playfully dubbed "Lord Voldemort" Colombian variety. In their café, staff are renowned for their educational approach, guiding customers through the nuances of each bean, while a rotating selection of guest roasters, including London's Square Mile Coffee, ensures a continually curated and diverse coffee experience.

name: Right Side Coffee
excerpt_short: Barcelona's champion roaster, pioneering specialty coffee with classic craftsmanship.
excerpt_long: Barcelona's champion roaster, Right Side Coffee, pioneers specialty with vintage machines, direct imports, and innovative producer collaborations.
summary: Right Side Coffee: Barcelona's pioneer, led by a 3x Spanish Roasting Champion. Blending tradition with innovation, they meticulously roast directly imported beans on vintage machines, pushing quality boundaries.
flavour_profile: Family legacy, champion roaster, vintage machines, direct trade, experimental, pioneer.
about:
Right Side Coffee: Barcelona's Roasting Pioneer with a Champion's Touch

Right Side Coffee, founded by Joaquin Parra, embodies a unique blend of deep family heritage and audacious entrepreneurial spirit. Emerging from a prominent Spanish coffee lineage, Parra launched Right Side from his garage in 2012 with a mere 2,000 euros, naming it to reflect the creativity and passion essential for its growth.

A celebrated figure, Joaquin is a three-time Spanish Roasting Champion and achieved an impressive 4th place at the 2011 World Roasting Championship, quickly establishing Right Side as a pivotal pioneer in Barcelona's burgeoning third-wave coffee scene.The roastery's distinct character is further defined by its commitment to both tradition and innovation. Right Side meticulously roasts 100% directly imported coffee on beautifully restored vintage machines – a 1993 Probat L12 and a 1959 UG22 – blending classic craftsmanship with modern precision.

They actively collaborate with producers on experimental fermentation and drying projects, pushing quality boundaries at the source. For over a decade, Right Side operated exclusively as a wholesale roaster, a unique model that allowed them to dedicate unparalleled focus to sourcing and roasting exceptional beans for influential early specialty cafés like Satan's Coffee Corner.

name: Hidden Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Barcelona roastery: crafting excellence, custom roaster, pure flavor, legacy.
excerpt_long: Crafting excellence in Barcelona: a legacy roastery with global expertise, custom roasting for pure flavor, and a passion for community.
summary: Born from a rich legacy, this Barcelona roastery crafts exceptional, high-scoring coffees on a custom roaster. Experience innovation, from 91-point beans to pesticide-free Japanese matcha.
flavour_profile: Lynx mission, custom roasts, 91 SCA, rare tech, direct matcha.
about:
The Soul of Specialty Coffee: Crafting Excellence in Barcelona

Born from a rich coffee legacy, this Barcelona roastery embodies a profound dedication to the craft. Co-founder Carlos Moral, whose father established a coffee import business in 1979, honed his expertise living on Nicaraguan coffee farms and training across Costa Rica, Kenya, and Ethiopia. Alongside matcha specialist Mateo Gonzalez, sourcing pesticide-free matcha directly from Japan, they’ve cultivated a unique character.

Their commitment to flavor purity is evident in their custom-designed roaster, meticulously constructed to optimize convection heat for a cleaner profile, a testament to their innovative spirit.

Their passion shines through their "Competition Series," featuring high-scoring coffees like the acclaimed Torogoz, boasting an impressive 91 SCA score. The Iberian lynx, their critically endangered wild cat logo, symbolizes their mission to illuminate specialty coffee from obscurity. This roastery is deeply woven into Barcelona's vibrant coffee scene, with Moral's brothers also founding respected companies. Their resilience and community spirit were powerfully demonstrated with the "#FUCKCORONA" Rwandan coffee edition, a testament to their unwavering support for their cafes and clientele.
address: C/ Constança esquina Déu i Mata, 08029 Barcelona

name: Cafès El Magnífico
excerpt_short: Barcelona's century-old pioneer: traceable single-origins, expert roasting, direct trade.
excerpt_long: El Magnífico: Barcelona's century-old roastery, pioneering traceable single-origins, global judging, and scientific roasting.
summary: Barcelona's Cafés El Magnífico, a century-old beacon, pioneered traceable single-origin coffee in 1987. Driven by expert judges, advanced Loring roasters, and direct trade, they craft unparalleled coffee experiences.
flavour_profile: Historic legacy, pioneering traceability, expert roasting, diverse brews.
about:
Cafés El Magnífico: Barcelona's Century-Old Beacon of Specialty Coffee

Steeped in history, Cafés El Magnífico's coffee journey in Barcelona began with a family colonial store in 1919, evolving into the renowned brand established in 1962. This pioneering spirit saw them introduce fully traceable single-origin coffees as early as 1987, a revolutionary concept at the time. Their commitment to elevating coffee culture is further cemented by the founder's role as an International Tasting Judge for the prestigious Cup of Excellence since 2002, and their co-founding of Spain's influential "Forum Cultural del Café" in 1996, a true benchmark for professionals.

Today, El Magnífico continues to innovate, utilizing advanced Loring roasters and a scientific, data-driven approach with Cropster software to ensure unparalleled consistency. Under the guidance of the third generation, with Claudia Sans Witty overseeing quality control and serving as an International Sensory Judge, their passion for coffee shines.

They champion a multitude of brewing methods, from Chemex to AeroPress, alongside espresso, offering an extensive, detailed coffee menu that once filled a book. This dedication, coupled with decades-long direct trade relationships, defines their unique character.

name: SlowMov
excerpt_short: SlowMov: Barcelona's pioneer, deliberate, sustainable, origin-focused coffee.
excerpt_long: SlowMov: Barcelona's pioneer roastery. Deliberate, origin-focused coffee, celebrating inherent flavors & fostering community.
summary: SlowMov, Barcelona's specialty coffee pioneer, embodies a "slow movement" philosophy. They meticulously source and roast unique, traceable beans, revealing inherent flavors, and cultivate a vibrant local food community.
flavour_profile: Slow living, origin-focused, experimental, community, sustainable.
about:
SlowMov: Barcelona's Soulful Brew, Rooted in Deliberation

SlowMov, a name embodying "Slow" and "Movement," champions a deliberate, appreciative approach to coffee, a refreshing contrast to hurried consumption. Pioneers in Barcelona's specialty coffee scene, founders François Justet and Carmen Callizo boldly forgo sugar in their café, inviting patrons to discover the inherent sweetness and nuanced flavors of their meticulously sourced beans. Their commitment extends beyond the cup, fostering a vibrant local food community through "La Colmena SlowMov," a weekly cooperative connecting consumers directly with local producers. This dedication to sustainability is further evident in their cargo bike deliveries and fully biodegradable coffee bags.

Driven by a passion for exceptional coffee, François and Carmen regularly journey to origin countries, cultivating direct relationships for fresh, seasonal, and traceable coffees. This hands-on approach allows for truly unique offerings, like an "intrinsic cherry processed" Catuaí from Panama with a 16-hour anaerobic fermentation, or a Colombian coffee from Luz Helena Salazar, distinguished by a 30-hour dry aerobic fermentation. Recently upgrading their roasting operation to a Probat UG22, SlowMov's philosophy is to let the coffee's origin speak for itself, crafting roasts that highlight the terroir's inherent qualities.

name: Syra Coffee
excerpt_short: Design-led roastery, sustainable innovation, upcycling coffee waste.
excerpt_long: Architect-led, sustainable roastery. Pioneering circularity, transforming coffee waste into award-winning products. Experience innovation.
summary: Syra Coffee masterfully blends architectural design with sustainable coffee innovation, pioneering circularity with award-winning upcycled products, VR farm tours, and rigorously cupped specialty roasts.
flavour_profile: Architectural design, circularity, VR farms, daily cupping
about:
Syra Coffee: Where Architectural Design Meets Sustainable Coffee Innovation

Syra Coffee, founded by architect Yassir Raïs, redefines the specialty coffee experience by seamlessly blending thoughtful design with a profound commitment to sustainability. Raïs's architectural vision shapes each space, creating an aesthetic that is both inviting and innovative. At the heart of their unique character is the Syra Lab initiative, a pioneering effort in circularity that transforms coffee waste into remarkable products.

This dedication is exemplified by their award-winning 3D-printed pot, crafted from upcycled coffee grounds, a testament to their ingenuity recognized by the Industrial Design Association. They further foster this spirit through their "Coffee & Circularity Competition," inviting fresh ideas for sustainable solutions.Beyond their design-forward approach, Syra Coffee offers an immersive journey into the world of coffee, including a virtual reality experience transporting customers to a coffee farm in El Salvador. Their commitment to quality is unwavering, with a rigorous daily cupping protocol ensuring every batch roasted meets their high standards.

Strategically expanding into smaller cities, Syra Coffee aims to make specialty coffee accessible to new communities. This innovative brand has even attracted investment from unexpected sectors, including tech and professional athletes like Sergi Roberto and Alexia Putellas, highlighting their distinctive appeal in the specialty coffee landscape.
address: Carrer de la Mare de Déu dels Desemparats, 8, 08012 Barcelona

name: Three Marks Coffee
excerpt_short: Born from serendipity, this fearless roastery experiments with personality & ethics.
excerpt_long: Born from serendipity, this roastery fearlessly crafts experimental specialty coffee. Dedicated to ethical sourcing & genuine connections.
summary: Born from a serendipitous trio, this specialty roastery fearlessly experiments, crafting unique coffees with a welcoming spirit. Dedication and direct trade define their soulful brew.
flavour_profile: Founders' passion, experimental, welcoming, creative, direct trade.
about:
The Soulful Brew: Where Serendipity Meets Specialty Coffee

Born from a serendipitous connection between a manager, a roaster, and a devoted customer, this specialty coffee haven is the brainchild of Marc Aguyé, Marco De Rebotti, and Marco Paccagnella. This trio, with Marco De Rebotti leading coffee sourcing and roasting, Marco Paccagnella shaping their distinctive branding, and Marc Aguyé ensuring happy cafes, embodies a truly unique spirit.

Their brand is celebrated for its "fearlessness" and willingness to experiment, setting them apart. This adventurous ethos extends to their hiring, prioritizing personality, believing "we can teach people how to make coffee, but we can't teach them how to be a nice person," fostering an undeniably welcoming atmosphere.

Their journey began with late nights, roasting all filter coffee on an IKAWA sample roaster, a testament to their unwavering dedication. This commitment to craft is infused with a creative flair, once operating a "speakeasy" style micro-café within a 3D design agency. Head of coffee, Marco De Rebotti, brings a fascinating background, having started as a hairdresser in London before finding his true calling. Looking ahead, their passion drives a clear goal: to travel to origin and forge direct, fair trade relationships with producers, ensuring every cup tells a story of quality and ethical partnership.

name: Nomad Coffee
excerpt_short: Barcelona's pioneering roastery, rare beans, B Corp, shaping coffee culture.
excerpt_long: Nomad Coffee: Barcelona's pioneering B Corp roastery, passionately crafting rare, exquisite beans and empowering coffee culture.
summary: Nomad Coffee: Barcelona's pioneering roastery, born from a champion barista's passion. Discover rare "Limited Edition" beans and join a B Corp committed to craft, community, and shaping coffee culture.
flavour_profile: Pioneering, champion-led, ethical, rare beans, academy.
about:
Nomad Coffee: Barcelona's Pioneering Spirit, Rooted in Passion

Born from the nomadic spirit of two-time Spanish National Barista Champion Jordi Mestre, Nomad Coffee began its journey as a humble coffee cart in London's bustling street markets. Mestre, who honed his craft in London's vibrant specialty coffee scene, brought his expertise back to Barcelona, where Nomad Coffee quickly became a pivotal pioneer.

Opening their first permanent space, Nomad Coffee Lab, in 2014, they organically cultivated a reputation for exceptional quality and a deep commitment to the craft, growing without external investors and shaping Barcelona's burgeoning coffee culture.Today, Nomad Coffee stands as a beacon of specialty coffee, celebrated for its "Limited Edition" series showcasing rare and unique beans. Beyond their exquisite coffee offerings, they empower the next generation of baristas and enthusiasts through "The Coffee Academy," a dedicated training space.

Their commitment extends beyond the cup, as a certified B Corporation upholding high social and environmental standards. This pioneering spirit also branches into other culinary ventures, including the beloved Pa de Kilo bakery, solidifying Nomad Coffee's unique character and enduring influence.

name: Plántate Café
excerpt_short: Madrid's coffee & plant haven: global roasters, unique brews, lush escape.
excerpt_long: Madrid's unique coffee oasis: where expertly roasted beans meet lush greenery. Experience world-class brews & a vibrant plant sanctuary.
summary: Madrid's unique coffee haven: a lush plant store meets specialty coffee, born from a Canadian's quest for perfection. Experience world-class roasters & creative brews amidst vibrant greenery.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, plants, unique drinks, vegan options, gear.
about:
Madrid's Green Oasis: Where Coffee Passion Blooms

Born from a Canadian expatriate's unwavering quest for the perfect cup, this Madrid gem is a testament to true coffee dedication. Before opening, the founder's commitment led him and his baristas to London, training with the world-renowned Square Mile roasters. This passion birthed a truly unique hybrid concept: a vibrant specialty coffee shop seamlessly intertwined with a lush plant store, offering everything from exotic orchids to resilient cacti.

It's a place where the aroma of freshly brewed coffee mingles with the earthy scent of greenery, creating an unparalleled urban sanctuary.The café's multi-roaster program is a connoisseur's dream, featuring house espresso from esteemed roasters like Puchero (Valladolid), Right Side (Barcelona), and Square Mile (UK). They proudly serve what's hailed as the "best decaf in the world" from Square Mile, ensuring no one misses out on exceptional flavor.

Beyond classic brews, their creative menu boasts signature delights such as the refreshing Orange Latte and invigorating espresso tonic. With a "50% Vegano" menu, diverse palates are catered to, and for those who wish to recreate the magic at home, high-quality equipment from brands like Hario is readily available.
address: Calle del Mesón de Paredes, 30, 28012 Madrid

name: HanSo Café
excerpt_short: Madrid's Hanso Cafe: Chinese heritage, Spanish charm, specialty coffee.
excerpt_long: Hanso Cafe: Madrid's unique blend of Chinese heritage & Spanish culture, serving scientific specialty coffee & unconventional delights.
summary: Madrid's Hanso Cafe blends vibrant Chinese heritage with Spanish culture, offering scientifically crafted specialty coffee and an unconventional menu in a uniquely designed space.
flavour_profile: Asian-Spanish fusion, precise brews, unique waffles, matcha cakes.
about:
Hanso Cafe: Madrid's Heartfelt Blend of Cultures and Coffee

Step into Hanso Cafe, a unique Madrid establishment where vibrant Chinese heritage gracefully intertwines with local Spanish culture. Founded by a passionate family of Chinese immigrants, including Nico and his wife who pursued their specialty coffee dream, this third-wave haven boasts an inviting ambiance. Its distinct blend of industrial and vintage design with a noticeable Asian influence creates a welcoming space unlike any other, pushing culinary boundaries with an unconventional menu and truly distinctive cafe experience.

Dedicated to coffee excellence, Hanso meticulously crafts each brew using a scientific approach, employing tools like a refractometer for optimal extraction. They proudly serve beans from Barcelona's renowned Nømad specialty coffee roaster, prepared on a top-tier La Marzocco GB5 espresso machine. Beyond exceptional coffee, indulge in their signature ham and cheese waffle topped with seaweed flakes, or sip an inventive Red Velvet Latte. Don't miss their artisanal, spongy homemade cakes, especially the unique matcha flavor, offering a dense, lemon-cake-like texture.

name: Unfltrd Coffee
excerpt_short: Rotterdam's sanctuary for extraordinary, competition-level coffee & innovative vegan treats.
excerpt_long: A sanctuary for extraordinary coffee. Discover rare, fruit-fermented beans & competition-level pours by Manhattan Coffee Roasters.
summary: Experience unparalleled coffee at our sanctuary. Featuring Rotterdam's Manhattan Coffee Roasters, we specialize in high-end, experimental, fruit-fermented beans & expert pour-overs. Vegan treats too!
flavour_profile: Competition coffee, experimental ferments, pour-over, vegan
about:
A Sanctuary for the Extraordinary: Unveiling a New Coffee Experience

Step into a realm where coffee transcends the ordinary, a sanctuary dedicated to the pursuit of unparalleled flavor and experience. This unique establishment proudly pours exclusively from the world-renowned, Rotterdam-based Manhattan Coffee Roasters, instantly setting a benchmark for quality and distinction. Here, the vibe is one of refined exploration, specializing in high-end, "competition-level" coffees that challenge perceptions and delight the senses.

It's a place for those who seek not just a cup, but a journey into the extraordinary, curated with passion and precision.The coffee portfolio is a testament to innovation, featuring rare and experimental beans, including those that have undergone groundbreaking fermentation processes with fruits like watermelon – a true house specialty. Each pour-over is an expertly prepared ritual, showcasing the nuanced complexities of these exceptional coffees.

To complement this exquisite beverage experience, a thoughtfully curated selection of vegan-friendly food and pastries awaits, ensuring every visit is a harmonious blend of taste and discovery. This is more than a coffee shop; it's a destination for the discerning palate, a celebration of specialty coffee at its most inventive.

name: Dabov Specialty Coffee
excerpt_short: Where jazz meets the world's best beans, precisely roasted for unparalleled taste.
excerpt_long: Dabov Specialty Coffee: Bulgaria's COE juror Jordan Dabov crafts exclusive, world-class beans with jazz-like precision.
summary: Dabov Specialty Coffee: Jordan Dabov, a jazz trumpeter and COE juror, orchestrates world-class beans into an unparalleled coffee experience at his Sofia "coffee laboratory."
flavour_profile: Jazz roots, COE exclusivity, advanced roasting, coffee lab.
about:
Dabov Specialty Coffee: Where Jazz Meets the World's Best Beans

Step into the world of Dabov Specialty Coffee, where the passion of a former professional jazz trumpeter, Jordan Dabov, orchestrates an unparalleled coffee experience. Since 2008, Dabov has been the driving force behind Bulgaria's specialty coffee movement, evangelizing a market that once didn't exist. Jordan's unique position as Bulgaria's sole Cup of Excellence (COE) juror since 2013 grants exclusive access to the world's most coveted beans, leading to historic purchases like Guatemala's #1 COE lot in 2016.

His global authority is further cemented by his role as a European judge at elite international events, including the Best of Panama 2019, where record-priced Geishas were selected.At Dabov, precision meets passion. Their roastery, equipped with a Probat for nano-lots and a high-tech Loring roaster, ensures uniform perfection in every bean. Commemorating their founding year, their 200.8-gram packages offer a week's worth of exceptional coffee. The flagship Sofia location transcends a typical café, operating as a "coffee laboratory" brimming with brewing devices, offering a succinct menu of meticulously prepared espresso and filter coffees.

Beyond the cup, Jordan Dabov, a certified European Specialty Coffee Association instructor, has cultivated over 3,000 coffee professionals through the DABOV Specialty Coffee Academy, sharing his profound knowledge and elevating the craft.

name: Geisha Specialty Coffee
excerpt_short: Madrid's serene haven for exquisite Geisha coffee, precision-brewed perfection.
excerpt_long: Madrid's serene, Japanese-inspired haven for exquisite Geisha coffee. Precision brewing and award-winning latte art define this unique sanctuary.
summary: Madrid's serene Geisha haven offers unparalleled precision in every cup. Experience Japanese-inspired calm, direct-sourced Panamanian Geisha, and minimalist brewing for an exquisite coffee journey.
flavour_profile: Exclusive Geisha, champion barista, tranquil Japanese haven.
about:
Geisha's Haven: Madrid's Sanctuary of Precision Coffee

Nestled in Madrid, this specialty coffee haven offers a "remanso de calma" with its serene, Japanese-inspired ambiance, a stark contrast to the city's bustle. Its location wasn't left to chance, but meticulously chosen through a data-driven viability study. Founder Juan, in collaboration with "The Bar&Bar" consultancy, meticulously crafted the café's concept and menu.

The focus here is on the revered Geisha coffee, particularly from Panama, sourced through excellent relationships with producers in countries like Ecuador, ensuring unparalleled quality and a true connection to origin.The café's philosophy, "When a grain is good, the best thing is not to disturb it," guides their minimalist brewing ethos, utilizing methods like the V60 to highlight natural flavors. Precision is paramount, with the xBloom, an all-in-one smart coffee machine, ensuring repeatable excellence.

Leading the bar is Jorge Barcos, a recognized latte art champion, having won "Nivel Verde" at The Lags Battle España in 2024. Complementing the exquisite coffee is a curated, short menu where every item, from the cheesecake to the açai bowl, has undergone rigorous selection.
address: Calle de las Navas de Tolosa, 6, 28013 Madrid

name: Naji Specialty Coffee
excerpt_short: Naji: Madrid's specialty coffee oasis, blending Arab warmth with pistachio magic.
excerpt_long: Naji Specialty Coffee: Madrid's unique blend of Arab hospitality, specialty coffee, and the unforgettable, heritage-inspired pistachio latte.
summary: Naji Specialty Coffee: Where Arab hospitality meets specialty coffee artistry. Experience Madrid's unique pistachio latte, a taste of Iraqi heritage, and a passion for every meticulously brewed cup.
flavour_profile: Pistachio latte, Iraqi soul, warm encounters, manual brews
about:
Naji Specialty Coffee: A Taste of Tradition and Passion in Madrid

Naji Specialty Coffee isn't just a café; it's a vibrant "point of encounter" where the warmth of Arab hospitality meets the art of specialty coffee. Founded by Naji, whose philosophy, "I don't drink coffee to wake up, I wake up to drink coffee," encapsulates a profound passion for the coffee experience. Their most celebrated creation, the pistachio latte, stands as a unique jewel in Madrid's specialty coffee scene.

This signature drink, inspired by Naji's Iraqi heritage and Middle Eastern sweet traditions, features a secret, creamy recipe, meticulously prepared by emulsifying a special pistachio cream with specialty espresso, topped with cremated milk and freshly grated pistachio.Beyond this iconic beverage, Naji Specialty Coffee extends its pistachio artistry to a delightful array of pastries, including a rich pistachio cheesecake and delicate pistachio financiers. The café's ambiance, infused with the inviting spirit of Arab culture, invites guests to linger and connect.

For coffee purists, Naji offers a diverse range of manual brewing methods like V60, Chemex, and Aeropress, expertly highlighting the intricate nuances of their carefully selected coffee beans. It's a destination where every cup tells a story of heritage, craftsmanship, and an unwavering love for coffee.

name: Osom Coffee
excerpt_short: Osom: Stereotype-free hub for curated coffee, community, and connection.
excerpt_long: Osom: A stereotype-free coffee sanctuary fostering genuine connection, curated craft, and community across three unique spaces.
summary: Osom is a stereotype-free community hub redefining specialty coffee. With three unique spaces, it fosters genuine connection, curated craft, and a hyper-local Madrid coffee experience.
flavour_profile: Community hub, monochrome aesthetic, co-working, local roast
about:
Osom: A Sanctuary of Connection and Curated Coffee Culture

Osom, envisioned by founders Martina Ocampo and Mey Paniza, redefines the specialty coffee experience as a truly "stereotype-free" community hub. This innovative concept actively dismantles traditional barriers between baristas and customers, fostering genuine connection across its three distinct spaces: the creative "Osom Coffee Atelier," the culinary-focused "Osom Coffee Cantine," and the versatile "Osom Coffeehouse," a dynamic venue for workshops, meetings, and even yoga.

Each location is a testament to their commitment to a welcoming, inclusive atmosphere.The soul of Osom is further expressed through its hyper-local partnership with Madrid's Hola Coffee, ensuring a meticulously sourced and expertly roasted coffee experience. This dedication extends to the cafes' striking monochrome aesthetic, featuring a curated collection of black and white ceramic mugs from ÅOOMI STUDIO's "SALT," "MESS," and "LUNA" collections, creating a visually cohesive and calming environment.

For those seeking productivity, the "Osom Coffeehouse" offers a dedicated co-working space with large tables and charging outlets, inviting patrons to immerse themselves in this unique blend of community and craft.

name: Camden Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Camden Coffee Roasters: Madrid's innovative roaster with a London soul.
excerpt_long: Madrid's Camden Coffee Roasters: a third-gen legacy blending London spirit with innovative, meticulously roasted specialty beans.
summary: Madrid's Camden Coffee Roasters: a third-gen, SCAE Master Roaster infuses London spirit into ONLY GOOD COFFEE. Discover rare varietals & experimental micro-lots like 200hr fermented beans. Innovation meets tradition.
flavour_profile: Third-gen heritage, experimental roasts, rare varietals, education.
about:
Camden Coffee Roasters: A Legacy of Innovation in Madrid's Coffee Scene

Camden Coffee Roasters brings a rich, third-generation roasting heritage and a distinct London spirit to the heart of Madrid. Guided by an SCAE Certified Master Roaster, their dedication to "ONLY GOOD COFFEE" is evident in every meticulously sourced and roasted bean.

This micro-roaster embodies a unique blend of British and Spanish coffee culture, offering a vibrant atmosphere where passion for quality and tradition converge to create an exceptional coffee experience.For the adventurous palate, Camden Coffee Roasters is a haven of innovation. They proudly feature experimental micro-lots, such as the intriguing "Colombia La Cabaña – 200hr Fermented," alongside rare varietals like the exquisite "Colombia Yellow Papayo."

Their commitment to high-altitude beans ensures complex and nuanced flavors in every cup. Beyond the brew, they foster a deeper appreciation for coffee through their "Roastery 101" workshop, inviting enthusiasts to explore the intricate world of specialty coffee.

name: Randall Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Madrid's pioneering roaster: direct farm-to-cup, fresh roasted in Mercado.
excerpt_long: Madrid's pioneering micro-roaster. Direct farm-to-cup, roasted-to-order perfection, embodying "coffee & kindness."
summary: Madrid's pioneering micro-roaster, Randall Coffee Roasters, crafts exceptional coffee with kindness. From direct farm partnerships to fresh-roasted perfection in Mercado de Vallehermoso, they embody "love the process."
flavour_profile: Madrid pioneer, direct trade, farm-to-cup, market hub, consulting.
about:
Randall Coffee Roasters: Crafting Madrid's Coffee Culture, Bean by Bean

Inspired by the UK's advanced specialty coffee industry, founder Barry Randall pioneered Madrid's micro-roasting scene in 2013. His philosophy, deeply rooted in "coffee and kindness" and "love the process," drives a commitment to quality and meaningful relationships. Randall Coffee Roasters meticulously manages its entire supply chain, directly importing beans via land, sea, and air, ensuring unparalleled control from source to cup.

This dedication extends to active collaborations with coffee farms, including a direct relationship with a Colombian farm, where they coordinate process improvements to enhance quality from cultivation to roasting.Experience their passion firsthand at their unique coffee shop, nestled within Madrid's vibrant Mercado de Vallehermoso. Every coffee is roasted to order, guaranteeing the freshest possible product and peak flavor.

Beyond the brew, Randall Coffee Roasters champions the specialty coffee community, offering consulting services to aspiring cafe owners, assisting with everything from business concepts to sourcing high-end equipment from brands like La Marzocco and Mahlkonig. They even bring their expertise to private events with a professional "Coffee Cart" service, truly embodying their role as a cornerstone of Madrid's evolving coffee culture.

name: The Fix Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Madrid's Nordic micro-lot sanctuary: exclusive roasts, artistic flair.
excerpt_long: Madrid's micro-lot sanctuary with a Nordic soul. Hugo roasts exclusive, seasonal coffees. Marta crafts brunch & treats. Global passion, local craft.
summary: Madrid's micro-lot sanctuary with a Nordic soul. The Fix offers meticulously roasted, exclusive micro-lots, stunning latte art, and an all-day brunch in a minimalist, modern ambiance.
flavour_profile: Exclusive micro-lots, Nordic design, artisan bakery, latte art.
about:
The Fix: Madrid's Micro-Lot Sanctuary with a Nordic Soul

Founded in 2018 by the international duo Hugo Jimenez (Colombia) and Marta Castillo (Madrid), The Fix brings a meticulously crafted coffee experience to Madrid. Head Roaster Hugo, drawing inspiration from specialty coffee pioneer Tim Wendelboe, roasts exclusive, frequently changing micro-lots in-house, sourced seasonally from diverse origins.

Patrons can expect exceptional selections, like a natural process from Benjamin Dianous in Panama or a unique offering from Eduardo Rojas in Colombia. Beyond the cup, Hugo's latte art is a pure spectacle, adding an artistic flourish to every meticulously prepared drink.The café's minimalist, Nordic-inspired aesthetic, featuring marble and wood, creates a clean, modern ambiance perfect for savoring their exquisite offerings. Complementing the world-class coffee is an all-day brunch and a delightful array of freshly made baked goods, expertly managed by Marta.

Her dedication ensures a diverse selection, including organic, gluten-free, and vegan options, catering to every palate. The Fix isn't just a coffee shop; it's a destination where global passion meets local craftsmanship, offering a truly unique and soulful experience.

name: Acid Café
excerpt_short: Madrid's Nordic roast pioneer, crafting unique coffee & culinary experiences.
excerpt_long: Nomad Coffee: Madrid's pioneer of Nordic roasts, blending minimalist design with innovative culinary and a captivating public bakehouse.
summary: Nomad Coffee: Madrid's pioneer of Nordic roasting, blending minimalist design with innovative culinary artistry and a public bakehouse for an unparalleled specialty coffee experience.
flavour_profile: Nordic roasts, fermented foods, minimalist design, artisan bakery.
about:
Nomad Coffee: Madrid's Nordic Roasting Pioneer

Founded by Argentine photographer-turned-entrepreneur Fede Graciano, this specialty coffee haven was born from a vision to revolutionize Madrid's coffee scene. It boldly introduced the lighter Nordic roast style, a stark contrast to the city's traditional palate, by exclusively partnering with acclaimed Danish roaster La Cabra.

This commitment to exceptional coffee is underscored by a visually striking, high-performance Kees van der Westen Mirage espresso machine, ensuring every cup is a testament to quality and precision.Beyond the brew, the café cultivates a unique soul and character, defined by a minimalist, industrial-chic aesthetic from Plantea Estudio, featuring a six-meter stainless steel counter and vibrant red Ferrés tiles.

Its in-house bakehouse transforms bread and pastry making into a public spectacle with street-facing kneading tables. The culinary program is equally innovative, employing extensive fermentation techniques to craft unique dishes like miso caramel french toast with turmeric chantilly. With a strong design-led identity, the brand has expanded its distinctive experience to Berlin, near the studios of renowned artists.

name: Hola Coffee
excerpt_short: Madrid's flavor alchemists, crafting accessible, experimental specialty coffee.
excerpt_long: Hola Coffee: Madrid's flavor alchemists. Champion-led, they innovate roasting to make exceptional, approachable specialty coffee for everyone.
summary: Hola Coffee: Madrid's flavor alchemists, led by a Spanish Barista Champion, innovate roasting to unlock unique profiles, making specialty coffee approachable and exceptional for all.
flavour_profile: Champion Barista, Flavor Lab, Inclusive Coffee, Unique Drinks
about:
Hola Coffee: Madrid's Flavor Alchemists & Accessibility Advocates

Founded by friends Pablo and Nolo, Hola Coffee embodies a journey from passionate bloggers to pioneering roasters, driven by a philosophy of inclusivity. Co-founder Pablo Caballero, the 2016 Spanish Barista Champion and World Barista Championships competitor, brings unparalleled expertise.

Their Lucero roastery is a vibrant "laboratory of new flavor profiles," where innovative roasting techniques are constantly explored to unlock coffee's full potential, ensuring specialty coffee is approachable and exceptional for everyone.Beyond their exquisite coffee, Hola Coffee is dedicated to demystifying the specialty world through a dedicated academy and an insightful podcast. Their commitment to creativity extends to unique offerings like the delightful blueberry matcha latte.

This spirit of innovation also led them to co-found Mision Cafe, blending their renowned coffee with delectable in-house baked goods. Starting from humble beginnings renting equipment, their independent roastery, established in 2019, now stands as a beacon of accessible excellence and experimental flavor.

name: Misión Café
excerpt_short: Mision Café: Specialty coffee with purpose, empowering community, crafted with care.
excerpt_long: Mision Café: Artisanal specialty coffee with a powerful purpose, empowering communities and crafting exceptional brews with care.
summary: Mision Café crafts artisanal specialty coffee with a powerful purpose: empowering individuals with disabilities and uplifting communities. Every brew fosters hope in a heartwarming space.
flavour_profile: Social impact, specialty coffee, direct trade, artisanal, inclusive.
about:
Mision Café: Coffee with a Conscience, Crafted with Care

Mision Café isn't just a coffee shop; it's a beacon of hope and inclusion, where every cup tells a story of purpose. Dedicated to empowering individuals with disabilities and uplifting vulnerable communities, Mision Café provides real opportunities, training, and respect, transforming lives one brew at a time. This profound social mission infuses the space with a unique, heartwarming vibe, making it a place where community thrives and every visit contributes to a greater good.

Their commitment extends to direct trade with local farmers, ensuring not only exceptional quality but also fair practices that support the very roots of their artisanal coffee.Step into Mision Café and experience an artisanal approach to specialty coffee, where responsibly roasted, carefully selected beans are the stars. Baristas here are true craftspeople, specializing in preparations that highlight the unique flavors of each bean, with a particular emphasis on exquisite filtered coffee methods.

Beyond the brew, Mision Café fosters community engagement through regular events, inviting customers to explore diverse coffee varieties and preparation techniques. Complementing their exceptional coffee is a curated menu of handcrafted cookies and baked goods, perfectly designed to enhance your coffee journey. It's a place where passion for coffee meets a powerful purpose, creating an unforgettable experience.

name: Toma Café
excerpt_short: Madrid's pioneering roastery, origin-focused, empowering women in coffee.
excerpt_long: Toma Café: Madrid's specialty coffee pioneer. Roasting for origin's sensory image, with "no logo" traceability & women-led "Matriarcas."
summary: Toma Café: Madrid's 2011 specialty coffee trailblazer. With "no logo" traceability and a focus on origin's sensory image, they set the standard, even empowering women in coffee.
flavour_profile: Pioneering, traceable, Matriarcas, diverse brews, espresso-tonic.
about:
Toma Café: Madrid's Pioneering Heart of Specialty Coffee

Born from a desire for exceptional coffee, Toma Café, founded in 2011 by Spanish-Argentinian couple Patricia Alda and Santiago Rigoni, ignited Madrid's specialty coffee revolution. Leaving advertising careers behind, they became trailblazers, establishing one of Spain's first third-wave coffee establishments and setting an unparalleled standard for quality and craft.

Their unique "no logo" packaging, a testament to their advertising roots, champions traceability, detailing every aspect from producer to harvest. Beyond their three vibrant Madrid locations and dedicated roastery, Toma Café has fostered a thriving coffee community, offering invaluable training and support to aspiring entrepreneurs. At its core, Toma Café's roasting philosophy is to deliver a "sensory image of the origin," meticulously highlighting the natural sweetness and respecting the producer's intention in every bean.

Their diverse brewing repertoire includes the meticulous Aeropress and Siphon, prepared with care during quieter moments. Don't miss their adventurous and refreshing signature "espresso-tonic." Furthermore, their "Matriarcas" coffee line proudly supports women in Bolivian coffee cooperatives, promoting economic independence and celebrating their vital contributions to the coffee world.

name: Milkees
excerpt_short: Lisbon's Milkees: In-house roasted coffee, Michelin-level pastries, local charm.
excerpt_long: Lisbon's Milkees: where Michelin-starred pastries meet expertly in-house roasted coffee and a warm, local community vibe.
summary: Milkees: Lisbon's haven for artisanal pastries by a Michelin-starred chef and exceptional coffee, featuring their own in-house roasted beans. A true local, pet-friendly gem.
flavour_profile: Chef pastries, in-house roasts, local coffee, fresh cookies, community.
about:
Milkees: Lisbon's Authentic Coffee & Pastry Gem

Discover Milkees, a Lisbon haven where the aroma of freshly baked goods and expertly brewed coffee creates an irresistible allure. Co-founder João Erthal, a former Michelin-starred pastry chef, brings a refined touch to their exquisite, house-made pastries, with the name "Milkees" celebrating the fresh milk central to their creations. Renowned for freshly baked cookies, new batches emerge from the oven every morning.

This pet-friendly cafe fosters a friendly, community-oriented atmosphere, inviting staff and customers to share stories in its charming, less touristy neighborhood, offering a truly local experience.Beyond the delectable pastries, Milkees is a true destination for coffee lovers. They proudly showcase exceptional coffees from esteemed local Lisbon roasters like Roastberry and Olisopo.

Adding to their unique character, Milkees also roasts its own coffee beans in-house, ensuring a diverse and high-quality selection. This dedication to both artisanal baking and specialty coffee, combined with a genuinely warm and welcoming vibe, makes Milkees a beloved local spot and a must-visit for an authentic Lisbon experience.

name: The Mill
excerpt_short: Lisbon's unique blend: Australian brunch, Portuguese soul, wood-fired coffee.
excerpt_long: Lisbon's unique coffee journey: Australian brunch meets Portuguese soul, featuring wood-fired Arabica, single-origin espressos & art.
summary: Discover Lisbon's unique fusion: Australian brunch, Portuguese soul, and wood-fired, hand-roasted Arabica. Enjoy distinct single-origin espressos in a creative hub that transforms from coffee haven to wine bar.
flavour_profile: Wood-fire roast, single-origin, ceramics, Scandi-design.
about:
Where Australian Brunch Meets Portuguese Soul: A Lisbon Coffee Journey

Discover a unique blend of cultures at this Lisbon gem, where the vibrant coffee and brunch scene of Australia harmonizes with the warm embrace of Portuguese hospitality. Every cup tells a story, crafted from locally sourced Arabica beans, meticulously hand-roasted over a wood fire at a local Lisbon roastery. The experience is elevated by a high-end, custom-made La Marzocco espresso machine, serving distinct single-origin espressos – from bright and acidic to rich, smoky, and earthy profiles.

By day, it's a specialty coffee haven with a serene Scandinavian-inspired interior; by night, it gracefully transforms into an inviting wine and tapas bar. Founded by Australian Paul Miller, who brought his international expertise from London to Lisbon, this café is more than just a coffee shop. It's a creative hub where passion for exceptional coffee meets artistry.

Beyond the exquisite brews and delightful brunch offerings, you'll find their own unique, hand-painted ceramics, adding another layer of bespoke charm to the experience. It’s a destination that truly embodies a distinctive character, inviting you to savor every moment, from the first sip of coffee to the last bite of tapas.
address: Rua do Poço dos Negros 1, 1200-335 Lisboa

name: SO Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Emotional, tiny-batch roasted micro-lots by passionate, award-winning roasters.
excerpt_long: SO Coffee: Emotionally-driven, tiny-batch roasted micro-lots by a lawyer, dancer & musician trio. Passion in every unique cup.
summary: SO Coffee: Born from passion, not banking. Unlikely founders pour emotion into every tiny batch of micro-lot coffee, directly sourced. Experience the soul of Porto's award-winning roastery.
flavour_profile: Artisanal micro-lots, champion baristas, direct trade, Porto soul.
about:
SO Coffee: An Emotional Journey in Every Tiny Batch

SO Coffee isn't just a roastery; it's a testament to passion, born from an unlikely trio: a lawyer, a ballet dancer, and a musician. This diverse, non-traditional background infuses their motto, "We are not bankers. We can be a little emotional about coffee," with genuine soul and character. Named "SO" as a diminutive for "southwest," reflecting Portugal's geographical position and their hometown of Porto, their journey began uniquely, nestled within "The Feeting Room" lifestyle stores in Porto and Lisbon, setting an unconventional tone from the start.

Their dedication to exceptional coffee shines through their "tiny batch" roasting, never exceeding 30kg, ensuring unparalleled freshness and a focus on exquisite micro-lots from single-origin, single-harvest plantations. This commitment extends to direct relationships, personally meeting farmers across Ecuador, El Salvador, Colombia, and Brazil to exchange ideas.

Their expertise is undeniable, with baristas like Rui Pedrosa achieving Portugal's best-ever result at the World Barista Championships in 2022 (top 15) and Luiz Lahutte Motta winning the national Brewers Cup in 2024. Their expansive three-floor Lisbon roastery is a true temple to coffee.
address: Rua do Campo de Ourique 193, 1350-062 Lisboa

name: Comēte Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Lisbon's industrial chic roastery: French passion, sustainable, precise, with pastries.
excerpt_long: Lisbon's industrial chic roastery, founded by Parisian pharmacists, offers sustainable, precise roasts & French pastries.
summary: Lisbon's industrial chic micro-roastery, founded by French pharmacists, crafts sustainable, meticulously roasted specialty coffees. Enjoy unique brews and Parisian pastries in a vibrant, green-powered space.
flavour_profile: Parisian passion, sustainable roasts, French pastries, Lisbon gem.
about:
Lisbon's Industrial Chic Roastery with a French Heart

Nestled in Lisbon, a true hidden gem emerges from the passion project of Alexandra and Adrien, former Parisian pharmacists who traded their careers for the art of coffee. This French couple's micro-roastery is a testament to dedication, where every bean is roasted on-site using a state-of-the-art Stronghold S7X electric roaster. Their commitment extends beyond flavor, with the roaster powered by 100% green energy and all coffee bags being fully recyclable. Even their shipping materials, sourced from recycled European content, underscore a profound dedication to sustainability.

The café itself exudes an industrial chic vibe, defined by sleek poured concrete floors and a striking bar crafted from modular metal elements, creating a unique neighborhood atmosphere. Here, the meticulous process of sample roasting and evaluation ensures unparalleled quality across their selection of classic and experimental coffees.

Complementing their exceptional brews is a small, tempting array of home-baked French pastries, including delicate Madeleines, offering a taste of Parisian charm in the heart of Lisbon. It's a place where passion, precision, and sustainability brew together.

name: Neighbourhood Café
excerpt_short: Lisbon's soulful roastery: unexpected origins, local craft, award-winning brunch.
excerpt_long: Lisbon's award-winning coffee haven, born from an unexpected quest, blends local craft, unique brunch, and soulful community vibes.
summary: Lisbon's unexpected coffee haven, born from a spontaneous vision, blends specialty roasts with local craft, unique brunch, and a vibrant community spirit.
flavour_profile: Lisbon's best brunch, local craft, unique design, global menu.
about:
Lisbon's Unexpected Gem: A Journey of Craft and Community

Born from an unexpected quest for a laundromat in Lisbon, this specialty coffee haven emerged from a founder's spontaneous vision after fleeing Iceland in 2010. With no prior café experience, an intensive 50-day build, and rigorous testing of specialty coffee and organic Portuguese wines, this establishment quickly captured hearts, earning the coveted title of "best restaurant in Lisbon for brunch" by Condé Nast readers.

Each location boasts a distinct character; the Graça café, a testament to deep local collaboration, showcases the talents of a local architect, woodworker, artist, and framer, while the Santos spot invites the city in with its floor-to-ceiling arched windows, blurring the lines between indoors and out.Beyond its celebrated specialty coffee, the café champions a broader commitment to local craft, offering a curated selection of Portuguese craft beers, organic wines, and hard kombucha.

Their food menu intentionally defies typical café fare, featuring signature delights like breakfast burritos with an optional kimchi kick, perfectly prepared Turkish eggs, and mouth-watering smash burgers. This unique blend of unexpected origins, rapid realization, community collaboration, and a truly distinctive menu creates a vibrant, soulful experience that goes far beyond a simple coffee stop.

name: Acento Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Acento: Highlighting distinct seasonal profiles, innovative, ethically sourced flavors.
excerpt_long: Acento crafts unique coffee flavors, meticulously highlighting distinct origins through innovative processes like Orange Peel and ethical direct trade.
summary: Acento crafts unique coffee experiences, highlighting distinct seasonal profiles with 'acentos.' From innovative Orange Peel processes to ethically sourced, women-led farms, we celebrate origin and flavor in every cup.
flavour_profile: Direct trade, experimental, women-produced, global espresso, terroir.
about:
Acento: Crafting Flavors, Celebrating Origins

Acento is more than a coffee roastery; it's a vibrant exploration of flavor, built around the concept of "acentos" – meticulously highlighting the distinct profiles of seasonal coffees. Their commitment to unique character shines through direct trade partnerships, like with Juan Urquia of Finca La Montanita in Honduras, ensuring exceptional quality and ethical sourcing. Innovation is at their core, exemplified by the "Orange Peel Experimental process," infusing beans with a distinct citrus finish.

They also champion social impact with their "Women Power" coffee from Veracruz, Mexico, a testament to female ownership and craftsmanship from farm to cup, and collaborate with APG to support small-scale farmers in Patlanalan.The roastery's portfolio is a journey through diverse terroirs and processing methods. Their dynamic house blend, "El Idolo," evolves throughout the year, showcasing a rotating selection of three single-origin coffees.

Acento’s dedication to origin is evident in offerings like the Aricha Adorsi Natural from Ethiopia's Yirgacheffe region, celebrating coffee's birthplace. Beyond their beans, the café offers espresso drinks inspired by unique serving styles from cultures worldwide, inviting patrons to experience global coffee traditions with every sip.

name: Comoba
excerpt_short: Historic pharmacy reborn: sustainable coffee, craft, and local, organic food.
excerpt_long: Historic pharmacy meets modern design. Savor sustainable, organic coffee, exceptional Cold Brew, and a meat-free menu.
summary: COMOBA: A specialty coffee haven where historic architecture meets modern design. Experience sustainable, small-batch roasted beans, acclaimed Cold Brew, and a mindful, meat-free menu. A unique blend of soul & craft.
flavour_profile: Historic charm, plant-based, ethical, local, artisanal.
about:
COMOBA: A Sanctuary of Sustainable Coffee & Design

Step into COMOBA, a specialty coffee haven where history breathes new life. Housed in a former pharmacy, its century-old arches and original flagstone floors blend seamlessly with a modern, minimalist aesthetic, creating a unique and soulful atmosphere. Here, the coffee experience is paramount, featuring fair trade and organic beans roasted in small batches by partners Outpost Coffee Roasters & Sgt. Martinho.

Celebrated by the specialty coffee community for its exceptional Cold Brew, COMOBA also crafts its own almond, coconut, and rice milks in-house, alongside innovative offerings like the intriguing Charcoal Latte.Beyond the brew, COMOBA's commitment to sustainability shines through its entirely meat-free menu. Focusing on seasonal, organic produce sourced directly from local Portuguese farmers and fishermen, every dish is free of refined sugars and processed products, minimizing environmental impact.

This dedication to craftsmanship extends to every detail, from artisanal matcha tea directly sourced from Japan to the beautiful, handmade Portuguese ceramics from the Algarve region. COMOBA is a true celebration of local artisans and a testament to thoughtful, high-quality living.

name: Olisipo Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Olisipo: Lisbon's soulful roastery, ethically sourced, community-driven, unique brews.
excerpt_long: Olisipo: Lisbon's soulful roastery, born from an epic journey to coffee's source. Ethical, community-driven, and a hub for unique brews.
summary: Olisipo: Lisbon's soul, forged by a journey to coffee's origin. We ethically roast unique beans, inviting you to share our passion in a vibrant community hub. Experience coffee's true essence.
flavour_profile: Ethiopian roots, community blends, ethical sourcing, hidden gem.
about:
Olisipo: Journey to the Source, Lisbon's Soulful Coffee Experience

Olisipo, a hidden gem in Lisbon's Ajuda parish, embodies a unique soul forged by an extraordinary journey. Co-founder Antony Watson's epic cycling expedition from the UK to Ethiopia, the birthplace of coffee, profoundly shaped the roastery's ethos, instilling unparalleled understanding and respect for the bean. This commitment extends to their "open-door policy," inviting neighbors to engage during roasting hours and actively seeking feedback for their unique house blend.

Beyond coffee, the space thrives as a vibrant cultural hub, featuring local art exhibitions and hosting regular workshops and cupping events, educating and engaging the community.Their dedication to ethical and transparent sourcing is paramount, emphasizing direct trade relationships with producers like Colombia's La Lomita farm, ensuring sustainable prices. Olisipo's curated coffee selection includes unique offerings such as a washed Tanzanian Peaberry alongside a naturally processed Brazilian, appealing to local preferences while introducing adventurous sensory profiles.
