Founded by Patrik Rolf, the esteemed Danish Barista Champion 2023 and 2019 World Brewers Cup runner-up, this specialty coffee haven redefines the coffee experience. Patrik, also the author of "From Nerd to Pro" and host of "Coffee with April" on YouTube, infuses his profound expertise into every detail.

Here, coffee transcends a mere beverage, elevating to an art form with a chef-like precision and a commitment to ethical sourcing where farmers dictate their coffee's price.

Step into a world where coffee meets culinary innovation, highlighted by a unique 6-course coffee tasting menu, priced at $120, designed to rival Michelin-starred dining. Indulge in house specialties like their signature drink, where coconut and normal milk are frozen and concentrated for intense flavor, or a refined espresso shaved ice with creme and mascarpone.

The 2.0 roastery, equipped with Loring and Stronghold machines, ensures scientific precision. The space itself, adorned with Rioto Miyaki's art and a Japanese noren, offers a distinct cultural aesthetic, further enhanced by their own "April Coffee Brewer" and international Brewers Cup competition.

name: Coffee Collective
excerpt_short: Pioneering direct trade, transparently connecting farmer, roaster, barista.
excerpt_long: Coffee Collective: Pioneering direct trade, transparently connecting farmers, roasters, and baristas for exceptional, ethically sourced coffee.
summary: Coffee Collective: Our "collective" philosophy unites farmer, roaster, barista. Discover transparent direct trade, meticulous roasting, and exceptional coffee, crafted with integrity.
flavour_profile: Direct Trade, Champion Baristas, Transparent Roasting, B Corp, Unique Cafes, Bespoke Roasts, Pioneering Origins, Collective Bakery, Coffee Education.
about:
Coffee Collective: A Journey of Transparency, Taste, and Education

Founded in 2007 by a team including World Barista Champion (2006) Klaus Thomsen, Coffee Collective embodies a unique "collective" philosophy. Their iconic triangle logo symbolizes the direct connection between farmer, roaster, and barista, a commitment reflected in their pioneering direct trade model. They pay prices far above market level, ensuring financial sustainability for producers and offering unprecedented transparency by detailing farmer payments.

This dedication to ethical sourcing and quality is showcased at their Frederiksberg roastery, where a glass wall invites customers to observe the roasting process and take courses. Certified as a B Corp, their high standards of social and environmental performance are evident.

Beyond their clean café interiors, one notable branch, Telefonkiosken, offers a charmingly unique experience within a historic phone booth. Coffee Collective meticulously develops individual roasting profiles, separating roasts for espresso and filter to highlight each bean's natural flavors.

They actively explore new and challenging regions, like Tolima in Colombia, bringing diverse origins to their customers. Complementing their exceptional coffee, their "Collective Bakery" produces fresh sourdough and exquisite pastries, perfectly designed to enhance the coffee experience. Their commitment to sharing knowledge extends to their book, "The Fundamentals of Excellent Coffee," cementing their role as educators.

name: Respekt Coffee
excerpt_short: Award-winning barista's craft, respecting heritage in every bean.
excerpt_long: Award-winning barista Zdeněk Smrčka crafts exceptional coffee, honoring heritage & small farmers with every respectfully roasted bean.
summary: RESPEKT COFFEE, by award-winning barista Zdeněk Smrčka, honors coffee's heritage. We directly source exceptional beans, preserving tradition and cultural methods for a truly respectful, unique cup.
flavour_profile: Traditional cultivation, cascara, award-winning.
about:
RESPEKT COFFEE: A Legacy of Respect in Every Bean

Born from a deep reverence for coffee culture during the pandemic, RESPEKT COFFEE, founded by multi-award-winning barista Zdeněk Smrčka, embodies a unique soul. Smrčka, a veteran of the specialty coffee scene, initially co-founded "Kávový klub" in 2010 before establishing RESPEKT COFFEE. This evolution solidified a philosophy centered on honoring cultural heritage and preserving traditional cultivation methods, ensuring every bean tells a story from its origin.

Their commitment extends beyond the cup, offering rare CASCARA from Costa Rica's renowned Hacienda Sonora.At RESPEKT COFFEE, the focus is on an exceptional coffee experience, sourcing directly from small farmers with a personal touch. While specific awards for RESPEKT COFFEE aren't detailed on the site, Zdeněk Smrčka's multiple barista competition wins speak to the expertise and passion behind the brand.

Their portfolio features a range of carefully roasted specialty coffees, available online and at their physical locations like Respekt Coffee Radlická and Kafe Kolej, inviting enthusiasts to discover the true essence of coffee, prepared with love and respect.

name: Kavárna co hledá jméno
excerpt_short: Prague's NABR: Industrial grit, artistic soul, exceptional coffee.
excerpt_long: NABR: Industrial grit meets artistic soul. Experience exceptional coffee, unique Eggs Benedict, and creative workshops in a revitalized urban haven.
summary: NABR: Industrial grit meets artistic soul in a revitalized workshop. Experience exceptional, house-roasted specialty coffee and unique culinary delights in this vibrant, art-filled haven.
flavour_profile: NABR roasts, industrial chic, art, unique Eggs Benedict, workshops.
about:
NABR: Where Industrial Grit Meets Artistic Soul and Exceptional Coffee

Step into Kavárna co hledá jméno, a specialty coffee haven born from the adaptive reuse of a derelict carpentry workshop. This unique space, envisioned by co-founder Jakub Zajíc and his team, retains an industrial "urbex" aesthetic, transformed into a vibrant hub where every detail tells a story. From the custom-made bar and signage crafted from a single poplar trunk by artist Jiří Korec, to the striking cosmonaut graffiti by Artwise, the café seamlessly blends raw urban charm with artistic expression.

It's a testament to urban revitalization, echoing the team's earlier success with the "Radlická kulturní sportovna" community project.Beyond its captivating interior, the café is a destination for exceptional coffee and culinary delights. They proudly roast their own specialty coffee under the brand "NABR," offering a curated selection of espresso and filtered coffee methods. While the website doesn't explicitly list awards, their commitment to quality is evident.

Complementing the coffee is a menu featuring homemade desserts, delicious breakfasts, and a famous, unique Eggs Benedict recipe developed by chef Ondra Kynčl, featuring Kampot pepper. The café also fosters a deeper appreciation for coffee through "ArtCoffee" events, combining experiential painting with espresso and insightful workshops.

name: Eska
excerpt_short: Eska: Culinary craft meets coffee excellence, with exclusive custom roasts.
excerpt_long: Eska: Prague's culinary pioneer, where fire-baked gastronomy meets meticulously crafted Nordbeans specialty coffee, perfectly paired.
summary: Eska: Prague's culinary heart, where our exclusive Nordbeans coffee, custom-roasted to perfection, receives the same meticulous attention as our innovative, fire-baked and fermented cuisine.
flavour_profile: Exclusive Nordbeans, custom roasts, expert pairings, unique origins.
about:
Eska: Where Culinary Craft Meets Coffee Excellence

Eska, a pioneering establishment in Prague since 2015, embodies a unique philosophy that seamlessly blends traditional techniques like fire baking and fermentation with modern gastronomy. This commitment to quality extends profoundly to their specialty coffee program, setting them apart as one of the few restaurants in Prague where coffee receives the same meticulous attention as the food.

Their long-standing partnership with Nordbeans, a leading Czech specialty coffee roastery, has blossomed into an exclusive line of coffee, featuring custom roasting profiles specifically designed to complement Eska's renowned sweet and savory baked goods.The Nordbeans x Eska collaboration offers a distinctive coffee portfolio, including a washed Kenyan for espresso, celebrated for its notes of currant, mango, and honey, and a natural Ethiopian for filter, boasting delightful apricot, mango, and tea undertones.

Eska's dedication to coffee excellence is evident through their high-end espresso machines, a dedicated brew bar, cold-drippers, and a team of top-tier baristas, many drawn from other esteemed Prague coffee establishments. These experts not only craft exceptional beverages but also offer thoughtful coffee and dessert pairings, enhancing the overall sensory experience. Eska's commitment to the coffee community is further highlighted by hosting specialized cupping events with Nordbeans, fostering appreciation and knowledge sharing.

name: Onesip Coffee
excerpt_short: Prague's pioneering espresso haven, award-winning, unique roasts & community.
excerpt_long: Prague's pioneering espresso haven since 2016. Award-winning baristas, unique gear, house-roasted Candycane, and rare coffees for a personal experience.
summary: Prague's OG espresso bar since 2016, OneSip pioneers specialty coffee with award-winning expertise, rare lever machines, and a curated selection of house-roasted Candycane and European gems.
flavour_profile: Pioneering espresso, award-winning baristas, unique machines, curated beans, community.
about:
OneSip: Prague's Pioneering Espresso Haven with a Heart

OneSip stands as an "OG espresso bar," having introduced specialty coffee to Prague's city center back in 2016. Co-owner Zdeněk Hýbl, an award-winning barista, brings serious credentials with a 3rd place in the Czech Barista Championship and 9th in Coffee Masters London. Their commitment to exceptional coffee is evident in their gear, featuring a "killer Kees van der Westen" and a rare mechanical espresso lever-type machine, once unique in the Czech Republic.

The playful wooden interior by Arbau studio and distinctive Danish Aoy studio cups add to the inviting atmosphere.Beyond the aesthetics, OneSip offers a meticulously curated coffee selection, including their own house-roasted Candycane brand and collaborations with esteemed European roasters like Round Hill Roastery and The Coffee Collective.

They're known for unique finds, such as a natural Colombian decaf processed with the sugar cane method, a rarity in Prague. Co-owners Zdeněk Hýbl and Adam are deeply embedded in the wider specialty coffee scene, fostering a strong community where staff, like the barista behind "365 Cups of Coffee," actively engage with customers, making every visit a truly personal experience.

name: Coffee Source
excerpt_short: Coffee Source: Real relationships, unique roasts, 15+ years of coffee mastery.
excerpt_long: Coffee Source: 15+ years of real relationships, exceptional roasts, unique varietals, and a comprehensive hub for specialty coffee.
summary: Coffee Source: 15+ years of real relationships with producers, crafting exceptional roasts. Discover unique varietals, experimental processes, and limited editions. Beyond fair, we're real.
flavour_profile: Experimental Roasts, Rare Varietals, Real Sourcing, Pro Gear
about:
Coffee Source: Real Relationships, Exceptional Roasts

Coffee Source embodies a distinctive philosophy: "We are not fair. We are real." This ethos underpins over 15 years of daily specialty coffee roasting, reflecting a deep commitment to authentic, transparent relationships with coffee producers that extend beyond mere certifications. Their extensive experience in the industry is evident in a meticulously curated coffee portfolio, featuring unique varietals and experimental processing methods.

Highlights include "Limited Editions" like the "Colombia El Diviso," boasting intriguing notes of hibiscus, sour worms, and pink grapefruit, alongside a range of specialty blends and single origins.Beyond their exceptional beans, Coffee Source is a comprehensive hub for serious coffee enthusiasts. They offer an impressive selection of high-end professional and home brewing equipment from renowned brands such as La Marzocco, Modbar, Profitec, and Eversys. Innovatively, they also provide specialty espresso capsules, making third-wave coffee accessible and convenient.

Their dedication to fostering coffee knowledge is clear through their educational "Courses," while their commitment to quality is showcased in popular blends like Dark Star and Black Blend. While specific awards or founders aren't prominently featured, their long-standing presence and unique sourcing approach speak volumes about their character and passion for specialty coffee.

name: Mazelab Coffee
excerpt_short: Mazelab: Rare varietals, award-winning precision, unique Japanese-inspired roastery.
excerpt_long: Mazelab Coffee: A mindful micro roastery by a World Brewers Cup finalist, renowned for rare varietals, exquisite Geisha, and precise light roasting.
summary: Mazelab Coffee: A sanctuary of precision. World Brewers Cup finalist Jackie Tran artfully light roasts rare varietals, including sought-after Geishas, with proprietary brewing for an unparalleled coffee experience.
flavour_profile: Rare varietals, innovative brewing, minimalist design, education
about:
Mazelab Coffee: A Sanctuary of Precision and Rare Varietals

Step into Mazelab Coffee, a "mindful micro roastery" and "creative playground" where founder Jackie Tran, a 2024 World Brewers Cup 6th place finalist, meticulously captures each coffee's natural character through artfully light roasting. Renowned for its exceptional portfolio, Mazelab offers an array of rare varietals, with a staggering 70% of customers seeking their exquisite Geisha options. This dedication to precision extends to their brewing, utilizing Jackie's proprietary "SOLO HIFLUX Filter 165" and "SOLO dripper," designed for consistent, high-quality extraction.

Beyond the brew bar, Mazelab's main café is a striking transformation from a former car repair shop into a minimalist, bright space featuring live trees in a Japanese garden style. This functional design, inspired by Japanese aesthetics, complements their impactful direct relationships with partners like Samaria Coffee Colombia and Onyx Coffee Guatemala, from whom they source unique offerings like coffee blossom honey. Mazelab also enriches the community with workshops on sensory skills and latte art, embodying a holistic approach to specialty coffee excellence.

name: Super Tramp Coffee
excerpt_short: Hidden Prague oasis: exclusive Casino Mocca coffee, secret garden, homemade pastries.
excerpt_long: Tucked in a Prague alley, this specialty coffee sanctuary offers a lantern-lit courtyard, exclusive Casino Mocca brews & homemade pastries.
summary: Tucked in a Prague alley, this secret garden oasis offers exceptional Casino Mocca coffee and artisanal Czech pastries. Experience vintage charm and meticulously curated brews in a unique, lantern-lit sanctuary.
flavour_profile: Secret garden, Mocca roasts, artisanal Czech pastries, V60 focus
about:
Prague's Hidden Oasis: A Specialty Coffee Sanctuary

Tucked away in a secluded Prague alley, this specialty coffee haven reveals a breathtaking, lantern-lit courtyard and patio, creating a secret garden oasis. Its unique blend of vintage and minimalist charm offers an escape from the city bustle. At its heart, the café exclusively brews beans from Casino Mocca, a renowned Hungarian roastery celebrated as pioneers in their country, whose quality rivals famous German or British roasters. This commitment to excellence ensures every cup is a testament to their dedication to the craft.

The coffee selection is meticulously curated, featuring an Ethiopian espresso known for its fruity, rich, and gently acidic profile, alongside a light-roasted El Salvador for V60, described as juicy and delicate. Filtered coffee primarily utilizes the V60 method, showcasing a refined approach to brewing. Complementing the exceptional coffee are artisanal, homemade Czech pastries, including a distinctive walnut-filled pastry likened to a "homemade poptart," with baristas well-versed in the chef's culinary expertise. A loyalty punch card program further fosters a vibrant community, inviting regulars to return to this enchanting spot.

name: Kavárna Pražírna
excerpt_short: Passionate roastery: diverse single-origins, intricate flavors, consistent quality.
excerpt_long: Discover a vibrant roastery's meticulously roasted, diverse single-origin coffees. Experience intricate flavors and a true journey of taste.
summary: Discover a vibrant roastery crafting exceptional single-origin coffees from exotic Nicaragua & Ethiopia. Meticulously roasted for intricate flavors, explore unique Robustas, decafs, and home brewing tools for your perfect cup.
flavour_profile: Exotic single-origins, intricate flavors, home brew focus.
about:
A Journey Through Flavor: Unveiling the Soul of Specialty Coffee

Discover a specialty coffee experience where every cup tells a story, reflecting a vibrant soul dedicated to exceptional taste. This roaster offers a rotating selection of single-origin coffees from diverse, often exotic regions like Nicaragua (Finca Santa Carmela) and Ethiopia (Chelbesa). Each boasts intricate flavor narratives, from "dried dates, molasses, papaya, mango, passion fruit" to "Peach, black tea, jasmine, lemonade, cane sugar." Their meticulous roasting ensures remarkable consistency in espresso, allowing even novice baristas to achieve a high-quality shot, embodying a deep commitment to freshness and third-wave coffee philosophy.

Beyond these captivating origins, their dedication to diversity shines. Experience an exceptional decaffeinated option, Colombia Atunkaa, challenging perceptions with its rich "chocolate, orange, cane sugar, pralines, cherries" profile. They also explore unique Robustas from Uganda and India, offering a comprehensive journey through varietals. For home brewing enthusiasts, a curated range of tools—grinders, Aeropress, French presses—is available, empowering you to recreate their exquisite coffee experience. This is more than coffee; it's an exploration of taste, quality, and passion.

name: Dos Mundos Coffee Roastery
excerpt_short: Where playful swings meet ethically sourced, expertly roasted coffee.
excerpt_long: Dos Mundos: Swing into a unique coffee journey. Discover meticulously roasted specialty beans, bridging two worlds from farm to your cup.
summary: Swing into Dos Mundos, where award-winning design meets ethically roasted specialty coffee, crafting a unique journey from green bean to perfect cup.
flavour_profile: Swings, award-winning design, ethical sourcing, expert roasts.
about:
Dos Mundos: Where Coffee Swings and Stories Brew

Step into Dos Mundos and discover a coffee experience unlike any other, where playful swings replace traditional chairs, inviting an interactive and memorable visit. This distinctive café interior, a masterpiece by architectural studio arch+, earned a nomination for "Interior of the Year 2016" and secured 2nd-3rd place in the Public Space category in 2017, a testament to its unique design.

Co-founder Lukáš Zugarovi's journey into specialty filter coffee began over a decade ago during a transformative trip to China, a refreshing departure from conventional espresso culture, shaping the very soul of Dos Mundos.The name "Dos Mundos," Spanish for "two worlds," perfectly encapsulates their deep understanding of coffee's dual states: green and roasted.

With their own roastery, equipped with Giesen W6 and W30 machines, they meticulously craft high-quality specialty coffee in-house. Beyond the brew, Dos Mundos champions human rights and fair dealings, ensuring equitable living standards for farmers globally. Their commitment extends to education, offering comprehensive cupping workshops and training baristas, while distinctive graffiti behind the bar visually narrates the bean's journey from origin to cup, completing this immersive coffee narrative.

name: The Miners Coffee & Roastery
excerpt_short: Prague's top roastery: ethical, innovative, productive, and perfectly brewed.
excerpt_long: Prague's globally acclaimed roastery: ethically sourced coffee, perfected for focus, pioneering sustainable innovation.
summary: Prague's globally recognized specialty coffee roastery. Experience ethically sourced, expertly roasted coffee in a minimalist haven designed for focus. Innovating with compostable capsules & integrated into workspaces.
flavour_profile: Focus-driven, sustainable capsules, direct trade, expert baristas, minimalist design, community events.
about:
Prague's Coffee Gem: Where Productivity Meets Perfection

Step into a world where exceptional coffee meets unparalleled focus. This specialty coffee haven, recognized globally as 40th among the world's top 100 coffee shops and a "Best Cafe in Prague for 2023," was founded on a unique vision: to cultivate an environment where productivity thrives alongside a sublime coffee experience. With its own roastery, they meticulously source specialty coffee directly from farmers, ensuring ethical practices and superior quality in every bean.

This dedication extends to their baristas, who undergo an intensive one-week training program and exam, guaranteeing consistent excellence in every cup served.Beyond the traditional café, this innovative brand pioneered compostable, Nespresso-compatible specialty coffee capsules in the Czech Republic, democratizing access to premium coffee at home.

Their distinctive minimalist interior design creates a serene backdrop, perfect for work or contemplation. Expanding their reach, they've integrated specialty coffee into coworking spaces and offices across Europe. They also foster community through events like "The Miners Run" and professional cupping sessions, inviting enthusiasts to deepen their appreciation for the craft.

name: EMA Espresso Bar
excerpt_short: EMA: Sweet, sustainable roasts from Alf & Bet, blending styles in a unique art space.
excerpt_long: EMA Espresso Bar: Crafting naturally sweet, balanced coffee with proprietary beans, sustainable practices, and unique art-filled spaces.
summary: EMA Espresso Bar, with "Alf & Bet" in-house, crafts specialty coffee prioritizing natural sweetness & balanced acidity. Sustainable, with a no-disposable-cup policy, it's a unique blend of flavor & conscious style.
flavour_profile: In-house roast & bake, sustainable, sweet profile, art, loyalty.
about:
EMA Espresso Bar: A Symphony of Sweetness and Sustainable Style

EMA Espresso Bar stands as a beacon of specialty coffee, distinguished by its unwavering commitment to quality, sustainability, and a unique roasting philosophy. At its heart lies "Alf & Bet," their in-house roastery and bakery, ensuring every cup and pastry served is crafted with proprietary beans and fresh ingredients daily. Their distinctive roasting approach, which prioritizes natural sweetness, balanced acidity, and minimal bitterness, positions their coffee profile as a delightful bridge between traditional American and lighter Nordic styles.

This dedication to flavor is supported by high-end equipment like the La Marzocco Strada EP 3-Group machine and a 3M Reverse Osmosis water filtration system, guaranteeing precision in every brew.Beyond the exceptional coffee, EMA cultivates a vibrant and conscious atmosphere. As of April 1st, 2024, they've embraced a strict no-disposable-cup policy, championing reusable alternatives and underscoring their commitment to environmental responsibility.

Patrons can explore a rotating selection of single-origin coffees and guest beans from esteemed micro-roasteries like Amsterdam's Dak Roasters, ensuring a continually evolving coffee journey. The Na Florenci location, with its blend of modern aesthetics and 1930s Czech functionalist architecture, complemented by art collaborations and a Karpuchina Gallery exhibition, offers a truly unique setting. A modern electronic loyalty program further enhances the customer experience, making rewards convenient and paperless.

name: La Bohème Café
excerpt_short: Czech pioneer, Dan Brown-featured, innovative roastery with direct farmer ties.
excerpt_long: Pioneering Czech roastery for 20 years, featured in Dan Brown's novel. Meticulously roasts daily, ensuring fresh, ethically sourced specialty coffee.
summary: Pioneering Czech specialty coffee for 20 years, this roastery, featured in Dan Brown's novel, meticulously roasts unique, ethically sourced beans daily for unparalleled freshness and innovative flavors.
flavour_profile: Pioneer, experimental processing, direct trade, Dan Brown.
about:
The Secret to Exceptional Coffee: A Czech Republic Pioneer

For nearly two decades, this establishment has pioneered the Czech Republic's specialty coffee scene, meticulously roasting and importing premium coffees. Their commitment to quality is rooted in direct farmer relationships, cultivated since 2008, ensuring unparalleled transparency and ethical sourcing. Daily roasting guarantees peak freshness, delivering an exquisite experience in every cup.

Their unique character is further cemented by a feature in Dan Brown's latest novel, "The Secret of All Secrets," a testament to their distinctive cultural impact. An insightful blog delves into advanced topics like the "Honey method" and Cold Brew, fostering a community of informed coffee enthusiasts.Their coffee portfolio showcases innovation, featuring unique and experimental processing methods such as the intriguing "Indonésie Kerinci Anaerobní." Beyond coffee, they are the exclusive distributor of premium Harney & Sons teas, reflecting a broader dedication to high-quality beverages.

A comprehensive wholesale program extends their specialty offerings to hotels, offices, cafes, and restaurants, spreading their passion far and wide. To complete the home brewing journey, they provide a wide array of coffee and tea accessories, including espresso machines, grinders, and barista tools, empowering enthusiasts to craft their perfect brew.

name: Stella Specialty Coffee Bar
excerpt_short: Stella: Brussels' dog-friendly specialty coffee, brewing hope & community.
excerpt_long: Stella Coffee Bar: Brussels' unique specialty coffee spot, blending a heartwarming story of resilience with a dog-friendly community.
summary: Stella Coffee Bar: A Brussels haven where specialty coffee meets heartwarming tales of resilience, community, and dog-friendly charm, fueled by a passion for quality and giving back.
flavour_profile: Stella's specialty coffee, dog-loving, community, unique drinks, animal aid.
about:
More Than a Café: A Story of Hope, Community, and Coffee

Stella Coffee Bar is a place where every cup tells a story of resilience and reunion. Named for the owner's beloved white shepherd, Stella, who is a regular at the café, its soul is deeply intertwined with the personal journey of founder Bassel Abou Fakher. After fleeing the war in Syria, Bassel was eventually reunited with his dog in Belgium, and that heartwarming spirit now defines this unique Brussels coffee spot.

The café radiates a warm, dog-friendly ambiance, most notably through a distinctive wall covered in Polaroid portraits of canine visitors. This isn't just a place for coffee; it's a community hub where customers are greeted by name and a powerful story of survival and love permeates the air.

The commitment to quality is as strong as its community ties. Stella exclusively serves specialty coffee sourced from dedicated plantations and roasted by Wide Awake Coffee Roasters, prepared with precision on a La Marzocco machine. Beyond classic espresso drinks, the menu features unique signature creations like the light, comforting Rose Water Latte and a fragrant Indian chai. This passion extends to a charitable mission, with a portion of earnings donated to animal shelters in Belgium, Colombia, and Lebanon. It’s a venture fueled by the artistic passion of its cellist owner, creating a truly special experience that will soon expand to include an artisanal bakery.

name: Kavárna Místo
excerpt_short: Místo: Prague's unique blend of Victorian charm, modern coffee artistry & rare beans.
excerpt_long: Experience Místo: Prague's gem for discerning coffee lovers, where vintage charm meets modern design and rare direct-trade beans shine.
summary: Místo: Prague's unique roastery. Victorian charm meets modern design. Indulge in rare, direct-trade specialty coffees, expertly roasted by Doubleshot on a vintage Probat. Discover unparalleled coffee excellence.
flavour_profile: Victorian charm, rare varietals, direct trade, craft roasts, charity.
about:
Místo: Prague's Gem for Discerning Coffee Lovers

Místo welcomes you into a unique space, blending Victorian intimacy with a bright, open, and modern aesthetic. Colorful hand-drawn wallpaper by Katerina Kynclova and simple Scandi-mod furniture create a delightful ambiance. At its heart, a high-end Kees van der Westen Spirit Triplette espresso machine signals serious coffee excellence. Filter enthusiasts can explore a rotating selection of manual brews like Clever, V60, and AeroPress, while connoisseurs can embark on a "Tasting Flight" to compare three distinct specialty coffees side-by-side, uncovering nuanced flavors.

This dedication to quality stems from Doubleshot, Místo's exclusive roaster, founded by enthusiasts who honed their craft on Central American farms and at 49th Parallel Coffee Roasters. Doubleshot's Direct Trade program ensures personally selected green beans from Latin America and Africa, roasted with tradition on a vintage 60-year-old Probat UG 22.

Their exceptional filter coffee has even graced the tables of Michelin-starred Degustation Bohême Bourgeoise. Expect rare varietals, such as honey-processed Geisha from Don Cayito, boasting notes of lemongrass, cherries, and tropical fruit. Beyond the cup, Místo champions community, donating all tap water proceeds to charity.

name: Kami
excerpt_short: Divine, terroir-driven specialty coffee, ethically sourced & locally roasted in Belgium.
excerpt_long: Kami: Divine, terroir-driven specialty coffee. Organic, direct-trade beans, expertly roasted in Belgium. Experience quality in every artisan cup.
summary: Kami: Where divine spirit meets terroir-driven specialty coffee. Meticulously sourced, locally roasted, and served with soulful artistry. Experience coffee's true essence.
flavour_profile: Kami spirit, terroir coffee, V60 mastery, artisan cups, local.
about:
Kami: Where Spirit Meets Specialty Coffee

Kami, named after the Japanese word for divinity, embodies a soulful approach to specialty coffee, reflecting a deep cultural inspiration and a profound commitment to quality. Co-founder Clara dedicated a year to intensive barista training at Mokxa in Lyon, mastering the art and science of coffee preparation. Together with Elias, they champion a terroir-driven philosophy, believing that soil and climate are as crucial for coffee quality as they are for fine wine.

This dedication is evident in their meticulously sourced organic, directly imported specialty coffee, which is then expertly roasted locally in Belgium, ensuring a transparent and hands-on journey from bean to cup.The Kami experience is elevated through thoughtful details, such as serving their exquisite Rwanda coffee, roasted in Antwerp, meticulously prepared using a V60 filter and presented in unique ceramic cups crafted by artist Kim Verbeke.

Beyond coffee, Kami extends its commitment to ethical sourcing and quality to its food menu, prioritizing local, organic, and seasonal produce. Guests can savor artisanal bread from Hopla Geiss and delightful pastries from Cookie Tree, alongside other fresh, seasonal offerings. While no specific awards are mentioned, the founders' passion and the exceptional quality of their offerings speak volumes, creating a truly divine and memorable coffee destination.

name: Belga & Co
excerpt_short: Antwerp-roasted, sustainable, global coffee journey, educating enthusiasts.
excerpt_long: Belga & Co: Consciously crafted, sustainably sourced specialty coffee, meticulously roasted in Antwerp. Experience a journey of flavor and purpose.
summary: Belga & Co: Antwerp-roasted, ethically sourced specialty coffee. We meticulously craft each bean, championing sustainability and inviting you on a conscious journey to "travel while drinking coffee" in our inspiring Brussels cafés.
flavour_profile: Freshly roasted, ethical, diverse, educational, inviting.
about:
Belga & Co: A Conscious Journey Through Exceptional Coffee

Belga & Co, co-founded by Charly Meerbergen, who brought a unique business acumen to the coffee world, embodies a philosophy where "Coffee" and "Company" intertwine, recognizing the vital role of every individual from farmer to consumer. This commitment to quality begins with their weekly roasting in Antwerp, where each bean's full flavor potential is meticulously developed, avoiding common pitfalls to highlight its inherent character.

Beyond the cup, Belga & Co actively champions over 50 sustainable projects through the Efico Foundation, demonstrating a profound dedication to social and environmental responsibility.Their mission extends to educating and inspiring customers, transforming them into coffee experts and inviting them to "travel while drinking coffee." In their inviting Brussels cafés, characterized by high ceilings, large windows, and often a terrace, guests discover a distinct third-wave experience.

The diverse menu features traditional espresso and filter options alongside Chemex and French press, innovative cold brew mocktails, and high-quality matcha lattes. Catering to all, they offer a notable selection of vegan pastries and various non-dairy milk options, ensuring a welcoming and inclusive atmosphere for every coffee enthusiast.

name: TULIPE - Specialty Coffee
excerpt_short: Tulipe: Woluwe's community-powered roastery, crafting quality, sustainable coffee.
excerpt_long: Tulipe: A community-built haven in Woluwe-Saint-Lambert, serving meticulously sourced, quality specialty coffee with a warm, welcoming spirit.
summary: Tulipe: Woluwe-Saint-Lambert's community-powered haven, where meticulously sourced specialty coffee from Replica meets a warm, welcoming atmosphere. Quality and connection in every cup.
flavour_profile: Modern, community hub, quality coffee, sustainable, local.
about:
Tulipe: Where Community and Quality Coffee Blossom

Born from a groundswell of local support, Tulipe Specialty Coffee is more than just a café; it's a neighborhood dream brought to life. With a massively successful crowdfunding campaign fueled by 306 backers, Tulipe was built on a foundation of community before its doors even opened.

This modern yet warm Woluwe-Saint-Lambert haven was envisioned as a welcoming space for residents, ICHEC students, and local professionals to unwind. Their partnership with the esteemed Replica roaster ensures every cup is a testament to their dedication to quality, serving meticulously sourced and roasted specialty coffee.

The soul of Tulipe lies in its commitment to both people and the planet. Beyond offering a cozy corner with books and magazines, they foster a unique community spirit, once even offering yoga classes as a crowdfunding reward. This dedication to well-being extends to their sustainable practices, notably their participation in the "Too Good To Go" initiative to combat food waste. With a stellar 4.6 Google rating, Tulipe has quickly become a cherished local institution, proving that great coffee is best enjoyed in a place that feels like home.

name: Fika
excerpt_short: Fika: Brussels' calm coffee sanctuary with dual-roaster beans & hidden garden.
excerpt_long: Escape to Fika, Brussels' unique coffee sanctuary. Savor dual-roaster single-origin coffee, homemade pastries, and a serene hidden garden.
summary: Fika: Brussels' serene coffee sanctuary. Savor unique dual-roaster single-origin Arabica from local & world-renowned roasters, embodying the Swedish 'fika' tradition in a hidden garden oasis.
flavour_profile: Fika, dual-roasters, homemade pastries, hidden garden
about:
Fika: Your Moment of Calm in the Heart of Brussels

Escape the city's hustle at Fika, a coffee sanctuary founded by Joana Soulard, a veteran of the Brussels specialty coffee scene. Inspired by the cherished Swedish tradition of "fika," this café is a tribute to slowing down and savoring the moment. The soul of Fika lies in its unique dual-roaster approach, offering meticulously prepared single-origin Arabica beans from both local favorite Café Capitale and the world-renowned April Coffee Roasters of Copenhagen. This curated selection ensures a truly exceptional and nuanced coffee experience for every palate, from the curious newcomer to the seasoned connoisseur.

The sensory journey continues with Joana's homemade pastries, including the unforgettable Kanelbullar (cinnamon buns), crafted with Belgian farm butter and milk. The space itself is a serene blend of Scandinavian minimalism and the classic charm of Brussels architecture, featuring beautiful "maisons de maître" floor tiles. Beyond the inviting interior lies a large, hidden garden terrace, offering a peaceful retreat overlooking the Church of St. Boniface. At Fika, every detail, from the high-quality local ingredients to the thoughtful design, invites you to pause and enjoy.

name: Buddy Buddy
excerpt_short: Brussels' first nut butter coffee bar, roasting specialty beans & crafting vegan spreads.
excerpt_long: World's first nut butter coffee bar & specialty roastery. Queer-owned, B Corp, 100% plant-based, crafting unique sips & spreads.
summary: Buddy Buddy: Brussels' pioneering nut butter coffee bar, crafting organic nut butters & roasting specialty coffee in Paris for a unique, 100% plant-based experience.
flavour_profile: Nut butter coffee, plant-based, queer-owned, B Corp, unique design.
about:
Buddy Buddy: A Nut Butter & Coffee Revolution

Welcome to the world's first nut butter coffee bar, a concept born from a simple craving. Life partners Julien and Matt started Buddy Buddy in their home kitchen, frustrated by the lack of quality vegan nut butters. This passion project blossomed into a Brussels-based atelier, where they craft organic nut butters in tiny batches from premium ingredients like Piedmont hazelnuts.

Their dedication to quality extends to their coffee; they roast their own specialty beans in Paris, sourced from trusted global partners. This hands-on approach ensures every sip and spoonful is packed with freshness and integrity, creating a truly unique café experience rooted in authentic craftsmanship.

The soul of Buddy Buddy extends beyond its innovative menu. As a certified B Corp and proud queer-owned business, they cultivate an inclusive, sustainable community hub. The café's vibrant, Wes Anderson-inspired interior, split between creamy white and peach red, sets a playful stage for their 100% plant-based offerings. Don't miss signature drinks like the "Le Buddy" peanut butter coffee or the Praline Mocha. To foster genuine connection, laptops are put away on weekends, encouraging conversation over coffee. It’s a space that’s as committed to its community and planet as it is to flavor.

name: Kaffabar
excerpt_short: Kaffabar: Passionately curated European specialty coffee & charming bulldog mascot.
excerpt_long: Kaffabar: A haven of curated European specialty coffees, diverse manual brews, creative cocktails, and a charming bulldog mascot.
summary: Kaffabar: Marc's passion brews exceptional European specialty coffees, from Chemex to creative cocktails. Meet Marcel, the charming bulldog mascot, in this acclaimed haven for coffee lovers.
flavour_profile: Curated European roasts, manual brewing, bulldog mascot, coffee cocktails.
about:
Kaffabar: Where Passion Meets Perfection in Every Cup

Step into Kaffabar, a haven born from owner Marc Daniels' leap from finance to a fervent pursuit of specialty coffee. This inviting space, with its modern aesthetic of black walls, marble tables, and warm wood, is more than just a café; it's an experience. Kaffabar meticulously curates its coffee selection from Europe's most esteemed roasters, including Mok, Lomi, and The Barn, ensuring a rotating menu of exceptional origins and blends. Whether you prefer the clean notes of a Chemex or the full-bodied sweetness of a V60, the knowledgeable staff are on hand to guide you through their diverse manual brewing methods.Beyond the exquisite coffee, Kaffabar delights with creative coffee cocktails like the popular Espresso Martini and Espresso Tonic. Even their tea service reflects a commitment to perfection, complete with a timer for optimal extraction. The café's unique soul is truly embodied by Marcel, Marc's beloved bulldog mascot, who often greets guests, adding a charming, personal touch that regulars adore. Complementing the beverages are delightful pastries like cakes, cookies, and muffins, alongside breakfast and lunch tartines. Kaffabar's dedication to quality has earned it recognition in publications like Bruzz and Gault Millau, solidifying its reputation as a must-visit destination for coffee aficionados.

name: My Little Cup
excerpt_short: Cozy haven for curated, ethical coffee, evolving roasters & seasonal delights.
excerpt_long: My Little Cup: a cozy haven for curated specialty coffees, ethical sourcing, and an ever-evolving adventure with unique guest roasters.
summary: My Little Cup: A cozy haven for meticulously curated specialty coffees. Discover unique, ethically sourced beans from Berlin, Brussels, and rotating guest roasters in a warm, community space.
flavour_profile: Curated roasts, global beans, cozy refuge, coffee education.
about:
My Little Cup: A Cozy Haven for Curated Coffee Experiences

Laurent Dano's journey into specialty coffee, fueled by a year of intense research and visits to small roasters, underpins My Little Cup's unique character. This dedication translates into a distinctive sourcing strategy, featuring precious beans from Berlin's Tres Cabezas and espresso roasts from Brussels' Wide Awake, known for direct farmer relationships and light profiles.

A rotating selection of "guest roasters" like Manhattan and Koppi ensures an ever-evolving coffee adventure. Behind the bar, educational charts and diverse equipment reflect a commitment to customer knowledge, further extended through the owner's engaging podcast.Nestled on a secluded street, My Little Cup offers a "super inviting and cozy" ambiance, a "warm refuge" bathed in natural light, complete with a vibrant orange terrace in summer.

Champions in hospitality, this charming space fosters a "coffee shop family" atmosphere, beautifully symbolized by a wall of staff polaroids. Beyond the inviting vibe, the café is deeply committed to ethical practices, ensuring transparency and fair prices for producers. Their innovation shines through seasonal beverage creations, like refreshing iced hibiscus and matcha tonics, making My Little Cup a truly special destination.
address: Rue de la Croix de Fer 53, 1000 Bruxelles

name: MOK Specialty Coffee Roastery & Bar
excerpt_short: Award-winning roastery, championing purity, traceability, and exceptional coffee.
excerpt_long: MOK Coffee: Award-winning roastery, led by a champion taster, dedicated to pure, traceable, and sustainably sourced specialty coffee.
summary: MOK Coffee: Europe's 2nd Best Roaster, led by a Belgian Cup Tasters Champion. We embody purity, integrity, and uncompromising quality through sustainable sourcing for an exceptional coffee journey.
flavour_profile: Expert roasts, direct trade, seasonal, award-winning, cafes.
about:
MOK Coffee: A Journey of Purity, Integrity, and Award-Winning Roasting

Founded in 2012 by Jens Crabbé, a two-time Belgian Cup Tasters Champion, MOK Coffee embodies a relentless pursuit of honest, remarkable, and uncompromising quality. This dedication has earned them the prestigious title of 2nd Best Specialty Coffee Roaster in Europe & Middle East. Roasting on a Probat UG22 with Cropster, MOK ensures precision and consistency, showcasing their commitment to the craft.

Their philosophy extends to strong, year-on-year partnerships with farmers, guaranteeing full traceability and a focus on highly seasonal sourcing from growers who prioritize quality and sustainable practices over price.Beyond their acclaimed roasting, MOK offers a holistic coffee experience across their two Belgian cafes in Brussels and Leuven. The Brussels location, nestled in the fashionable Dansaert district, features hand brews, filter, espresso, and fermented soft drinks, complemented by a seasonal vegetarian kitchen with a gluten/dairy-free focus.

In Leuven, guests can enjoy their exceptional coffee alongside locally baked cakes, pastries, and toasted sandwiches. MOK's influence reaches globally, supplying independent cafes, restaurants, and online customers with their passionately sourced and expertly roasted beans, including diverse origins like Ethiopia, Brazil, Colombia, and Tanzania.

name: Wide Awake Coffee
excerpt_short: Brussels roastery: precise, sustainable, transparently sourced, community-focused.
excerpt_long: Wide Awake Coffee: Brussels roastery with soul, precision, and a passion for exceptional, transparently sourced coffee and community.
summary: Wide Awake Coffee: Brussels' soul-driven roastery, meticulously crafting transparently sourced, exceptional beans with Loring precision. Passion, community, and sustainability in every cup.
flavour_profile: Ethical sourcing, precise roasting, unique Brussels locations, sustainability.
about:
Wide Awake Coffee: A Brussels Roastery with Soul and Precision

Wide Awake Coffee, founded in 2019 by Rutger & Senina, embodies a passion for exceptional coffee, music, and Brussels, aiming to make specialty coffee accessible and inclusive. They champion a transparent sourcing model, paying 2-7x the "fair-trade" FOB price to specialty importers and cooperatives, eschewing the term "Direct Trade" for its oversimplification.

Their commitment to quality is evident in their Loring roaster, ensuring clean and consistent roasts, complemented by a color sorter for defect removal and a rigorous, data-driven QC system that includes logging coffee color and cupping every batch.Their Brussels presence is marked by three distinct experiences: the flagship St Catherine café with extensive coffee and fresh pastries, the fast-paced Flagey espresso bar, and the intimate Dansaert slow brew bar, which also houses their roastery, QC lab, and training space—a hub designed by local craftspeople.

Wide Awake actively fosters community through monthly, mostly free cuppings and events, promoting appreciation for fair pricing. Their "Green Deal" reflects a strong sustainability ethos, offering discounts for reusable containers, delivering wholesale in reusable buckets, using 100% recyclable CO2 neutral packaging, and powering their Atelier with Belgian renewable electricity. Their current coffee portfolio includes blends like Duchess (Brazil & Colombia), Duke (Brazil), Moon Safari (Colombia), and single origins such as Anemone (Kenya Githaiti AA).

name: BOUCHE Specialty Coffee
excerpt_short: Curated grand cru coffee tastings, artistic ambiance, and vibrant community.
excerpt_long: Bouche: Brussels' curated coffee haven. Experience "grand cru" tastings, inspired by fine wine, fostering joyful connection and discovery.
summary: Bouche offers a curated coffee journey in Brussels, inspired by fine wine. Experience personal tastings of "grand cru" beans in a stunning, community-focused space, stripping away pretense for joyful discovery.
flavour_profile: Curated tastings, rare vintages, elegant design, community.
about:
Bouche: A Curated Encounter with Coffee

In the heart of Brussels, Bouche reimagines the café experience, drawing inspiration from the world of fine wine. Founder Antoine has cultivated a space centered on connection, highlighted by a dedicated counter for personal, barista-led tastings. Here, guests can explore "grand cru" coffees, some meticulously preserved from previous years, as Antoine shares the unique story behind each bean.

This isn’t about quiet reverence; it’s a joyful and accessible approach to specialty coffee, stripping away pretense to foster genuine discovery. The commitment to excellence is underscored by an elegant La Marzocco machine, ensuring every cup is a perfect expression of its origin.

The café’s soul is reflected in its stunning architecture by Ana Laborie, where high ceilings, curving walls, and a magnificent stone countertop create a sophisticated yet calming ambiance. Bouche is a hub for community, intentionally creating "no-laptop" areas to encourage conversation and regularly hosting pop-ups with local artists and florists. This artistic spirit infuses every detail, from the vividly designed cold brew cans to the fanciful Frizbee Ceramics cups that hold your filter coffee. It’s a multi-sensory experience designed not just for tasting, but for lingering, interacting, and connecting.

name: Sala Coffee
excerpt_short: Award-winning coffee, European charm. Hand-crafted brews, soulful ambiance. A true gem.
excerpt_long: Award-winning Sala Coffee: European-inspired, specialty brews. Hand-crafted with passion. A soulful space for coffee lovers.
summary: Award-winning specialty coffee in Christchurch. European-inspired, minimalist, and inviting. Hand-crafted brews, artisanal treats, and a community hub.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, award-winning barista, European charm, artisanal
about:
Sala Coffee: Christchurch’s Award-Winning Specialty Gem

Tucked away on Treffers Road in Wigram, Sala Coffee radiates the kind of European-inspired hospitality that feels both inviting and quietly refined. This specialty coffee destination is the proud stomping ground of Eduardo Santos, who was crowned Outstanding Barista at the 2024 Canterbury Hospitality Awards—a testament to the skill and passion that infuse every cup. Step inside and you’ll find a space with soulful ambiance, where attention to detail shapes the daily ritual of hand-crafting each brew. Regulars and newcomers alike are drawn in by Sala’s warm energy, minimalist aesthetic, and the steady hum of serious coffee craft.

Sala’s coffee portfolio is a thoughtfully curated affair, focusing on high-grade, specialty beans prepared with precision—whether you’re after a vibrant filter or a perfectly textured flat white. Their commitment to quality extends beyond the espresso machine, with a rotating selection of beans that spark curiosity and delight. Alongside coffee, Sala tempts with an artisanal menu of pastries and light bites, offering everything from buttery croissants to seasonal treats. For the true aficionado, asking for the barista’s recommendation or a single-origin pour-over is a must. Sala Coffee isn’t just a café; it’s a community hub where award-winning expertise meets genuine, understated charm.

name: Sign of the Kiwi Cafe
excerpt_short: Heritage cafe with breathtaking views. Specialty coffee, date scones, and soul. Christchurch's best.
excerpt_long: Heritage meets specialty coffee at Sign of the Kiwi. Breathtaking vistas, award-winning brews, and date scones await. Christchurch coffee with soul.
summary: Savor specialty coffee at Sign of the Kiwi Café. Historic charm meets breathtaking vistas. Enjoy award-winning brews, date scones, and unmatched views. Christchurch's coffee with soul.
flavour_profile: Heritage café, panoramic views, specialty coffee, local fare
about:
Sign of the Kiwi Café: Where Heritage Meets Specialty Coffee Amid Breathtaking Vistas

Perched atop Dyers Pass Road, the Sign of the Kiwi Café is a living slice of Christchurch history—a heritage space built in 1916–17 by conservationist Harry Ell, now reimagined as one of the city’s most beloved specialty cafés. Framed by panoramic views of the Canterbury Plains and the Port Hills, this warm, welcoming outpost consistently ranks among Christchurch’s top coffee destinations, earning both a Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice Award and the affection of local and visiting coffee lovers alike. Step inside to a vibe shaped by rustic charm, sunlit tables, and a friendly team passionate about hospitality.

The coffee program stands out for its excellent brews that pair beautifully with a menu crafted from fresh, local, and often organic ingredients. While the café keeps its precise bean portfolio under wraps, regulars praise the skilled baristas and reliably flavorful espresso. Signature bites like their famous date scones—reputed as the city’s best—freshly baked breads, gourmet toasted sandwiches, and a tempting selection of cabinet food anchor the menu. All-day breakfasts, vegan, and gluten-free options ensure everyone leaves satisfied. For the ultimate experience, grab a seat outdoors with your scone and flat white, soak in the heritage-rich atmosphere, and savor the unmatched views—this is Christchurch specialty coffee with soul.

name: Supreme Supreme
excerpt_short: Christchurch's specialty coffee gem. Ethically sourced, expertly roasted, and community-loved.
excerpt_long: Christchurch's vibrant coffee gem. Coffee Supreme's roastery offers ethically sourced, expertly roasted specialty coffee. Cozy diner vibes, community spirit.
summary: Christchurch's Supreme Supreme: a vibrant café with a soul as rich as its expertly roasted specialty coffee. Ethical sourcing, warm vibes, and delicious treats await.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, diner vibe, ethical sourcing, community hub
about:
Supreme Supreme: Christchurch’s Specialty Coffee Gem

Supreme Supreme isn’t just another café—it’s a vibrant Christchurch institution with a soul as rich as the coffee it pours. Created by the renowned Coffee Supreme team, this space on Welles Street seamlessly blends an inviting American diner vibe with the unmistakable aroma of expertly sourced and roasted specialty coffee. Visitors are met with warm hospitality and an atmosphere that buzzes with community spirit, making it a favourite for both locals and visitors seeking a top-tier brew or a cozy space to linger.

Coffee Supreme, the powerhouse behind Supreme Supreme, is a B Corp certified specialty coffee roaster celebrated for ethical sourcing from origins like Brazil, Colombia, Ethiopia, and more. Their reputation for excellence is clear in offerings that span the classics—flat whites, long blacks, cold drip, and single origin espresso—crafted by a well-trained crew. Supreme Supreme’s food menu is a standout, too, with fluffy pancakes, all-day breakfasts, hot sandwiches, and irresistibly fresh-baked pastries like cinnamon buns. For those keen on a house specialty, don’t miss their smooth cold drips or signature blends. This café is a testament to everything that makes New Zealand’s specialty coffee culture world-famous.
address: 10 Welles Street, Christchurch Central City, Christchurch 8011

name: Child Sister
excerpt_short: Eclectic coffee sanctuary. Soulful space. Exceptional coffee, creative brunch. A Christchurch favorite.
excerpt_long: Eclectic Christchurch coffee sanctuary. Soulful spot for specialty coffee & brunch. Ethically sourced beans, creative menu, and welcoming vibes.
summary: Eclectic Christchurch coffee sanctuary. Child Sister: soulful spot for specialty coffee & brunch. Ethically sourced beans, creative Korean-inspired menu, and a welcoming vibe.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, Korean fusion, cozy vibe, local favorite
about:
Child Sister: Christchurch’s Eclectic Coffee Sanctuary

Nestled on Manchester Street, Child Sister stands out as a soulful destination for specialty coffee lovers and brunch aficionados alike. Since opening in 2019, this café has quickly become a local favorite, thanks to its calming, stylish interior and eclectic, welcoming vibe—a true refuge amid the city’s energy. Opposite the lively Margaret Mahy playground, Child Sister draws a diverse crowd, from families and Central City workers to coffee connoisseurs in search of their next memorable cup. The café’s coffee program proudly features Lyttelton Coffee Company, one of Christchurch’s most respected roasters, renowned for their dedication to quality and vibrant, ethically sourced blends. Whether you prefer a classic flat white or want to explore single-origin flavors, the baristas here deliver with skill and consistency.

Beyond exceptional coffee, Child Sister is recognized for its imaginative menu, fusing fresh New Zealand ingredients with creative, Korean-inspired twists—think kimchi omelettes alongside locally baked pastries and gluten-free, plant-based options. This commitment to culinary creativity and hospitality has earned Child Sister a coveted Tripadvisor Travelers’ Choice Award, consistently ranking among the city’s top cafes and brunch spots. For first-time visitors, savor a pastry with your expertly crafted espresso, or linger over brunch to soak in the café’s character and charm. Child Sister is more than just a café; it’s a true community hub where every detail, from the coffee selection to the décor, reflects a passion for quality and originality.
address: 277 Manchester Street, Christchurch Central City, Christchurch 8011

name: Underground Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Bold, punchy flavors. Christchurch's rebellious coffee roasters. Bright, unforgettable cups.
excerpt_long: Christchurch's bold coffee rebels. Underground Coffee Roasters: hot air roasted, bright flavors. Beatnik blend: intense espresso. A cornerstone of Kiwi coffee culture.
summary: Christchurch's bold coffee rebels. Underground Coffee Roasters: hot air roasted, punchy flavors. Beatnik blend: intense espresso. A cornerstone of Kiwi coffee culture.
flavour_profile: Bold roasts, urban vibe, hot air method, punchy flavors
about:
The Bold Heartbeat of Christchurch: Underground Coffee Roasters

Deep in the heart of Christchurch’s urban pulse, Underground Coffee Roasters brings a rebellious spirit to New Zealand’s specialty coffee scene. For over two decades, this iconic roastery has championed hot air roasting—a method that elevates coffee’s clarity and accentuates bold, punchy flavors, delivering a cup that’s both bright and unforgettable. The café’s home base at 190 Durham Street exudes a creative, industrial vibe—think exposed brick, bustling espresso bar, and an undercurrent of Kiwi ingenuity—making it a cherished haunt for both local coffee aficionados and visiting explorers.

Underground’s portfolio is built on showstoppers: the “Beatnik” blend is their flagship—big, intense, and not for the faint-hearted, perfect for espresso lovers who crave depth and character. Their classic house blend balances sweetness with caramel smoothness, while the water-processed Brazil decaf packs a chewy molasses punch for those seeking full flavor with zero buzz. Across the menu, filter and espresso preparations are dialed in by skilled baristas, complemented by a curated selection of breakfast and lunch fare. Underground’s commitment goes beyond the cup—they offer barista training, industry expertise, and support for local cafés, defining themselves not just as a roaster, but as a cornerstone of Christchurch’s vibrant coffee culture.
address: 190 Durham Street South, Christchurch Central City, Christchurch 8011

name: Espresso Studio by Fushoken
excerpt_short: Bold espresso, bold spirit. Christchurch's Espresso Studio: Triple shot, triple soul.
excerpt_long: Bold spirit. Triple shot espresso. Famed for quality and innovation. Japanese pastries. A vibrant Christchurch coffee experience.
summary: Espresso Studio: Christchurch's bold coffee haven. Triple shot espresso, vibrant energy, and Japanese-inspired treats. Experience coffee with soul.
flavour_profile: Triple shot espresso, Japanese fusion, bold flavors, vibrant energy
about:
Espresso Studio by Fushoken: Triple Shot, Triple Soul in Christchurch

Step into Espresso Studio by Fushoken and you’ll instantly feel its bold spirit—this is no ordinary coffee stop. Nestled on Cashel Street in the heart of Christchurch, the cafe has earned a reputation among coffee aficionados for serving triple shot espresso as the default, setting a fearless tone for every cup. The space may be compact, but the energy is unmistakably big and vibrant, with the baristas' craft at the center of the experience. The aroma of specialty coffee beckons from the street, promising some of the city’s strongest—and most talked-about—espresso. While they haven’t snagged official awards just yet, the crew is famed locally for their unwavering quality and innovation, making this spot a must-visit for those who take their coffee seriously.

Espresso Studio’s portfolio showcases a deep respect for both coffee and Japanese culinary culture. Alongside expertly pulled shots and silky matcha lattes, the cafe offers the iconic Japanese GENJI PIE pastry, a delightfully flaky, sweet treat that pairs beautifully with their robust brews. The team once featured indulgent cheese biscuits and Nutella cookies, reflecting their commitment to unique flavors, but now sticks to their strengths in coffee and select pastries. Housed in the lively Riverside Market precinct, Espresso Studio thrives on connection, energy, and a devotion to elevating the everyday coffee ritual—perfect for those seeking a powerful start or a midday recharge.
address: 6/82 Cashel Street, Christchurch Central City, Christchurch 8011

name: Coffee Embassy Cellar Door
excerpt_short: Christchurch's hidden gem. Specialty coffee, roasted with passion. Industrial chic, community-focused.
excerpt_long: Christchurch's hidden gem. Since 2014, roasting & pouring specialty coffee in an industrial-chic space. Transparency, passion, and community.
summary: Christchurch's hidden gem. Coffee Embassy roasts exceptional specialty coffee in an industrial-chic space. Experience bold blends, single origins, and a community passionate about "better coffee."
flavour_profile: Specialty roasts, industrial-chic, curated experience, local gem
about:
Coffee Embassy Cellar Door: Christchurch’s Hidden Gem for Specialty Coffee

Nestled in a revamped mechanic’s workshop at the intersection of Lichfield Street and Fitzgerald Avenue, Coffee Embassy Cellar Door has been roasting and pouring some of Christchurch’s finest specialty coffee since 2014. With a mission to deliver “better coffee,” the roastery’s soul shines through its industrial-chic space, where transparency, passion, and community come together. Coffee Embassy partners closely with local businesses and is a proud home for coffee purists seeking a curated experience. The vibe is informal yet modern, making it perfect for both seasoned aficionados and the simply curious, all welcomed by genuinely friendly staff.

Coffee Embassy’s portfolio is both bold and diverse, featuring house blends like the rich Embassy Blend, the adventurous Ranger Blend, and the dynamic Beyond Blend, alongside rotating single origins sourced from globally renowned growing regions. Signature offerings include meticulously brewed filter coffees such as the sought-after Cesar Buesaquillo and Jose Edin Ramirez—each reflecting Embassy’s commitment to variety and traceability. For those looking to explore, their Single Origin Filter Subscription delivers a new taste journey every month. Light breakfast and brunch fare add to the experience, making Coffee Embassy a destination for both epicureans and everyday coffee lovers. Don’t miss their expert-recommended filter brews or take home a bag to savor Embassy’s character any time.
address: 107 Fitzgerald Avenue, Christchurch Central City, Christchurch 8011

name: Prima Roastery
excerpt_short: Christchurch's ethical coffee haven since '95. Fairtrade, organic beans, zero-waste. Taste Aotearoa's best!
excerpt_long: Prima Roastery: Christchurch's ethical coffee gem since 1995. Family-owned, Fairtrade, organic beans. Freshly roasted, zero-waste, and community-focused.
summary: Since 1995, Prima Roastery, a family-owned gem in Christchurch, roasts Fairtrade & organic beans. Pioneers in sustainability, offering exceptional coffee and a welcoming vibe.
flavour_profile: Ethical roasts, artisanal blends, eco-friendly, community hub
about:
Prima Roastery: Christchurch’s Sustainable Coffee Jewel

Prima Roastery, tucked away in Sydenham since 1995, is more than just a specialty coffee spot—it’s a cornerstone of Christchurch’s ethical coffee scene. Family-owned, Prima stands out for its dedication to both craft and community, sourcing Fairtrade and organic beans from the world’s renowned coffee regions. As pioneers, Prima was the first New Zealand roastery to adopt compostable packaging as early as 2011, earning the Ecoware Waste Warrior Award in 2018 for their zero-waste philosophy and ongoing commitment to sustainability. The roastery’s inviting vibe and approachable team make it the go-to destination for freshly roasted beans, expert advice, and an authentic taste of Aotearoa’s specialty coffee culture.

The coffee portfolio at Prima is celebrated for blends like the ultra-smooth “Dash” and the popular “TB3” blend, which locals and visitors alike rave about for their depth, balance, and versatility. Guests can enjoy single-origin pour-overs or espresso-based classics, all prepared with meticulous attention. Beyond coffee, Prima offers a curated selection of food and pastries, making it a perfect spot to linger. Whether you’re filling your own canister with fresh beans or seeking brewing gear and tips, Prima’s passionate crew ensures every visit is memorable. Pro tip: Don’t leave without trying their Fairtrade Organic blends—true standouts in both ethics and flavor.

name: Upshot Coffee/ Roastery
excerpt_short: Heathcote's heart: Upshot Coffee. Fairtrade soul, small-batch roasted, ethically sourced, exceptional coffee.
excerpt_long: Heathcote's heart: Upshot Coffee. Fairtrade soul, roasted in small batches since 2005. Ethical, exquisite coffee, crafted with passion.
summary: Upshot Coffee: Heathcote's heart. Since 2005, we roast 100% Fairtrade Organic beans in small batches. Experience exquisite flavors, ethical passion, and community.
flavour_profile: Ethical roasting, artisanal blends, rustic charm, community
about:
The Heart of Heathcote: Upshot Coffee’s Fairtrade Soul

Nestled in the rustic charm of Christchurch’s Heathcote Valley, Upshot Coffee exudes a unique character that blends genuine hospitality with a passion for ethical coffee. Operating from a cozy, modified shipping container at the historic Riding School site, Upshot is a haven for those who appreciate both the craft and the conscience behind each cup. Since 2005, this boutique roastery has specialized in 100% Fairtrade Organic coffees, sourced meticulously from seven countries. Every bean is roasted in small batches using traditional methods, unlocking the exquisite intensity and complexity that only high-altitude, shade-grown Arabicas can offer. The team’s two decades of roasting experience ensure that every batch sings with unique, carefully nurtured flavors.

Upshot’s coffee portfolio showcases distinctive single origins and thoughtfully crafted blends, all roasted in a traditional drum roaster to highlight each origin’s character. The coffee shop’s laid-back, welcoming vibe is complemented by a menu of locally inspired food and pastries—perfect accompaniments for your brew. With an ethos rooted in sustainability and Fair Trade principles, Upshot’s legacy is as much about nurturing community and grower relationships as it is about delivering exceptional coffee. Whether you’re sipping an espresso in the sunlit courtyard or grabbing freshly roasted beans to go, you’ll taste authenticity and ethical passion in every cup.
address: 131 Bridle Path Road, Heathcote Valley, Christchurch 8022

name: Addington Coffee Co-op
excerpt_short: Soulful coffee, roasted fresh. Community-focused, ethically sourced, and brimming with heart.
excerpt_long: Addington Coffee Co-op: Christchurch's heart. House-roasted Jail Breaker blend fuels community transformation. Ethical, delicious, and brimming with character.
summary: Addington Coffee Co-op: Christchurch's heart. House-roasted, fair trade coffee fuels community transformation. Soulful space, ethical business, and delicious fare.
flavour_profile: Ethical roasts, community focus, vibrant atmosphere, global impact
about:
Addington Coffee Co-op: Soulful Coffee and Social Good in Christchurch

Step through the doors of Addington Coffee Co-op and you instantly sense a space alive with purpose and warmth. Founded in 2008 by visionaries like Age Palmer, this beloved Christchurch institution is woven around community and ethical business—redistributing a remarkable 70% of its profits back to local and global causes. The café’s soul radiates both in its fair trade ethos and the welcoming hum of its sun-filled dining room, where regulars and newcomers gather over house-roasted coffee and wholesome fare. The Addington Coffee Co-op isn’t just about caffeine; it’s about community transformation, from supporting schools in Christchurch to building teacher housing in Ethiopia.

The heart of the café’s offerings is its Jail Breaker blend, a fair trade, organic coffee roasted fresh onsite, delivering balanced flavours with a conscience. Coffee aficionados will appreciate the thoughtfully sourced beans, with options tailored for espresso or filter methods. The menu boasts irresistible brunches and a wide selection of vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free fare, earning Addington accolades like Tripadvisor’s Traveler’s Choice award and a stellar reputation as Christchurch’s top coffee destination. Insider tip: don’t skip the house-made pastries, and take home a bag of Jail Breaker to relive the experience. Addington Coffee Co-op is more than a coffee spot—it’s a movement, brimming with character and heart.

name: Switch Espresso Roastery
excerpt_short: Ethically sourced, award-winning coffee. Christchurch's vibrant specialty coffee hub.
excerpt_long: Experience Christchurch's coffee heart. Switch Espresso: ethically sourced beans, award-winning roasts, and a family-like passion for the perfect cup.
summary: Experience Christchurch's heart of specialty coffee. Switch Espresso, founded by Hamish Evans, ethically sources and roasts award-winning beans with a Kiwi twist.
flavour_profile: Ethical sourcing, award-winning blends, craft training, vibrant hub
about:
Switch Espresso Roastery: Heart of Christchurch’s Specialty Coffee Scene

Step into Switch Espresso Roastery and you’ll feel the pulse of Christchurch’s specialty coffee culture. Founded by Hamish Evans—a long-standing figure in the city’s café scene—Switch Espresso began its journey in New Brighton, 2006, and now thrives in a boutique roastery on Birmingham Drive. What sets Switch apart is a fierce dedication to ethical sourcing, forging direct and transparent relationships with farmer collectives worldwide. The team—described as family—channels this ethos into every roast, delivering award-winning blends and single origins with a signature Kiwi twist. Their commitment is evident not just in their beans, but in the modern training space where rising baristas hone their craft under expert guidance.

Switch’s coffee portfolio is genuinely impressive: from rich, balanced blends like the celebrated Valvet, to vibrant single origins and decafs, all roasted fresh to order. Their dedication to sustainability and quality has earned them a loyal following and recognition as an award-winning roaster. Black Betty, their flagship café, is renowned locally for both its coffee and inviting food menu—making it a must-visit for anyone seeking a true Christchurch coffee experience. For the best taste of their craft, try a hand-poured filter, and don’t miss their fresh pastries or signature brunch plates. Switch Espresso is more than a roastery—it’s a vibrant hub where community, craft, and coffee converge.
hours_weekday: Mon - Thu: 8:00 AM - 3:00 PM, Fri: 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM

name: Vivace Espresso
excerpt_short: Christchurch's coffee heart. Artisan roasters since 1997. Quality, community, and unforgettable flavors.
excerpt_long: Christchurch's coffee heart since 1997. Vivace Espresso: artisan roasting, exceptional beans, and a legendary, welcoming vibe.
summary: Christchurch's coffee heart since 1997. Vivace Espresso: artisan roasting, exceptional beans, and a legendary, welcoming vibe. Experience coffee with soul.
flavour_profile: Artisan roasting, specialty blends, local icon, vibrant atmosphere
about:
Vivace Espresso: Christchurch’s Coffee Institution with Heart

Step into Vivace Espresso and you’ll feel the pulse of true Christchurch coffee culture. Founded in 1997 by Bernard Smith, a third-generation coffee pioneer whose family helped introduce specialty roasting to the city, Vivace stands as an enduring symbol of artisan craft and resilience. Bernard’s roots run deep—his grandparents and father were among Christchurch’s first coffee roasters, and Bernard himself has guided Vivace through everything from earthquakes to industry revolutions, always with an eye on quality and innovation. As a founding member of the NZ Specialty Coffee Association and voted New Zealand’s Best Coffee Roaster in 2014, Vivace’s reputation is built on unwavering dedication to the craft and community.

Vivace sources high-quality Arabica beans, roasting fresh daily to highlight nuanced flavors in single-origin offerings and expert blends. Their roasting approach—eschewing commodity coffee in favor of beans that shine with natural sweetness and complexity—shows in every cup. Standout blends and single origins are brewed with precision, and the food menu is legendary, especially the cheese scones: hot, fresh, and irresistible. Whether you’re after a classic flat white, a black coffee with real character, or a plate of pastries to start your day, Vivace delivers with soul, energy, and a welcoming atmosphere that locals and newcomers alike cherish.

name: Unknown Chapter Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Crafted coffee, soul-stirring space. Christchurch's specialty roastery, where every cup tells a story.
excerpt_long: Christchurch's heart beats here. Unknown Chapter: specialty coffee, soul, and craft. Roasted on-site, seasonal brews, and a vibrant café.
summary: Christchurch's heart beats here. Unknown Chapter: specialty coffee, roasted on-site. Vibrant flavors, ethical sourcing, and a soulful café experience.
flavour_profile: Craft roasting, seasonal beans, vibrant atmosphere, ethical

Unknown Chapter Coffee Roasters: A Chapter of Craft, Soul, and Specialty Coffee in Christchurch

Unknown Chapter Coffee Roasters pulses with the energy of Christchurch’s specialty coffee scene, blending meticulous sourcing with a warm, bustling café vibe. More than just a coffee shop, this modern roastery and café is set in the heart of the city, where beans are roasted on-site, filling the air with promise. Their ever-changing coffee menu reflects the seasons and harvests, offering a portfolio built on traceable, specialty-graded beans—think vibrant Ethiopian Yirgacheffe and smooth Guatemalan origins. Here, the focus is on crafting each cup to accentuate origin character, with passionate baristas employing world-leading brew methods and profile roasting for nuanced flavors. Their commitment to ethical sourcing means every batch supports small-scale farmers and upholds sustainability.

The soul of Unknown Chapter lies in its welcoming, bright space—a place where locals gather for brunch, chat over artisan brews, or retreat into a sunlit corner. Their food menu is as thoughtful as their coffee: house-baked pastries, slices, and brunch fare rooted in wholesome, free-range produce and family recipes, all evolving with the seasons. Accolades like TripAdvisor’s Travelers’ Choice 2024 and a ranking among Christchurch’s top cafés underscore their consistent excellence. For a true house specialty, don’t miss their homemade French toast or smoked salmon—favorites among regulars. Whether you’re a specialty coffee aficionado or simply seeking a vibrant, soulful spot, Unknown Chapter promises a memorable experience, cup after cup.
address: 254 Saint Asaph Street, Christchurch Central City, Christchurch 8011

name: Dear Deer Coffee Roasting Bar Newmarket
excerpt_short: Japanese heritage meets Kiwi craft. Custom roasts, rare beans, and a perfect cup.
excerpt_long: Dear Deer: Japanese coffee heritage meets Kiwi innovation. Custom roast your beans from 19 countries. Fresh, unique, and award-winning coffee awaits.
summary: Dear Deer: Where Japanese coffee heritage meets Kiwi craft. Custom roast your beans from 19 countries, watch it unfold. A unique, fresh coffee experience.
flavour_profile: Custom roasts, global beans, Japanese heritage, cozy ambiance
about:
Dear Deer Coffee Roasting Bar: Where Tradition Meets Your Perfect Roast

Step into Dear Deer Coffee Roasting Bar in Newmarket and you’ll feel the soul of a true specialty coffee experience. Helmed by Yukichi Shikano, who carries a storied coffee heritage dating back to his grand-uncle—the founder of world-renowned Japanese brand Kalita—Dear Deer seamlessly blends meticulous Japanese coffee culture with Kiwi ingenuity. The heartbeat of the space is its signature Jet Roaster, a small-batch machine allowing guests to choose from over 45 varieties of beans spanning 19 countries, then watch their custom roast unfold in real time. This interactive, hyper-fresh approach ensures each cup is uniquely tailored, from light to dark roast, capturing the rare character of each origin. The cozy, thoughtfully designed space by Mille Interiors makes it a community hub for both aficionados and newcomers.

Dear Deer’s rotating coffee portfolio includes fair trade and organic offerings like Honduras and Indonesia Mandheling, while their espresso, hand drip, and filter methods highlight the best global beans. Their award-winning approach, rooted in decades of tradition and innovation, has earned glowing praise for both quality and variety. Pair your coffee with a toasted signature sandwich from local favorite Cheese on Toast for the ultimate café experience. Whether you seek a bold espresso or want to experiment with a bespoke blend, Dear Deer promises a coffee journey as vibrant and personal as its history.
hours_weekday: Mon - Wed, Fri: 7:30 AM - 3:00 PM, Thu: 7:30 AM - 2:45 PM
