about:
C4 Coffee Co.: Christchurch’s Coffee Mecca

Step into C4 Coffee Co. at 113 Fitzgerald Ave and experience a cornerstone of Christchurch’s specialty coffee scene—a vibrant hub where roastery, café, and retail merge in perfect harmony. C4 is renowned for its dedication to the craft, serving as both a local specialist coffee roaster and an acclaimed brew bar. Their triple-shot venue exudes a dynamic, industrial vibe, drawing in everyone from casual sippers to serious coffee aficionados. C4’s passion is evident in every detail, from the rich aroma of freshly roasted beans to their curated selection of top-notch espresso machines and brew gear. Accolades such as the Tripadvisor Travelers’ Choice award and rave reviews attest to their consistent quality and their reputation for serving “the best coffee in Christchurch.”

The coffee portfolio here is as diverse as it is impressive: signature house blends like the robust Krank and the smooth Stout offer something for every palate, alongside micro-lot single origins and standout specialty picks like the Colombia El Oasis Lactic Natural. With a strong focus on ethical sourcing, including Fair Trade Organic options, and an evolving menu of breakfast, brunch, and decadent pastries, C4 truly elevates the café experience. Whether you’re in for a quick takeaway, a deep-dive into filter methods, or a relaxed brunch with friends, C4’s knowledgeable team ensures every visit is memorable. Don’t miss their brew bar specialties or their expertly crafted tasting packs—perfect for anyone eager to explore the world of specialty coffee at its finest.

name: Open Coffee
excerpt_short: K'Road's heart. Open Coffee: Specialty brews, warm vibes, and community soul. Your daily dose of belonging.

about:
Open Coffee: K’Road’s Eclectic Heartbeat for Specialty Coffee Aficionados

Nestled on the vibrant strip of Karangahape Road, Open Coffee is much more than just a café—it’s a sanctuary for Auckland’s creatives, dreamers, and devoted coffee lovers. Led by owner Christy Tennent, this snug, brick-clad spot radiates warmth and inclusivity, with reclaimed wood tables perfect for people-watching or intimate conversations. Open Coffee has become a beloved community hub, hosting regular market nights, pop-ups, and fostering connections that stretch beyond a morning caffeine fix. Its unique soul lies in the welcoming yarns exchanged over beautifully crafted cups, capturing the spirit of K’Road’s diverse and ever-evolving crowd.

The baristas at Open Coffee are known for their expertise and passion, consistently delivering exceptional espresso, velvety flat whites, and a regularly rotating portfolio that showcases premium blends and single origin selections, sourced with care. The partnership with Auckland’s well-respected Eighthirty Coffee Roasters ensures each cup sings with clarity and character. Pair your brew with their renowned toasties—oozing with melted cheese—and a selection of flaky pastries for a simple, satisfying treat. While there’s no cooked breakfast, Open’s honest menu and soulful atmosphere keep locals coming back. For the full experience, snag a spot by the window and watch K’Road’s world go by—Open Coffee is about savoring more than just your drink; it’s about belonging.

Coffee General: Birkenhead’s Artisan Coffee Haven

In the heart of Birkenhead, Coffee General radiates the soulful spirit of true specialty coffee, blending a welcoming neighborhood vibe with expert craft. As home of ALLIES Artisan Coffee Roasters, Coffee General is anchored by their ethos of fairness, freshness, and quality. They champion direct support of coffee farmers and local community initiatives, serving up meticulously roasted beans right on-site. Step inside and you’ll be greeted by the rich aroma of single origins, signature blends, and a daily rotating batch brew, all complemented by the hiss and hum of skilled baristas working the espresso machine.

Coffee General’s shelves feature an ever-evolving portfolio—espresso, filter, and retail beans—curated by a crew passionate about every step from farm to cup. Food at Coffee General is equally thoughtful: fresh pastries, breakfast sandwiches, and salads crafted from local ingredients round out the menu. The casual, relaxed setting makes it a prime spot to catch up with friends or enjoy solo moments. For curious newcomers, don’t miss their house specialty batch brew, often showcasing limited-run, award-worthy coffees. Coffee General is more than a café—it’s a hub united by coffee, community, and a genuine love for the craft.

name: Meebz Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Auckland's heart: Meebz Coffee. Passionate roasters, exceptional flavors, ethically sourced.
excerpt_long: Auckland's Meebz: Passion meets precision. Mike Tan's roastery offers 20+ coffees, from bold blends to rare microlots. Fresh, fair, and flavorful.

flavour_profile: Artisanal roasts, rare origins, cozy vibe, flavor adventure
about:
Meebz Coffee Roasters: Where Passion Meets Precision in Auckland’s Heart

Stepping into Meebz Coffee Roasters in Milford is like entering a sanctuary dedicated to the art of specialty coffee. Founded by Mike Tan, a celebrated artisan roaster and silver medalist at the 2023 New Zealand Barista Championships, Meebz exudes a rare blend of warmth, expertise, and relentless pursuit of flavor. The vibe is welcoming and unpretentious, with the small, passionate team eager to help you discover your new favorite. The café’s soul is evident in every detail—from the friendly banter at the espresso bar to the sight and aroma of freshly roasted beans, precision-crafted in small batches for unbeatable freshness.

Meebz offers a dizzying portfolio: over 20 meticulously sourced coffees from small farms and microlots across South America, Sumatra, Central America, Ethiopia, and beyond. Their flagship Meebz Blend impresses with bold, creamy texture and rich chocolate notes—a customer favorite, especially for espresso. Seasonal rarities and award-winning microlots appear on the exclusive Superlative menu, catering to the true aficionado. Ethical sourcing, organic and decaf options, along with an expertly curated selection of single origins, ensure every cup is an adventure. Pair your brew with a freshly baked pastry and let the spirit of specialty coffee—fresh, fair, and flavor-focused—work its magic at Meebz.

name: Daily Daily Coffeemakers
excerpt_short: K Road's coffee haven. Single-origin, soulful brews. Expert baristas, welcoming vibes.
excerpt_long: K Road's coffee haven. Daily Daily: single-origin gems, expertly brewed. Welcoming vibes, passionate baristas, and the best specialty coffee.
summary: Auckland's Daily Daily: Specialty coffee with soul. Single-origin beans, expertly brewed. Welcoming vibes, perfect pastries. Your daily dose of coffee bliss.

about:
Daily Daily Coffeemakers: Where Specialty Meets Soul on K Road

Daily Daily Coffeemakers has carved out a cult following on Auckland’s Karangahape Road, standing as a beacon for true coffee devotees. This multi-roaster shop is celebrated for its rotating selection of single origin specialty coffees, showcasing diverse beans sourced from renowned roasters around the world. Each cup—whether meticulously brewed as espresso, a hand-poured filter, or their signature daily drip—offers a new journey in flavor, expertly guided by baristas who clearly love both their craft and their customers. The ambiance is equal parts welcoming and laid-back, with natural light, curated playlists, and communal tables where regulars and newcomers blend seamlessly. The team, notably Albert and Josh, are often praised for their hospitality and encyclopedic coffee knowledge, making every visit memorable and inspiring.

While Daily Daily is first and foremost about the coffee, it’s also adored for its lineup of pastries and small bites, ideal for settling in or grabbing on the go. Their approach—thoughtful, unpretentious, always quality-focused—has earned them consistent five-star reviews and recognition as “the best specialty coffee in Aotearoa” by loyal locals. Little details matter here: the perfect oat cappuccino, recommendations for unique origins rarely seen elsewhere, and a calm escape on busy days. Whether you’re after a world-class filter, an expertly extracted espresso, or simply a place with heart, Daily Daily Coffeemakers is Auckland’s essential specialty coffee stop.

name: Coffee Pen
excerpt_short: Eden Terrace's hidden gem. Specialty coffee, artisanal precision, and a warm soul.
excerpt_long: Eden Terrace's hidden gem. Coffee Pen: specialty coffee, artisanal precision, and a warm soul. Discover the finest 3% of beans.

flavour_profile: Artisanal coffee, cozy gem, specialty beans, cult pastries
about:
Coffee Pen: Eden Terrace’s Hidden Gem for the True Coffee Devotee

Tucked away on Basque Road, Coffee Pen is an Eden Terrace mainstay celebrated for its warm, inviting soul and an uncompromising passion for specialty coffee. As a proud collaboration with ARK Roasting Company, Coffee Pen serves some of the world’s finest 3% of coffee beans, distinguishing itself with artisanal precision and a deep respect for the craft. The baristas here are true coffee artisans, consistently praised for their knowledge and attention to detail—whether you’re in the mood for a velvety flat white, a perfectly spiced chai latte, or a pour-over that lets origin flavours shine. The rotating menu keeps things fresh, ensuring every visit offers something new to discover, from rich espresso-based drinks to thoughtfully curated teas.

Coffee Pen’s character is amplified by a dynamic food program: think gooey brownies, homemade cakes, and a range of pastries that have earned cult status among locals—the wagyu beef & mozzarella pie is a standout. Their cakes, from matcha to apple salted caramel, are available for special order and reflect the kitchen’s creativity. With a cozy atmosphere, approachable staff, and a reputation as a “hidden gem,” Coffee Pen is a cherished fixture in Auckland’s specialty coffee scene—a must-visit for both connoisseurs and comfort-seekers alike.

name: Remedy Coffee
excerpt_short: Auckland's quirky living room. Ethically sourced coffee, community, and good vibes.

Remedy Coffee: Auckland’s Living Room for Coffee Lovers

Remedy Coffee sits at the crossroads of community and caffeine, a welcoming refuge in the heart of Auckland’s CBD that pulses with vibrant character and an unmistakably laid-back soul. This café, beloved by regulars and newcomers alike, is more than just a spot for top-notch coffee—think of it as Auckland’s own quirky living room. Lined with bookshelves, mismatched picture frames, and buzzing with the energy of people playing board games or swapping novels, Remedy radiates an unpretentious, eclectic vibe that’s as warm as their expertly crafted flat whites. The café is an accredited Conscious Consumers destination, reflecting a commitment to ethical sourcing and environmental responsibility.

Remedy’s coffee portfolio showcases carefully selected Red Rabbit beans, with a rotating single origin espresso on offer every three months, and an impressive range of filter options featuring guest roasters like Flight Coffee and Point of Yield. Iced coffee, filter, and cold brew sit comfortably alongside non-dairy and decaf options, ensuring there’s a cup for every preference. The food menu leans into comfort, featuring pastries and sourdough from artisanal Auckland bakeries, classic bagels, and inventive vegan choices. Remedy is more than a café—it’s a vibrant hub where locals unwind, visitors linger, and the coffee always comes first.

excerpt_short: Britomart's coffee haven. Small-batch roasted, world-class beans. Experience the soul of coffee.

Espresso Workshop Britomart: Where Coffee Craft Meets Urban Community

Nestled in the heart of Auckland’s Britomart, Espresso Workshop is a sanctuary for specialty coffee lovers who value both expertise and soul. Since opening its doors in 2007, this independently owned roastery has built a sterling reputation by obsessively sourcing world-class green beans and roasting them in small batches to unlock distinctive, traceable flavors. Their Britomart espresso bar offers a dynamic yet inviting space where the vibe hums with the passion of skilled baristas and a community that shares a love for exemplary coffee. Recognized for quality with accolades like TripAdvisor’s Travelers’ Choice, Espresso Workshop embodies the city’s specialty coffee movement—where every cup is an experience, not just a product.

Their coffee portfolio is a compelling showcase: core blends like Mr White, Mr Black, Mrs Green (organic), and Mōrena reflect their deep roasting know-how, while frequent single origin selections and the award-winning Colombia Santa Maria highlight their commitment to diversity and transparency. Guests can expect consistently excellent espresso, hand-brewed filter options, and a cabinet of fresh, high-quality pastries and savory bites. Whether you’re a seasoned taster or a curious newcomer, the team’s dedication to both craft and customer experience makes every visit memorable—tip: ask about their current single origin features or attend one of their lively masterclasses to deepen your appreciation for the specialty coffee world.

name: Red Rabbit Coffee Parnell

Red Rabbit Coffee Parnell: A Beacon for True Coffee Lovers

Step into Red Rabbit Coffee Parnell and you’ll instantly feel the energy of a space built by coffee obsessives for coffee lovers. Nestled at the base of the XERO building on Saint Georges Bay Road, this light-filled café radiates good vibes and offers an open, welcoming vibe. Founder and head roaster Steve Barrett, a fixture in New Zealand’s specialty coffee scene, pours his award-winning passion into every cup. Red Rabbit’s impressive resumé includes Silver and Bronze wins at the Australian International Coffee Awards and roasting for the NZ AeroPress Champion. This isn’t just a coffee stop—it’s a destination where single origin, seasonal beans are meticulously roasted and brewed, honoring small farms and global origins.

Red Rabbit’s menu is anchored by clean, sweet, and vibrant single origin options—whether you crave a fruity Ethiopian V60 pour-over or a perfectly balanced espresso. Their fresh cabinet selection brims with house-baked pastries and creative fare, while the kitchen serves up hot plates to match any mood. Baristas are always ready to recommend a filter or espresso experience tailored to your palate, all with a no-snobbery approach that invites you to learn and savor. For coffee adventurers, don’t skip the award-winning blends or filter specials—here, every cup is an education in excellence, hospitality, and fun.

name: Ozone Coffee

about:
Ozone Coffee Roasters Auckland: The Beating Heart of Specialty Coffee

Step into Ozone Coffee Roasters in Grey Lynn and you immediately feel the soulful buzz of a space crafted by passionate coffee pioneers. Founded in 1998 by three dedicated coffee lovers in New Plymouth, Ozone has become an anchor of New Zealand’s specialty coffee scene, with a vibrant Auckland eatery that’s as committed to sustainability as to exceptional flavour. Proudly B Corp certified, Ozone champions direct relationships with world-class producers and places social and environmental responsibility at the core of its ethos. Their roastery bustles daily, bringing out the unique character in every bean and serving a rotating menu of award-winning single origins from Costa Rica, Ethiopia, Peru, Brazil, and more. Whether you’re chasing a punchy house blend or a rare single estate, each cup reflects their mastery in sourcing and roasting.

The vibe is unmistakably welcoming—think bustling communal tables, low-waste dining, and a menu celebrating local suppliers and inventive dishes. Recognised as one of Auckland’s top cafés, Ozone’s eatery offers options for every palate, including standout vegetarian and vegan fare, all beautifully plated. Pair your brew with a signature pastry or brunch classic, and don’t miss the “In My Mug” subscription if you want to take the Ozone experience home. From sustainable sourcing to their inclusive, dog-friendly space, Ozone strikes a unique balance between world-class coffee expertise and genuine community connection—a must-visit for any coffee lover in Auckland.

name: Chuffed

Chuffed: Auckland CBD’s Hidden Coffee Oasis

Nestled down a vibrant corridor on High Street, Chuffed is a sanctuary for specialty coffee lovers seeking both quality and atmosphere. Recognized repeatedly as one of Auckland’s top cafes since 2014, Chuffed has earned a reputation for its passionate team, warm service, and consistent excellence—backed by the Travelers’ Choice award placing it among the city’s best. The stylish white-washed space opens up to an inviting oasis courtyard, perfect for those who appreciate a tranquil retreat amid the CBD buzz. Whether you’re after a lively breakfast, a relaxing brunch with friends, or a quiet solo coffee, Chuffed’s unique blend of character—marked by bright blue walls, a disco-lit entry corridor, and a welcoming vibe—sets it apart.

Chuffed’s coffee program is a true highlight, offering Rocket House espresso beans alongside a curated range of brew methods, including batch brew, cold-drip, and the essential flat whites. The menu celebrates fresh, seasonal New Zealand produce with inventive dishes and standouts like house-made crumpets (served sweet or savoury), generous ‘Big Chuffed’ breakfasts, and superb cabinet sandwiches. Vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free options abound. Don’t miss their specialty toasts or a pastry paired with your coffee. For those with a penchant for sustainability, free coffee grinds are on offer for your compost. Chuffed’s combination of specialty brews, laid-back yet refined character, and all-day excellence cements its place as a must-visit in Auckland’s specialty coffee scene.

name: Eighthirty Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Auckland's soulful specialty coffee hub. Authentic spirit, quality brews, and community vibes since 2009.
excerpt_long: Auckland's soulful specialty coffee hub. Since 2009, Eighthirty crafts exceptional blends, from classic to unique, in a vibrant, sustainable space.
summary: Auckland's soulful specialty coffee hub since 2009. Eighthirty crafts exceptional blends, from classic to unique, with a commitment to quality and community.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasts, vibrant blends, community hub, ethical sourcing
about:
Eighthirty Coffee Roasters: Auckland’s Soulful Specialty Hub

Step into Eighthirty Coffee Roasters and you’ll feel the pulse of Auckland’s specialty coffee scene. Since 2009, this standout roastery—founded by Glenn Bell, a pioneer who brought his dream north from Wellington—has evolved from a modest K Road spot into a community hub known for its authentic spirit and commitment to quality. The space buzzes with creativity, powered by top-tier gear like the Loring S35 Kestrel roaster and Modbar pour-over system, overseen by Head of Coffee Jessica MacDonald, formerly of Square Mile, London. Here, Eighthirty crafts blends to suit every palate, from their classic dark chocolate-laced standard blend to a fragrant orange blossom and vanilla brew that has become a local favorite.

Eighthirty’s coffee portfolio is a journey: you’ll find rotating single origins sourced in partnership with producers like Colombia’s Cofinet, plus staple blends (Standard, Classic, Organic) perfect for espresso, pour-over, or V60. The café culture extends beyond exceptional brews—expect vibrant, wholesome food options, from fresh salads and artisan sandwiches to muesli and irresistible slices of The Caker’s cake. Sustainability is central to their story, seen in their ethical sourcing and eco-friendly packaging. Whether you’re a seasoned aficionado or a casual sipper, Eighthirty’s warm, knowledgeable staff and ever-evolving coffee selection guarantee a rich experience every visit.

name: Kōkako Organic Coffee Roastery
excerpt_short: Ethical Auckland roasters. Organic, Fairtrade, and community-focused. Freshly roasted, peak flavor.
excerpt_long: Auckland's first organic coffee roastery. Since 2001, ethically sourcing & roasting specialty coffee. Committed to Fairtrade, sustainability, and community.
summary: Auckland's ethical coffee pioneer. Since 2001, Kōkako roasts organic, Fairtrade beans. Experience fresh, sustainable brews and support community & conservation.
flavour_profile: Organic, ethical, innovative, specialty coffee, sustainability
about:
Kōkako Organic Coffee Roasters: Auckland’s Ethical Coffee Pioneer

Step inside Kōkako Organic Coffee Roasters and you’ll immediately sense a blend of heritage, heart, and innovation that sets this Auckland staple apart. Founded in 2001, Kōkako was the city’s first organic coffee roastery—sparked by Helen and Christian’s vision and later championed by Mike Murphy, who secured Biogro organic certification in 2009. Kōkako’s soul runs deep: every cup represents a commitment to Fairtrade, organic, and climate-neutral values, with their roots entwined in environmental stewardship and community, including ongoing support for the Rotoehu Ecological Trust and local conservation efforts.

The flagship space at Commercial Bay is more than a café—it’s a showcase of specialty coffee culture. Alongside house staples like the Mahana and Aotea blends, Kōkako delights aficionados with evolving single origins and ethically sourced decaf, all roasted fresh for peak flavour. Whether you’re sipping a meticulously brewed Chemex, V60, or indulging in their acclaimed organic drinking chocolate, every brew is elevated by a focus on sustainability and craft. Don’t miss the inviting food menu, featuring premium pastries to complement your cup. For home baristas, the knowledgeable team offers advice on grind and brew methods—making every visit both a treat and a learning experience.

name: Atomic Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Auckland's iconic coffee. Three decades of passion, Kingsland edge, rich flavors, and ethical sourcing.
excerpt_long: Auckland's iconic coffee roasters. Three decades of passion, Kingsland edge. Rich flavors, ethical sourcing, and a vibrant space.
summary: Kingsland's iconic coffee haven. Three decades of passion, small-batch roasting, and a vibrant, unpretentious vibe. Rich flavors, ethical sourcing, and community.
flavour_profile: Craft roasts, bold flavors, urban vibe, ethical sourcing
about:
Atomic Coffee Roasters: Auckland’s Iconic Blend of Craft and Culture

Atomic Coffee Roasters is a cornerstone of Auckland’s specialty coffee scene, alive with three decades of passion and an unmistakable Kingsland edge. Founded in 1992 on Ponsonby Road, this pioneering roaster quickly outgrew its bohemian beginnings, moving to Kingsland in 1995 and embedding itself in the creative fabric of the city. Today, Atomic’s flagship roastery and trio of espresso bars deliver a welcoming, unpretentious vibe—where tattoos are as common as latte art and quality is never compromised. Their reputation is built on rich, robust flavors, exacting small-batch roasting, and an unwavering commitment to Fairtrade, ethical sourcing, and sustainability. Recognition like the Spatial Colour Award at the Best Design Awards speaks to their vibrant space and innovative spirit.

Atomic's coffee portfolio is a celebration of origin and craft: their Golden Bean award-winning blend, honored for its smooth dark roast and intricate notes of dark chocolate, stone fruit, honey, and savory caramel, stands out for milk-based drinks. Signature blends like ‘Veloce’ and rotating single origins from coffee-rich regions—Ethiopia, India, Guatemala—showcase the roaster’s dedication to clarity and depth in every cup. Expect skillfully pulled espresso, seasonal filter roasts, and a considered menu of fresh pastries and café fare. Housed in a space as lively and characterful as the coffees themselves, Atomic is where Auckland’s coffee aficionados and casual sippers alike find their fix and their community.

name: Allpress Roastery Auckland
excerpt_short: Auckland's coffee heart. Allpress: where craft, heritage, and perfect brews meet.
excerpt_long: Auckland's coffee heart. Allpress Roastery: where Kiwi coffee craft began. Experience decades of passion, innovation, and the perfect cup.
summary: Experience the heart of NZ coffee at Allpress. Founded in 1989, this Auckland roastery blends heritage, craft, and innovation. Taste the perfect cup.
flavour_profile: Artisan roasting, craft coffee, industrial-chic, NZ heritage
about:
Allpress Auckland Roastery: Where Kiwi Coffee Craft Began

Step into the Allpress Roastery in Freemans Bay, Auckland, and you feel the pulse of New Zealand’s specialty coffee scene. Founded by industry pioneer Michael Allpress—whose journey began in 1989 with a humble coffee cart at Victoria Park—this roastery is a living tribute to decades of innovation and coffee passion. Michael and co-founder Tony Papas were early adopters, moving from simply serving espresso to roasting their own beans when existing options didn’t meet their exacting standards. Today, you’re welcomed into Browns Mill, an architecturally striking space where minimalist timber and natural light set the stage for a sensory coffee experience. The beating heart of Allpress, this café isn’t just a place for a caffeine fix—it’s a landmark for coffee lovers seeking transparency, craft, and heritage.

Allpress’s signature Espresso Blend is always on the machine, while the baristas showcase their ever-evolving Coffee Galaxy single origins and limited-run selections—ready for tasting or to take home. Their dedication to the perfect cup is matched by a commitment to innovation: Allpress pioneered the use of custom-built hot-air roasting technology, ensuring every roast is clean, sweet, and consistent. While the coffee takes center stage, the kitchen delivers artisan sandwiches, light lunches, and a tempting lineup of fresh pastries daily. Whether you’re a local aficionado or a visitor tracing the roots of New Zealand’s coffee revolution, the Allpress Auckland Roastery is a must-visit destination where heritage and hospitality brew together.

name: Roastville Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Marrickville's heart of specialty coffee. Freshly roasted, sourced with care. Taste the story.
excerpt_long: Experience coffee artistry at Roastville. Sourcing the finest beans, roasting to order. A Marrickville hub for quality, character, and community.
summary: Roastville: Marrickville's heart of specialty coffee. Sourcing the finest beans, roasting fresh. A community hub for coffee lovers, offering unique flavors and barista workshops.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasts, vibrant café, coffee education, local gem
about:
Roastville Coffee Roasters: The Soul of Marrickville's Specialty Coffee

Nestled in the heart of Marrickville, Roastville Coffee Roasters stands out as a destination for coffee lovers who crave both quality and character. Their approach is built on a simple, unwavering mission: source the world’s finest beans and do them justice. With direct relationships with growers from the mountains of South America to the plateaus of Ethiopia, every cup here tells a story of origin, care, and artisanal expertise. The Roastville team, backed by decades of coffee know-how, crafts a portfolio spanning single-origin espressos, signature blends, and filter coffees, all freshly roasted to order. Whether you’re a filter devotee or a flat white traditionalist, Roastville’s bespoke offerings cater to every palate, promising a new depth of flavor in each experience.

Roastville isn’t just about coffee—it’s about cultivating a vibrant community hub where passion and education go hand in hand. They offer popular barista workshops, inviting enthusiasts to elevate their brewing skills and deepen their appreciation of specialty coffee. Their café setting buzzes with creative energy, complemented by an enticing menu of fresh food and pastries that perfectly match their diverse coffee lineup. With locations in Marrickville and Mosman, Roastville’s reputation for excellence is reinforced by loyal local fans and a commitment to transparency and quality. Take a seat, savor something special, and discover why Roastville has become a beloved cornerstone of Sydney’s coffee scene.

name: Haven Coffee - Haymarket
excerpt_short: Sydney's soulful specialty brews. A haven for coffee lovers, where innovation meets comfort.
excerpt_long: Sydney's Haven Coffee: Soulful specialty brews in Haymarket. Experience meticulous coffee, from V60 to espresso, alongside delicious bites. A true coffee haven.
summary: Sydney's Haven Coffee: a Haymarket haven. Minimalist space, exceptional brews. From V60 to espresso, find your perfect cup. Breakfast bites & community converge.
flavour_profile: Specialty brews, modern ambiance, Asian-inspired eats, coffee tailoring
about:
Haven Coffee Haymarket: Sydney’s Soulful Specialty Brews

Nestled in the heart of Haymarket, Haven Coffee stands as a true mecca for specialty coffee lovers seeking both innovation and comfort. Founded by the coffee-focused trio Roy Yu, Kit Tran, and Bruce Koo—well-regarded in Sydney’s specialty scene—Haven’s sleek, minimal space echoes the cafes of Hong Kong and Tokyo, blending wood, marble, and polished steel for a calming, modern vibe. While the design invites you to linger, it’s the coffee that captures your senses: baristas craft everything from V60 pourover to aeropress and classic espresso, all with meticulous attention to extraction and flavor. Their house Compass Blend sits proudly beside single origins like Kenya Kijani Kiboko AA Plus and El Salvador Finca El Limo, available for sipping or as beans to take home.

Haven’s menu showcases Sydney’s best breakfast bites—think five cheese toasties, curated bagels, and Asian-inspired eats like the signature daikon cake. The kitchen’s creativity perfectly complements the “coffee tailoring” experience, where you’re invited to consult with baristas to find your ideal cup. Recognized with TripAdvisor’s Travelers’ Choice award, Haven’s approachable expertise, innovative workshops, and consistently top-tier brews have earned it a devoted following among foodies and caffeine devotees alike. For those seeking more than just a caffeine fix, Haven offers a sanctuary where exceptional coffee and community converge in style.

name: Nine Yards Coffee
excerpt_short: Sydney's Northern Beaches: Award-winning coffee, roasted fresh. Passion, flavor, and community.
excerpt_long: Sydney's Northern Beaches: Award-winning roastery. Transparent, immersive coffee experience. Freshly roasted micro-lots, community spirit.
summary: Experience coffee artistry at Nine Yards. Award-winning roastery on Sydney's Northern Beaches. Sourced micro-lots, fresh roasts, and a passionate team.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasts, vibrant atmosphere, community hub, flavor
about:
Nine Yards Coffee: Heart and Craft on Sydney’s Northern Beaches

Nine Yards Coffee stands out as a multi-award-winning specialty roaster and cafe at the heart of Sydney’s Northern Beaches, renowned for its transparent, immersive approach to coffee. The vibrant, two-story space lets guests peer into the roastery, blending industrial textures with warmth—think concrete walls, wooden tables, and lush greenery. Upstairs, the cupping room invites patrons to deepen their coffee knowledge or simply soak in the buzz over a meal. The soul of Nine Yards is its passionate team, whose dedication shows from their friendly service to their meticulous roasting—a process that artfully brings out the best in every bean.

The coffee portfolio is a rotating showcase of meticulously sourced micro-lots and single origins from Brazil, Colombia, Ethiopia, and beyond, always roasted fresh on-site. Signature blends like the “Forever Hipster” and “Brightside Blend” anchor a menu featuring their celebrated seasonal offerings. Coffee connoisseurs rave about their filter roasts—such as the Colombia Eduar Giraldo—and house specialties like their acclaimed sticky chai. The all-day menu covers Sydney brunch classics, hearty vegetarian options, and crowd-pleasers like the Japanese crumbed chicken burger and kombucha Bircher muesli. Nine Yards also champions sustainability, ensuring every cup embodies both quality and conscience. This is a destination for true coffee lovers seeking both depth of flavor and community spirit.

name: Primary Coffee
excerpt_short: Sydney's micro-roastery. Weekly roasts, exceptional freshness. Single origins & blends, crafted with precision.
excerpt_long: Sydney's micro-roastery. Weekly Wednesday roasts ensure peak freshness. Single origins & blends, crafted with care. A haven for coffee purity.
summary: Sydney's micro-roasting gem. Weekly Wednesday roasts ensure peak freshness. Single origins & blends, crafted with care. A haven for coffee purity.
flavour_profile: Micro-roasting, weekly roasts, specialty coffee, fresh beans
about:
Primary Coffee Roasters: Sydney’s Micro-Roasting Gem

Tucked into Sydney’s inner city, Primary Coffee Roasters stands out with its singular approach to specialty coffee. Unlike larger operations, Primary roasts only once a week—on Wednesdays—ensuring each batch is exceptionally fresh and thoughtfully crafted. This micro-roaster’s minimalist, modern space pulses with energy, blending the comforting aroma of freshly roasted beans with a spirit of quiet craftsmanship. The vibe is laid-back yet focused: a haven for coffee aficionados seeking purity and precision in every cup.

Primary’s coffee portfolio showcases an evolving selection of single origins and meticulously developed blends, with offerings that celebrate clarity, sweetness, and vibrant character in the cup. Their filter coffees and espresso-based drinks highlight the direct relationships they nurture with producers, emphasizing traceability and seasonality. While renowned for their coffee, the café also curates a menu of house-made and thoughtfully sourced pastries, offering perfect pairings for your brew. Whether you’re keen to discover a limited single-origin release or enjoy a perfectly dialed-in flat white, Primary Coffee Roasters rewards a discerning palate. For optimal freshness, insiders know to grab their beans just after roast day—when the flavor is at its peak.

name: Da Bang Coffee
excerpt_short: Cozy Surry Hills gem. Expertly crafted specialty coffee, warm vibes, and a local favorite.
excerpt_long: Dabang Coffee: Surry Hills' cozy gem. Expertly crafted coffee, warm vibes, and a friendly atmosphere. Your daily dose of urban cool.
summary: Dabang Coffee: Surry Hills' cozy specialty gem. Warm, unpretentious, and buzzing with creative energy. Expertly crafted coffee, a daily ritual for locals.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, cozy vibe, local gem, expert brews, pastries
about:
Dabang Coffee: Surry Hills’ Cozy Specialty Gem

Nestled in the heart of Surry Hills on Foveaux Street, Dabang Coffee radiates an inviting, down-to-earth charm that has earned it a dedicated local following. The understated shopfront belies an interior pulsing with warmth and creative energy—the sort of place where regulars linger over expertly crafted flat whites and baristas greet you by name. Open weekdays from early morning, Dabang stands out as a daily ritual for Sydney’s specialty coffee lovers, offering an ambience that blends comfort with a dash of urban cool. While Dabang’s fame is rooted in its unwavering commitment to quality coffee, the mood here is refreshingly unpretentious, making every visit feel like a much-needed coffee break with friends.

Dabang Coffee showcases a diverse coffee portfolio, celebrating both blends and single-origin offerings sourced from some of the world’s most acclaimed coffee regions. The team’s precision in espresso—and a careful hand with filter methods—ensures each cup highlights the nuanced flavors specialty coffee is loved for. While details on award-winning beans or certifications aren’t spotlighted, the café’s vibrant Instagram presence reveals a steady rotation of delicious pastries and savory bites, proving this is more than a caffeine stop—it’s a destination for top-tier coffee moments in Sydney. For the best experience, arrive early, chat with the barista, and don’t miss out on a pastry to complement your brew.
address: Shop 2/44a Foveaux St, Surry Hills NSW 2010

name: Skittle Lane Circular Quay
excerpt_short: Sydney's design-led specialty coffee. Ethically sourced, expertly roasted, and perfectly brewed.
excerpt_long: Tiny, design-led haven in Circular Quay. Skittle Lane: exceptional, ethically sourced coffee, hand-roasted with passion. A sensory escape.
summary: Tiny, design-led haven in Circular Quay. Skittle Lane roasts ethically sourced beans, offering exceptional espresso & pour-overs. A sensory escape for coffee lovers.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasts, minimalist design, ethical sourcing, urban oasis
about:
Skittle Lane Circular Quay: Sydney’s Design-Driven Specialty Coffee Gem

Skittle Lane Circular Quay distills the vibrant spirit of Sydney's specialty coffee scene into an intimate, design-forward space in the heart of the Quay Quarter Lanes. Founded by husband-and-wife duo Jodie Foster and Dean Wooding, Skittle Lane has earned a cult following since opening its CBD flagship in 2015. Their Circular Quay location is tiny yet welcoming, mirroring a European espresso bar experience where every detail matters—from the neutral, minimalist interiors to the selection of artisan ceramics and classic ACME wares. The genuine hospitality and thoughtful atmosphere make each visit special, inviting both a quick morning takeaway or a rare moment to linger in the urban bustle.

As a specialty coffee roaster, Skittle Lane sets itself apart through an unwavering focus on quality and ethics. Their beans are sourced from small-scale producers across Africa and South America, always through B-Corp certified importers and with full traceability to origin. Each batch is hand-roasted at their Brookvale roastery, offering a seasonal menu that celebrates both refined espresso blends and vibrant single origins. At Circular Quay, their espresso bar serves signature blends alongside meticulously brewed pour-overs, and you’ll find ready-to-brew drip bags and a curated selection of pastries. For those seeking a sensory escape—with a side of exceptional coffee—Skittle Lane Circular Quay is a must-visit for enthusiasts and everyday drinkers alike.

name: ASLAN Coffee Roasters - The Rocks
excerpt_short: Indonesian soul, Sydney roast. Bold flavors, friendly vibes. Your coffee haven in The Rocks.
excerpt_long: Indonesian soul in every cup. ASLAN Coffee Roasters, The Rocks, since 2009. Bold flavors, passionate baristas, and a lion's heart.
summary: Indonesian soul in every cup. ASLAN Coffee Roasters, The Rocks, since 2009. Bold flavors, passionate baristas, and a lion's heart.
flavour_profile: Indonesian-inspired, bold flavors, artisanal roasting, cozy vibe
about:
ASLAN Coffee Roasters – The Heart of The Rocks’ Coffee Culture

Nestled in the historic Nurses Walk, ASLAN Coffee Roasters at The Rocks channels the spirit of the Indonesian Archipelago into every cup. Since their founding in 2009, ASLAN has earned a loyal following thanks to their soulful approach: sourcing beans predominantly from Indonesia and roasting in Sydney to highlight bold, complex flavors. The house ASLAN Blend—built around organic beans from the female-led Ketiara Co-Op—offers rich notes of hazelnut, dark cacao, and earth, while their seasonal single origins like Bali Batur or Ethiopia Yirgacheffe cater to adventurous palates. Enjoy traditional espresso, batch brew, cold brew, or nitro, all meticulously prepared by passionate baristas in an intimate space marked by friendly service and a striking lion mural.

ASLAN isn't just about coffee; their all-day breakfast and brunch menus draw crowds for fluffy pancakes crowned with berries and ice cream, alongside a curated lineup of pastries and cakes. With every detail—from the warmth of the staff to their commitment to coffee excellence—ASLAN Coffee Roasters crafts a nostalgic, comforting experience that resonates with both locals and visiting aficionados. Whether you’re dropping in for a morning flat white or seeking a quiet refuge in The Rocks, ASLAN’s vibrant atmosphere and dedication to quality make it a true standout in Sydney’s specialty coffee scene.

name: King Carlos Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Sydney's King Carlos: Specialty coffee with soul. Direct-sourced, slow-roasted, award-winning beans.
excerpt_long: Sydney's King Carlos: Specialty coffee with soul. Ethically sourced, slow-roasted 100% Arabica beans. Award-winning Re Firma Blend & barista school.
summary: Hurstville's King Carlos: Sydney's specialty coffee soul. Ethically sourced, slow-roasted 100% Arabica. Award-winning Re Firma Blend & seasonal delights await.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasts, ethical sourcing, vibrant atmosphere
about:
King Carlos Coffee Roasters: A Sydney Specialty with Soul

Step into King Carlos Coffee Roasters in Hurstville and you’re immediately greeted by an atmosphere that buzzes with genuine passion for coffee culture. This Sydney-based roaster has etched its name into the specialty coffee scene with a commitment to sustainability, ethical sourcing, and a mindful approach to every cup. With years of roasting mastery behind them, their beans are always 100% Arabica, direct-sourced from the world’s premium coffee-growing regions. The air here is filled with the aroma of floral, mild, and impeccably rounded coffees—every sip a testament to their slow-roasting philosophy and relentless pursuit of flavor perfection.

Their multi award-winning Re Firma Blend stands as their flagship offering, boasting seven medals across top-tier competitions like the Sydney Fine Food Show, Golden Bean, and Australian International Coffee Awards. The portfolio also features a spectrum of carefully selected single origins and rotating seasonal blends, all available alongside freshly baked pastries and light café fare. Whether dropping in for a golden-bodied espresso or seeking hands-on barista training at their in-house school, coffee enthusiasts find King Carlos a vibrant hub. It’s more than a roastery—it’s a celebration of Sydney’s spirited coffee community, led by a team whose expertise and love for the craft shine through every detail.
hours_weekday: Mon - Thu: 7:00 AM - 1:00 PM, Fri: 7:00 AM - 2:00 PM

name: Frankie's Beans
excerpt_short: Sydney's award-winning coffee soul. Frankie's Beans: quality, innovation, and vibrant energy.
excerpt_long: Frankie's Beans: Sydney's award-winning roastery. Frank's vision: perfect pours & industry standards. Inventive blends, hearty bites. Experience coffee artistry.
summary: Frankie's Beans: Sydney's award-winning coffee soul. Frank's vision: perfect pours & industry elevation. Inventive blends, single origins, and hearty bites await.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasts, innovative blends, vibrant atmosphere
about:
Frankie’s Beans: The Soul of Sydney’s Award-Winning Coffee Scene

Step into Frankie’s Beans and you’re instantly embraced by the vibrant energy that defines Sydney’s specialty coffee culture. Founded by Frank, a visionary dedicated to both perfecting every pour and elevating the coffee industry’s standards, this Darlinghurst roastery stands as a beacon for passionate coffee lovers. Renowned for pushing traditional boundaries, Frankie’s Beans is a multi-award-winning specialty coffee roaster, consistently celebrated for their relentless pursuit of quality and innovation. Their commitment to sourcing some of the world’s finest beans sets them apart, earning them a loyal following and critical acclaim within both mainstream circles and the specialty coffee industry.

The coffee lineup showcases inventive blends and single origins, with the “3 Colombians Blend” earning particular fame—each Colombian bean is roasted three ways to unlock layers of complexity and character. Their baristas expertly prepare everything from smooth lattes to rich espressos, ensuring every cup sings with depth and vibrancy. Frankie’s Beans also delights visitors with hearty meals and creative bites, such as cheeseburger jaffles and corn fritters, making it a true destination for both foodies and coffee aficionados. For the full Frankie’s experience, ask for their award-winning blend and pair it with a house specialty for a taste of Sydney’s coffee artistry at its finest.
address: 1 Kings Cross Rd, Potts Point New South Wales, Australia

name: Seventhwave Coffee & Tea
excerpt_short: Industrial heart, soulful brews. Seventhwave: Redfern's coffee art, community-driven.
excerpt_long: Industrial roastery with soul. Since 2018, crafting specialty coffee, fueling community, and brewing positive change, cup by cup.
summary: Industrial heart, soulful brews. Seventhwave: Redfern's coffee haven since 2018. Meticulous craft, community impact. Patience, experimentation, and exceptional coffee.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasts, community impact, industrial charm, flavor
about:
Seventhwave Coffee & Tea: An Industrial-Hearted Roastery with Soul

Tucked away in Redfern’s creative warehouse district, Seventhwave Coffee & Tea is where meticulous coffee craft meets meaningful community impact. Since 2018, this industrial-roaster-meets-brewery has made waves with a hands-on approach—reimagining coffee as a serious, evolving art and never just a fleeting trend. The ethos here is clear: patience, experimentation, and a refusal to rest until each roast unlocks its full potential. Their coffee portfolio features gems like the House blend, The Tsunami, Sumatra Blue Bianca, and Flying Jaguar, alongside celebrated single origins like Colombia Anaerobic, Tanzania Peaberry, and Bolivia Java Anaerobic. Cold drip coffee matures for months, and the New Guinea batch is known to bloom after weeks of rest, offering a nuanced experience even for the most discerning palates.

But Seventhwave isn’t only about the cup—it’s about connection. As proud Café Smart partners, every pour helps drive grassroots initiatives, including vital support for women at risk in Sydney. The food menu is crafted to complement their nuanced brews, and tastings (by appointment) come with pro tips and lively chats about getting the most from hardware or hand-blended teas. Accolades include features on The Today Show and in The Sydney Morning Herald, cementing their reputation as pioneers in Australia’s specialty coffee scene. Seventhwave is more than a coffee destination—it’s a Redfern heartbeat, fueling positive change, cup by cup.
address: Warehouse B/15 Cornwallis St, Redfern NSW 2016

name: Numero Uno Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Italian heritage meets Sydney's specialty coffee. Ethically sourced, award-winning roastery.
excerpt_long: Italian heritage meets Sydney's specialty coffee. Freshly roasted beans, ethical sourcing, and award-winning blends await. Experience coffee innovation.
summary: Italian heritage meets Sydney's specialty coffee. Founded by Gina Di Brita, ethical sourcing & award-winning roasts. Experience authentic, innovative coffee.
flavour_profile: Italian heritage, specialty roasts, ethical sourcing, artisanal
about:
Numero Uno Coffee Roasters: Where Italian Heritage Meets Sydney’s Specialty Coffee Scene

Tucked away in the heart of St Peters, Numero Uno Coffee Roasters channels over two decades of passion and expertise into every cup. Founded by Gina Di Brita—a trailblazer in the industry and noted coffee judge—this roastery thrives on Italian-inspired tradition while driving the vanguard of Australia’s specialty scene. The industrial-chic espresso bar sits just steps from the humming roastery, filling the air with the heady aroma of freshly roasted beans and crafting a sanctuary for coffee lovers craving authenticity and innovation. Numero Uno’s ethos centers on ethical sourcing, championing women-led producers and forging direct trade relationships that elevate not just the final cup, but the entire global coffee supply chain.

The portfolio showcases a curated range of blends—like the seasonal Picasso, organic South Central, and bold Black Mamba—alongside rotating single origins from Papua New Guinea, Brazil, Colombia, Rwanda, Ethiopia, and beyond. This dedication to quality has notched prestigious accolades, including multiple Golden Bean awards for their espresso and organic creations, and solidified Gina’s reputation as Australian Coffee Woman of the Year. Pair your chosen brew with house-made pastries or branch out with specialty teas and Ghanaian hot chocolate. Whether you’re a seasoned barista or a weekend coffee adventurer, insider tip: let the team recommend their latest single origin for a tasting journey that truly captures Numero Uno’s soul.

name: Pablo & Rusty's Sydney CBD
excerpt_short: Sydney CBD's Pablo & Rusty's: Exceptional coffee, purpose-driven, and community-focused.
excerpt_long: Sydney CBD's Pablo & Rusty's: Coffee passion meets purpose. Exceptional brews, inventive brunch, and a commitment to sustainability. A vibrant refuge.
summary: Sydney CBD's Pablo & Rusty's: a purpose-driven haven. Exceptional brews, inventive brunch, and a commitment to sustainability. Experience coffee with a conscience.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, sustainable, vibrant atmosphere, ethical sourcing
about:
Pablo & Rusty’s Sydney CBD: Where Coffee Passion Meets Purpose

Nestled on bustling Castlereagh Street, Pablo & Rusty’s Sydney CBD outpost is a cornerstone of the city’s specialty coffee scene. Founded in 2003 by Saxon Wright, whose vision was inspired by his coffee-loving brothers-in-law, this café pulses with character and community at its core. Step inside and the lively hum, chic fit-out, and dedicated staff reveal why Pablo & Rusty’s consistently ranks among Sydney’s top coffee destinations. Beyond exceptional brews, the venue’s commitment to sustainability shines: the business is B Corp certified, a 1% for the Planet member, and holds Carbon Neutral Organisation Certification, proof that every cup poured benefits both drinker and planet.

The menu showcases inventive brunches, fresh pastries, and house specialties that have earned the café a Travelers’ Choice award and hundreds of glowing reviews. Coffee aficionados will appreciate their portfolio of expertly roasted blends—like Trailblazer, Pioneer, and Porter—as well as ethically sourced single origins served through a range of brew methods. Whether you’re after a meticulously crafted espresso or a memorable brunch, Pablo & Rusty’s offers a vibrant, purpose-driven refuge for anyone seeking more than just a morning caffeine fix.

name: Terra Cotta Roasters Darlington
excerpt_short: Darlington's heart. Specialty coffee, community, and a welcoming soul. Balcony bliss.
excerpt_long: Darlington's heart. Specialty coffee, community, and inclusivity. Balcony sanctuary. Balanced, sweet house blend. Welcoming everyone with open arms.
summary: Darlington's heart. Specialty coffee, chef-crafted fare, and a community hub. Inviting soul, open arms, and a perfect brew for everyone.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, community hub, inclusive vibe, culinary flair
about:
Terra Cotta Roasters: Darlington’s Heartbeat for Specialty Coffee and Community

Tucked away just off Cleveland Street in Darlington, Terra Cotta Roasters sets itself apart with an inviting soul and strong commitment to inclusion and diversity. The open balcony, dappled in sunlight, is a local sanctuary where regulars gather for meticulously brewed specialty coffee and chef-crafted fare. Terra Cotta’s house blend stands out for its balanced richness and subtle sweetness—a harmonious cup that lingers in memory. Their menu showcases quality and care, featuring expertly prepared classics like roaster’s eggs, signature sandwiches, and decadent pulled pork burgers, complemented by hand-crafted drinks ranging from velvety flat whites to aromatic batch and cold brews. Every visit is underscored by warm, attentive service that keeps Darlington abuzz with loyal fans.

Though not yet spotlighted with major coffee awards, Terra Cotta Roasters consistently earns glowing reviews for both its coffee and its culinary creations, securing a Travellers’ Choice accolade and a cherished spot among Sydney’s top cafes. The space radiates a progressive ethos, proudly supporting diversity and advocating for inclusivity and anti-racism. Whether you’re a longtime local or visiting student, Terra Cotta Roasters offers more than just specialty beans: it’s a community hub where everyone is welcomed with open arms—and a perfectly brewed cup. Pro tip: snag a seat on the balcony and pair your morning batch brew with a house-baked pastry for the quintessential Terra Cotta experience.

name: Knock Specialty Coffee
excerpt_short: Knock: Surry Hills' coffee haven. Fresh brews, community vibes, and specialty coffee perfection.
excerpt_long: Knock: Surry Hills' coffee haven. Minimalist, warm, and community-focused. Experience expertly crafted espresso and rotating, adventurous brews.
summary: Knock Specialty Coffee: Surry Hills' haven for coffee lovers. Minimalist, warm, and community-focused. Experience expertly crafted espresso and rotating, fresh brews.
flavour_profile: Specialty brews, cozy ambiance, local charm, expert baristas
about:
Knock Specialty Coffee: Surry Hills’ Sanctuary for the Devoted Coffee Lover

Step into Knock Specialty Coffee and you’re greeted by more than just the aroma of perfectly extracted espresso—it’s a reflection of Surry Hills’ contemporary spirit with a welcoming neighborhood heart. Tucked into 246 Cleveland Street, Knock radiates with charm: minimalist interiors, warm lighting, and a gentle buzz of conversation, all expertly paired with the rich, nuanced brews from Veneziano Coffee Roasters. Crowd favorites include espresso and cold brew, crafted on premium equipment like the La Marzocco Linea PB and Mahlkönig grinders, ensuring every cup meets specialty standards. The coffee menu skews towards the adventurous, always fresh, and always rotating, while breakfast, lunch, and decadent desserts round out the experience for those seeking more than just caffeine.

Beyond coffee, Knock’s reputation is built on community and consistency. Loyal locals rave about the smooth texture of their flat whites, the robust flavor profiles, and the friendly baristas who know their regulars by name. While the spotlight shines brightest on coffee, Knock’s offerings extend to thoughtfully curated pastries and all-day bites, making it a go-to for catch-ups or a quick refuel. Whether you’re diving into a filter or sipping a creamy piccolo, Knock Specialty Coffee stands as a testament to Sydney’s specialty scene—unpretentious, passionate, and always striving for that perfect cup.
address: Shop 1/246 Cleveland St, Surry Hills NSW 2010

name: The Q on Harris
excerpt_short: Indonesian coffee haven. Micro-roastery with creative eats. Freshly roasted, traceable beans.
excerpt_long: Indonesian coffee haven. Micro-roastery with rare beans, roasted fresh. Creative eats, friendly vibes. A Sydney gem for coffee lovers.
summary: Discover The Q on Harris: Sydney's hidden gem. Indonesian specialty coffee, roasted in-house. Experience traceable origins, creative eats, and a vibrant community vibe.
flavour_profile: Indonesian beans, micro-roastery, creative eats, cozy courtyard
about:
The Q on Harris: Sydney’s Hidden Gem for Indonesian Coffee and Creative Eats

Tucked away on Harris Street in Ultimo, The Q on Harris stands out as a soulful specialty café and micro-roastery that captures the heart of the city’s coffee scene. Owner Benji Salim, renowned for his passion and deep Indonesian roots, directly sources rare and high-quality beans from Indonesian farms, roasting each lot on-site for ultimate freshness. The aroma of Java, Sumatra, and Sulawesi coffees fills the air, with house favorites like Java Frinsa Estate Peaberry and Toarco Toraja available as espresso or filter—each cup tells the story of traceable origins and Benji’s dedication to sustainable sourcing. The Q on Harris regularly earns raves, even achieving TripAdvisor’s Travelers’ Choice award, and is known for its friendly service and lively community vibe.

The food at The Q is as memorable as its coffee. Chef Toiya brings Japanese flair to a seasonal menu anchored in quality and simplicity. Must-try dishes include the signature Kabuki Waffle and Kinoko Tartine, alongside smoked meats lovingly prepared in-house for a rich, unique flavor. The bright courtyard, dotted with greenery and thoughtful design touches, invites guests to linger over a slow meal or coffee flight. For an authentic and inventive coffee experience—where every cup is a testament to artisan roasting and genuine hospitality—The Q on Harris is a destination you’ll want to revisit often.

name: Sample Coffee
excerpt_short: Ethical, community-focused micro-roastery. Sourcing globally, roasting locally. Coffee with heart.
excerpt_long: Sydney's heart, Sample Coffee: ethical, community-focused, and meticulously crafted. Discover global beans, roasted to perfection, and savor every cup.
summary: Sydney's heart, Sample Coffee: micro-roasters with soul. Ethically sourced beans, roasted to perfection. Community, joy, and exceptional coffee await.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasts, ethical sourcing, community hub, cozy vibe
about:
Sample Coffee: Sydney’s Boutique Micro-Roaster with Heart

Nestled in the heart of Sydney, Sample Coffee Roasters stands out as a specialty coffee haven where meticulous craft meets genuine community spirit. Homegrown since its founding, Sample Coffee has built a loyal following thanks to its philosophy of “thinking small” — focusing on the details, nurturing long-term relationships with farmers, and creating moments of joy for every customer. As a Certified B Corporation since 2019 and re-certified in 2022, Sample is recognized for its ethical sourcing, sustainability, and transparent operations, setting it apart in Australia’s competitive coffee scene. Their bright, welcoming spaces exude a calm, purposeful vibe, perfect for lingering over a carefully brewed cup or connecting with fellow coffee lovers.

Sample Coffee’s ever-evolving portfolio celebrates diversity, featuring responsibly sourced beans from origins across the globe, roasted on their state-of-the-art Loring for clean, expressive flavors. Whether you crave the clarity of a single-origin filter or the depth of a signature blend, there’s a cup for every palate. Beyond coffee, Sample is known for its wholesome food and inviting pastries, making it a favorite brunch and catch-up spot. Barista training, rotating seasonal menus, and vibrant community events round out the experience. For those eager to discover more, their home subscription service and in-café brew guides invite you to explore the world of specialty coffee, Sample-style.
address: Shop 1a, 118 Devonshire Street (cnr, Holt St, Surry Hills NSW 2010

name: Artificer Coffee
excerpt_short: Pure specialty. Unfiltered vibe. Artificer Coffee: where quality is the sole focus.
excerpt_long: Artificer Coffee: Pure specialty, unfiltered vibe. Exceptional coffee, no distractions. Single origins, in-house roasted. Quality is the sole focus.
summary: Artificer Coffee: Pure specialty, unfiltered vibe. Sunlit Surry Hills haven. Exceptional coffee, no distractions. Single origins, seasonal blends. Quality is the unwavering focus.
flavour_profile: Specialty roasts, purist approach, minimalist vibe, expert craft
about:
Artificer Coffee: Pure Specialty, Unfiltered Vibe

Artificer Coffee, nestled on sunlit Bourke Street in Surry Hills, Sydney, is a purist’s temple for specialty coffee. Here, the philosophy is singular—exceptional coffee, no distractions. Co-founders Shoji Sasa and Dan Yee are both highly respected figures in Australia’s specialty coffee scene, renowned for their uncompromising approach to bean selection, roasting, and brewing. In the minimalist, Scandinavian-inspired space, you’ll find a knowledgeable team obsessed with extracting the unique essence of each origin. The vibe is buzzing but unpretentious; a stand-up bar invites direct conversation with the baristas, and the aroma of freshly roasted beans wafts from the Joper roaster tucked behind the counter.

Artificer’s coffee portfolio is ever-evolving, with all beans roasted in-house. Expect a rotating cast of single origins—available as espresso or filter (batch brew and Kalita Wave)—and a seasonal blend that consistently delivers layered flavor and clean, fruit-driven finishes. While there’s no food or pastry menu (true to their no-frills ethos), every cup is meticulously crafted, batch brews are pre-sieved for clarity, and even the espresso cups are thoughtfully weighty. Artificer stands out as a must-visit for anyone serious about coffee and searching for a place where quality is the sole, unwavering focus.

name: Edition Roasters
excerpt_short: Japanese-Scandi soul. Omni-roasted, single-origin coffee. A curated experience.
excerpt_long: Japanese-Scandi minimalism meets coffee mastery. Omni-roasted single origins, exquisite flavors, and a unique experience await.
summary: Japanese-Scandi minimalism meets coffee mastery. Edition Roasters: single-origin beans omni-roasted to perfection. Experience a unique coffee journey.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasts, Japanese-Scandi fusion, curated experience
about: ## Edition Roasters: Japanese-Scandi Minimalism Meets Coffee MasteryStep into Edition Roasters and you’ll instantly sense you’re somewhere special—where Japanese and Scandinavian aesthetics harmonise in a space inspired by old Japanese farmhouses. This award-winning cafe doesn’t just serve coffee; it curates an experience crafted for the truly discerning. Edition prides itself on omni-roasting single origin coffee, meticulously bringing out distinct profiles from beans sourced worldwide: Colombia’s fruit-forward El Diviso, El Salvador’s vibrant Geisha from Finca El Manzano, and Brazil Mantiqueira’s deeply caramel notes. Every cup is a journey—whether it’s the toffee apple sweetness of their Costa Rican filter or the molasses and yuzu spark in their sugarcane decaf. Edition’s unique soul extends beyond coffee. Expect a menu where Japanese-Scandi fusion elevates the ordinary to the exceptional, like fine-dining level udon, delicate milk bun rolls, or yuzu white chocolate desserts. Specialty teas—from China’s Chung Feng jasmine to Japan’s Gyokuro—round out the offerings for non-coffee drinkers. With a loyal following and multiple sleek Sydney locations, Edition is Sydney’s next-level cafe destination; don’t leave without taking home a bag of their beans for your own kitchen ritual.
hours_weekday: Mon - Wed: 8:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Thu - Fri: 8:00 AM - 10:00 PM

name: Paramount Coffee Project
excerpt_short: Sydney's specialty coffee haven. Rotating roasters, exceptional brews, and vibrant vibes.
excerpt_long: Sydney's PCP: Where specialty coffee thrives. Rotating global roasters, exceptional quality, and a vibrant community. Brunch is a must!
summary: Sydney's PCP: Where specialty coffee thrives. Discover global roasters, enjoy seasonal eats, and connect with coffee culture in a vibrant, welcoming space.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, global roasters, innovative brunch, vibrant atmosphere
about:
Paramount Coffee Project: Sydney’s Epicenter of Specialty Coffee Culture

Paramount Coffee Project (PCP) in Surry Hills embodies the creative pulse of Sydney’s specialty coffee scene, brought to life by industry icons Jin Ng (Paramount House), Mark Dundon (Seven Seeds), and Russell Beard (Reuben Hills). Since opening in 2013, PCP’s approach has been refreshingly unbiased—showcasing an ever-rotating selection of local and international coffees, all chosen via blind cuppings to ensure exceptional cup quality and flavor. The café’s vibrant space buzzes with energy, offering an inviting atmosphere where coffee aficionados and curious newcomers can discover single origins and blends from leading roasters around the globe. Transparency in sourcing and a commitment to fostering connections between producers and drinkers is the beating heart of their operation.

PCP’s menu extends beyond stellar coffee: expect a seasonal breakfast and lunch lineup crafted with local produce. House specialties like crumbed eggs on toast with mustard braised ham hock and skillet pancakes with mascarpone and rosemary-poached stone fruit are cult favorites, consistently winning praise for their creativity and flavor. Vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free options ensure all are welcome at the table. Whether you’re sipping a meticulously dialed-in espresso or indulging in one of Sydney’s best brunches, PCP stands out as a must-visit destination—where passion for specialty coffee meets a genuine sense of community and innovation.

name: The Coffee Roaster
excerpt_short: Sydney's soulful specialty coffee haven. Award-winning roasts, community, and craft. Experience the journey.
excerpt_long: Sydney's soulful specialty haven. Witness the craft, taste award-winning blends & single origins. Led by Mick, experience coffee with a Sydney soul.
summary: Sydney's soulful specialty haven. Witness the craft, from bean to cup. Award-winning coffee, roasted with passion. Community and flavour, hand-in-hand.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasting, specialty blends, welcoming vibe, local gem
about:
The Coffee Roaster: Sydney’s Soulful Specialty Haven

Step into The Coffee Roaster on Botany Road, and you’re instantly immersed in the energy of a true specialty coffee institution—a place where craft and community go hand-in-hand. This Alexandria roastery isn’t just about serving premium Arabica; it’s about showcasing the entire journey. Watch the magic happen behind glass as passionate roasters use their Chinook Air Flow system, maximising flavour and aroma in every batch. The crew, led by the ever-welcoming Mick, delights in introducing newcomers to their medal-winning house blend, single origins, and signature Nitro Cold Brew. Take advantage of their generous welcome: mention it’s your first time, and you might just score a complimentary cup—though don’t expect to fool the team twice with their steel-trap memory.

The Coffee Roaster’s shelves sparkle with industry acclaim, from Reserve Champion Gold for espresso at the Royal Hobart Fine Food Show to a bounty of silvers and bronzes at the Royal Sydney Fine Food Show and the Australian International Coffee Awards. Their vibrant coffee portfolio includes both robust blends and stand-out single origins, with award-winning chocolate-coated Kenyan Peaberry beans a must-try novelty. Beyond coffee, the café’s food is equally celebrated—Eggs Benny, bacon and egg rolls, freshly baked pastries, and muffins offer a hearty complement. Whether you’re after a pour over, a cold brew, or an unbeatable local breakfast, The Coffee Roaster delivers an honest, unpretentious specialty coffee experience with a distinct Sydney soul.

name: Brother Baba Budan
excerpt_short: Melbourne's rebellious coffee icon. Unforgettable vibe, expertly roasted, and specialty brews.
excerpt_long: Melbourne's rebellious coffee icon. Brother Baba Budan: quirky, compact, unforgettable vibe. Seven Seeds coffee since 2003. Meticulously brewed, award-winning beans.
summary: A rebellious Melbourne coffee icon. Brother Baba Budan: quirky, compact, and serving exceptional Seven Seeds coffee since 2003. Unforgettable vibe, minimal seating, maximum flavor.
flavour_profile: Quirky vibe, specialty coffee, iconic chairs, CBD gem
about:
Brother Baba Budan: Melbourne’s Rebellious Coffee Icon

Step into Brother Baba Budan and you’re met with more than just the aroma of expertly roasted coffee—you’re greeted by a café with true character and an unforgettable vibe. Named for the legendary merchant who famously smuggled seven coffee seeds out of Yemen, this quirky, compact spot in Melbourne’s CBD has etched its place as one of the city’s specialty coffee pioneers. The instantly iconic ceiling festooned with chairs perfectly mirrors the playful, rebellious soul of the venue, while the minimal seating encourages a quick yet memorable coffee break for locals and visitors alike.

Serving Seven Seeds coffee since 2003, Brother Baba Budan is the go-to destination for those seeking meticulously brewed espresso, batch brew, or filter methods, all poured from the revered La Marzocco Linea PB. The rotating bean portfolio features traceable origins and award-winning lots, ensuring every cup delivers Melbourne’s signature depth and complexity. Alongside the stellar coffee, a selection of toasted sandwiches, cakes, and pastries tempts from the counter, while shelves are stocked with retail beans, drip coffee bags, and specialty treats like Birdsnake Chocolate and Fly High Chai. For the ultimate experience, arrive early—then savor a slice of Melbourne’s coffee heritage in one of its most beloved, storied cafes.
hours_weekday: Mon - Wed: 7:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Thu - Fri: 7:00 AM - 6:00 PM

name: Reuben Hills
excerpt_short: Surry Hills' specialty coffee pioneer. Roasting excellence, South American flavors, and community.
excerpt_long: Experience the soul of Australian specialty coffee at Reuben Hills. Roasting magic, vibrant flavors, and community converge in Surry Hills.
summary: Experience the vibrant soul of Australian specialty coffee at Reuben Hills. Transparency, community, and exceptional coffee from around the world.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, on-site roastery, vibrant atmosphere, global origins
about:
Reuben Hills: Surry Hills’ Trailblazer in Specialty Coffee

Step into Reuben Hills and you’re immediately immersed in the vibrant soul of Australia’s specialty coffee movement. Founded by Russell Beard, a noted leader in the coffee scene, and Nathan Borg, an expert roaster, Reuben Hills was designed with transparency and community at its core. The industrial-chic space in Surry Hills seamlessly combines a bustling cafe, on-site roastery, and visible coffee lab, welcoming enthusiasts to watch the roasting magic unfold. Natural light, polished concrete, and exposed beams set the stage for passionate conversation and exploration, while public cuppings and brew guides empower visitors to learn and taste alongside the pros.

Reuben Hills’ coffee portfolio is a dynamic showcase of the world’s finest origins—think Ethiopia’s legendary Yirgachefe, Colombian microlots like the award-recognized Geisha from Israel Hernández, and standout single origins from Peru, Kenya, and Guatemala. Their house and espresso blends are celebrated for their brightness and balance, while unique offerings like steep bag sachets make specialty coffee accessible anywhere. Beyond coffee, the menu delights with inventive South American-inspired fare—famed for their baked eggs, fried chicken, and diverse brunch options, all with vegan and gluten-free choices. Whether you’re dropping by for a cup, a meal, or a masterclass in coffee, Reuben Hills is a must-visit for those seeking excellence, creativity, and genuine hospitality in every cup.

name: Single O Surry Hills
excerpt_short: Sydney's original specialty coffee rebels. Pioneering third-wave since 2003. Passion in every cup.
excerpt_long: Sydney's original specialty coffee rebel. Single O: pioneering third-wave roasting since 2003. Expect vibrant energy, killer coffee, and a dash of irreverence.
summary: Sydney's original specialty coffee rebel. Founded in 2003, Single O pioneered third-wave roasting. Expect vibrant energy, rotating single origins, and a legendary brew.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee pioneer, vibrant atmosphere, unique blends
about:
Single O Surry Hills: Sydney’s Original Temple of Specialty Coffee

Single O Surry Hills is where Sydney’s specialty coffee scene found its rebel soul. Founded in 2003 by a pair of single-origin devotees fleeing the corporate grind, this Reservoir Street icon became the launchpad for third-wave roasting and brewing in the city. With a legendary reputation among locals and travelers alike, Single O pioneered the world’s first batch brew bar and led early advocacy on climate impact in coffee, always mixing high standards with a streak of caffeine-fueled irreverence. The main café and neighboring Sideshow brew bar hum with a vibrant, communal energy — curbside tables, contagious coffee passion, and friendly staff who remember your favorite origin.

The heart of Single O is its ever-evolving coffee portfolio: expect their signature Reservoir blend alongside a rotating cast of up to four single origins, including standout picks like “Sweet Nectar” (honey, tropical fruit), “Balanced Like a Monk” (ripe berry, spice, chocolate), and the annual Sugarplum Festival Reserve Blend. Both espresso and filter fans are equally spoiled, with batch brew on tap, cold brew, single-origin pour overs, and even home-compostable “Parachute” drip bags. The all-day menu is a brunch lover’s delight, with plenty of vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free options. Drop by for a meticulously crafted cup, stay for the ingredient-driven food, and soak up the pioneering spirit that keeps Single O at the top of Sydney’s coffee game.

name: Mecca Coffee - Alexandria
excerpt_short: Sydney's specialty coffee pioneer. Direct trade, light roasts, and community vibes.
excerpt_long: Sydney's specialty coffee heart. Mecca Alexandria: direct trade, light roasts, and a community vibe. Exceptional coffee, ethical sourcing, and delicious food.
summary: Sydney's specialty coffee heart. Mecca Alexandria: direct trade, light roasts, and a community vibe. Exceptional beans, award-winning brews, and a soulful experience.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee pioneer, light roasts, ethical sourcing, vibrant
about:
Mecca Alexandria: Where Sydney’s Specialty Coffee Heart Beats

Step into Mecca Alexandria and you’ll feel the buzz of a true specialty coffee institution—one that helped shape Sydney’s modern coffee scene. Founded by Paul Geshos in 2005, Mecca pioneered direct trade and light roasting, bringing the vibrant flavors of origins like Ethiopia, Kenya, and Mexico to Australian cups long before it was the norm. The café, set in a striking industrial warehouse designed by architect Cameron Krone, serves as both a bustling roastery and a warm, community-centric café. Mecca’s dedication to ethical sourcing, transparency, and farmer partnership is at the core of every cup. The Producers Series rotates daily on pour-over and batch brew, spotlighting exceptional single producer lots, while award-winning and rare coffees—like the COE-winning Geisha from Honduras—demonstrate their relentless pursuit of quality and distinction.

Beyond its coffee pedigree, Mecca Alexandria hums with an inviting, laid-back energy. Chef Brett Barbuto crafts a seasonal menu with cult favorites like Avo Toast—elevated by miso emulsion—as well as the Chilli Scram and the MFC Roll. All food is prepared on-site, with bread and pastries sourced from top local bakeries. Whether you’re a purist chasing rare micro-lots or a local after the perfect brunch, the knowledgeable team will guide you through the day’s brews and menu specials. For a taste of Sydney’s specialty coffee at its soulful best, Mecca Alexandria is unmatched—a beacon for coffee lovers, industry pros, and everyday regulars alike.

name: Small Batch Roasting Co.
excerpt_short: Melbourne's specialty coffee soul. Ethically sourced, expertly roasted. Taste the difference.
excerpt_long: Experience Melbourne's specialty coffee soul. Ethically sourced, expertly roasted beans. Candyman blend: grape, plum, lemon, chocolate. Quality redefined.
summary: Experience Melbourne's specialty coffee soul. Ethically sourced beans, expertly roasted in a former warehouse. Candyman blend: grape, plum, lemon pastry, dark chocolate.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasts, ethical sourcing, vibrant flavors, warehouse charm
about:
Small Batch Roasting Co.: North Melbourne’s Heartbeat of Specialty Coffee

Stepping into Small Batch Roasting Co. is a journey into the soul of Melbourne’s specialty coffee scene. Founded by Andrew Kelly, a pioneer who first won hearts with the acclaimed Auction Rooms café, Small Batch is driven by a relentless quest for ethically sourced, transparently traded, and exquisitely roasted beans. Housed in a former warehouse with an antique Italian roaster, the space hums with a deep reverence for provenance—every origin is carefully chosen, from the lush highlands of Guatemala, Colombia, and Ethiopia to direct relationships nurtured with farmers across El Salvador and Costa Rica. The flagship blend, Candyman, is a masterclass in balance and complexity, offering vibrant notes of grape, plum, lemon pastry, and dark chocolate. Here, quality is not just an aspiration—it’s a daily ritual, reflecting both science and art in every batch.

Small Batch’s fame is matched by its dedication: twice named Melbourne’s best café by The Age Good Café Guide, and celebrated for supporting sustainable agriculture and regenerative practices. The coffee portfolio is a showcase of vibrant single origins and blends, with Candyman reigning supreme—crafted to shine both as an espresso and with milk. Food is far from an afterthought; expect elevated, fresh café fare and top-tier pastries to complement your cup. For a true house specialty, request your coffee as the baristas recommend and let them guide you through a tasting journey that redefines “good coffee.” Whether you’re a seasoned aficionado or a curious newcomer, Small Batch invites you to slow down, savor, and discover what coffee can truly be.
address: 3-9 Little Howard St, North Melbourne VIC 3051

name: OKO cafe
excerpt_short: Melbourne's OKO Cafe: Precision, passion, and world-class specialty coffee. Intimate, immersive experience.
excerpt_long: Melbourne's OKO Cafe: Danny's passion brews world-class coffee. Intimate space, global beans, precision brewing. Experience coffee immersion.
summary: OKO Cafe: Melbourne's coffee haven. Intimate space, world-class brews. Precision meets passion. Experience coffee artistry with Danny.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, precision brewing, intimate space, global beans
about:
OKO Cafe: Where Precision Meets Passion in Melbourne’s Specialty Coffee Scene

Tucked away on A’Beckett Street in Melbourne’s CBD, OKO Cafe stands as a testament to how specialty coffee can be both an art and an experience. Led by Danny, whose encyclopedic coffee knowledge and warm, approachable personality have quickly made him a local legend, OKO invites visitors into an intimate, minimalist space designed for true coffee immersion. The small, cube-like venue seats just a handful of guests, encouraging deep conversation with baristas and fellow aficionados while the world goes by outside. Here, the focus is clear: making world-class coffee deeply accessible, with a rotating roster of some of the most respected producers and roasters from around the globe.

OKO’s menu champions quality over quantity, featuring meticulously crafted white and black coffees, standout batch brews, and an ever-changing selection of exotic single origins—recent highlights include a Sidra roasted by Terroir Laboratory, boasting vibrant, fruit-forward notes. The cafe uniquely deploys the iDrip system for filter coffees, allowing baristas to precisely control every variable and consistently extract the best from each bean. Though food options are limited, the occasional pastry pairs perfectly with the nuanced coffees on offer.
