excerpt_short: Yorkshire's heartfelt roastery. Expertly roasted, ethically sourced specialty coffee. Pure flavour, remarkable freshness.
excerpt_long: Yorkshire's heartfelt roastery. Family-run since 2008, Limini crafts specialty coffee with care. Ethical, fresh, and expertly roasted.
summary: Family-run since 2008, Limini Coffee roasts with heart in Yorkshire. Expect expertly roasted beans, ethical sourcing, and a welcoming vibe.
flavour_profile: Yorkshire roastery, artisanal blends, ethical sourcing, warm vibe
about:
Limini Coffee: Yorkshire’s Heartfelt Roastery Experience

Limini Coffee in Cleckheaton, West Yorkshire, is a family-run specialty coffee roaster with a soul as warm as its expertly roasted beans. Founded in 2008 by Youri, who still personally develops every roast profile, Limini has earned a sterling reputation for both the quality of its coffee and its genuine, approachable vibe. Stepping inside, you’re greeted by a blend of craft and care, with Limini’s team dedicated to both the artistry of roasting and to building lasting relationships—whether you’re a home enthusiast or a bustling café owner. Sustainability and ethical sourcing run deep here, with Limini working alongside trusted importers and championing environmental practices.

The coffee portfolio is a testament to their obsession with excellence: signature blends like the 100% Arabica Limini Blend, offering smooth caramel and dark chocolate notes, sit alongside a rotating menu of single origins, Swiss Water Process decaf, and limited edition releases. Every bean is roasted in an oxygen-free environment for pure, clean flavours and remarkable freshness. Limini’s service also extends to supplying top-tier Italian espresso machines and sharing their expertise through training and insightful coffee articles. Whether you’re after velvety espresso, ethical beans, or just a welcoming spot to deepen your coffee journey, Limini Coffee is Yorkshire’s specialty gem.
address: Unit 6, Luddite Way Business Park, Rawfolds Way, Rawfolds, Cleckheaton BD19 5DQ

name: Bean Brothers Coffee Company Ltd
excerpt_short: Artisan coffee, roasted daily. Huddersfield's gem, in a historic mill. Exceptional beans, passionate craft.
excerpt_long: Huddersfield's artisan coffee gem. Hand-roasted in a historic mill. Exceptional single origins & house blends. Great Taste Award winners since 2012.
summary: Hand-roasted specialty coffee, crafted in a Huddersfield mill. Bean Brothers: where passion meets precision. Exceptional single origins & award-winning blends.
flavour_profile: Artisan roastery, award-winning blends, textile mill charm
about:
Bean Brothers Coffee Company: Huddersfield’s Artisan Coffee Gem

Nestled in a characterful old textile mill in Huddersfield’s Fairfield Mills, Bean Brothers Coffee Company radiates the spirit of true specialty coffee craftsmanship. Founded by two brothers determined to elevate coffee quality and consistency—originally at their celebrated shop, Coffeevolution—Bean Brothers has become a beacon for coffee lovers across West Yorkshire and beyond. Since 2012, this family-run, small batch roastery has built its reputation on meticulous sourcing, daily hand-roasting, and a hands-on approach that ensures every bag reflects uncompromising standards. Their skilled team thrives on the art of profiling and roasting the world’s finest beans, focusing on exceptional single origins while also mastering their own house espresso blends, beloved in cafes nationwide.

Bean Brothers’ dedication to quality is recognised at the highest level: they are Great Taste Award winners, with standouts like the rum-infused Captain’s Grog and the enduring house blend “Derek” claiming top honours. Visitors and customers delight in a curated portfolio that ranges from bold blends to seasonally rotating single origins, available through flexible subscriptions and retail offerings. While Bean Brothers is first and foremost about coffee, their space and ethos foster a welcoming, passionate community—offering barista training, expert brewing advice, and a genuinely personal touch that sets them apart in the UK specialty coffee scene.
address: Bean Brothers Coffee Co, Britannia Mills, Huddersfield HD1 3ER

name: Echelon Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Leeds' micro-roastery. Passionate about quality, ethical sourcing, and craft. Memorable coffee experience.
excerpt_long: Leeds' micro-roastery, Echelon, crafts specialty coffee with heart. Ethically sourced beans, expertly roasted, served in a welcoming space.
summary: Leeds' micro-roastery, Echelon, crafts specialty coffee with heart. Ethically sourced beans, expertly roasted, served in a welcoming space. Experience coffee with soul.
flavour_profile: Micro-roastery, ethical sourcing, vibrant flavors, cyclist-friendly
about:
Echelon Coffee Roasters: Leeds’ Micro Roastery with Heart and Craft

Nestled in the Penraevon Industrial Estate since 2018, Echelon Coffee Roasters has carved its own path in Leeds’ specialty coffee landscape. This micro roastery pulses with a contagious passion for quality, transparency, and direct relationships with farmers—a philosophy expressed in every cup they serve. The laid-back, welcoming space is a haven for both devoted regulars and curious cyclists rolling in for a post-ride espresso. Echelon’s vibe is rooted in attention to detail, ethical sourcing, and a genuine respect for the craft of coffee roasting, making every visit both comforting and memorable.

The Echelon portfolio highlights a careful selection of African and Central American origins, with standouts like the Kenya AA Kathangariri—a favorite among specialty aficionados for its vibrant character. Their seasonal espresso, “Esprechelon,” showcases the roastery’s commitment to freshness and expressiveness. While Echelon isn’t a household name on the European awards circuit yet, its local renown comes from consistently delivering top-tier coffees and championing traceable, farmer-focused sourcing. Expect excellent pour-overs, classic espresso, and a curated offering of fresh pastries—fuel for both coffee geeks and the Leeds community. Whether you’re popping in or pedaling by, Echelon offers a memorable specialty coffee experience with genuine soul and substance.
address: Unit 20, Penraevon Industrial Estate, Leeds LS7 2AW

name: La Bottega Milanese
excerpt_short: Milanese soul, Yorkshire heart. Authentic Italian espresso, locally roasted, and utterly delicious.
excerpt_long: Experience Milan in Leeds. Authentic Italian espresso, Yorkshire roasted. Vibrant, welcoming, and detail-obsessed. Taste the tradition.
summary: Experience Milan in Leeds. Authentic Italian espresso, locally roasted. Enjoy expertly crafted coffee, pastries, and aperitivi.
flavour_profile: Italian espresso, Yorkshire roasts, vibrant atmosphere, quality
about:
The Soul of Milan in Leeds: La Bottega Milanese

La Bottega Milanese stands as a living homage to Milanese café culture, buzzing with the energy of true Italian espresso tradition and Leeds’ vibrant urban community. Founded by Alex and his passionate team, this espresso bar is celebrated for its genuine Italian hospitality, contemporary flair, and meticulous attention to quality. The bright, welcoming interior, communal tables, and continental-style outdoor seating invite guests to stay, socialize, and savor every sip. Repeatedly ranked among Leeds’ top coffee destinations, La Bottega Milanese has earned the Travellers’ Choice award on Tripadvisor, a testament to its consistent excellence and devoted clientele.

The coffee portfolio here is both thoughtful and authentic. Signature blends like ‘La Classica’ are roasted locally in Yorkshire, marrying third-wave roasting precision with the deep heritage of Italian espresso. Expect perfectly balanced flat whites, punchy espressos, and a rotating selection of specialty drinks—even matcha lattes. Morning visits feature freshly baked pastries, while lunchtime showcases Italian sandwiches, salads, and pastas—all using the best ingredients sourced from Italy and Yorkshire. Cakes and sweet treats tempt throughout the day, and evenings offer aperitivi, wine, and small plates. Dog-friendly and detail-obsessed, La Bottega’s mantra—“If it’s not from Italy, it’s from Yorkshire”—shines through in every cup and plate.
hours_weekday: Mon - Wed: 7:30 AM - 5:00 PM, Thu - Fri: 7:30 AM - 8:00 PM

name: Maude Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Leeds' hidden gem. Under the railway, crafting specialty coffee with passion and ethical sourcing.
excerpt_long: Beneath Leeds' railway, Maude crafts specialty coffee. Ethical sourcing meets flavor excellence. A decade of passion in every small-batch roast.
summary: Beneath Leeds' railway, Maude Coffee Roasters crafts specialty coffee with soul. Ethical sourcing meets flavor excellence in every small-batch roast. A decade of passion in every cup.
flavour_profile: Micro-roastery, ethical sourcing, railway arch, specialty coffee
about:
Maude Coffee Roasters: Leeds’ Under-the-Railway Gem for Specialty Coffee

Maude Coffee Roasters is the beating heart of Leeds’ specialty coffee scene, tucked away under the railway lines on Railway Street. Founded in 2014 by Matthew “Matt” Van Elkan—an innovator whose journey from food photography and barista work to coffee roasting shaped Maude’s unique soul—this micro-roastery is renowned for uncompromising quality and a decade-long dedication to ethical sourcing and flavor excellence. The Maude team’s tight-knit, passionate approach shines through their small-batch, meticulously curated coffees, which have garnered high praise from local coffee aficionados and specialty media alike. Their reputation is further reflected in special releases, such as the Summit Box Set, which celebrated Maude’s tenth anniversary by showcasing their best coffees from the past decade, preserved and presented for true enthusiasts.

Maude’s portfolio is a coffee lover’s playground, featuring standout offerings like the Foundation Blend (a dynamic Colombian Castillo and Caturra blend), the distinctive Café Delas, Peralta Honey, Finca Patricia, and a smooth Rainbow Decaf. Each coffee is sourced through responsible, traceable supply chains, highlighting Maude’s commitment to both quality and global impact. Complementing their coffee menu are rare brewing wares, including Origami and Orea brewers, and a select range of grinders. While the ambiance radiates the character of its railway arch home—industrial, inviting, and unpretentious—the focus remains on world-class coffee and warm service. For newcomers, sampling the Foundation Blend as a filter or espresso is a must, and catching a limited release or anniversary offering is a true privilege. Maude isn’t just a coffee stop—it’s a Leeds institution for those who crave authenticity, character, and craftsmanship in every cup.
hours_weekday: Mon - Tue, Thu - Fri: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Laynes
excerpt_short: Leeds' independent coffee icon. Craft, community, and exceptional coffee. A true haven.
excerpt_long: Leeds' independent coffee icon. Since 2011, Laynes Espresso serves craft coffee with a unique spirit. Exceptional brews, food, and community.
summary: Laynes Espresso: Leeds' independent coffee icon. Since 2011, serving craft coffee with passion. Exceptional brews, delicious food, and a vibrant community.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, craft focus, local icon, artisanal food
about:
Laynes Espresso: Leeds’ Independent Coffee Icon

Just steps from Leeds Train Station, Laynes Espresso stands as a beacon for specialty coffee lovers seeking uncompromising quality and a unique, independent spirit. Since opening its doors in 2011, Laynes has helped revolutionize the city’s coffee scene with an unwavering focus on craft, service, and community. Owned and run by passionate baristas, the shop is renowned for its collaboration with respected London roaster Square Mile, consistently serving their signature Red Brick espresso blend alongside rotating guest coffees from celebrated roasters like North Star. Whether you’re after a flawlessly pulled flat white or a carefully brewed filter, Laynes’ expertly trained team delivers each cup with precision and warmth. Their status within the scene is underlined by a loyal local following and recognition as a Travelers’ Choice award winner for 2024.

Laynes is more than a coffee haven—it's also celebrated as one of Leeds' best breakfast and brunch spots, with a lively open-plan kitchen and an extensive menu that caters to vegetarians, vegans, and gluten-free diets. The pastry selection, baked in their own Laynes Bakery in Armley, is legendary; don’t miss the decadent cakes or the Brioche Bostok slice, a beloved local secret. By day, Laynes buzzes with commuters and creatives savoring exceptional coffee; by night, it transforms with a curated offering of wines, beers, and coffee cocktails. As a true independent, Laynes embodies Leeds’ coffee culture, offering more than a caffeine fix—a place with soul, character, and an authentic sense of belonging.

name: North Star Coffee Shop
excerpt_short: Ethical coffee, roasted with soul. Yorkshire's B Corp champion, brewing community & flavor.
excerpt_long: Experience Leeds' heartbeat: North Star Coffee. Ethically sourced specialty coffee, roasted with passion. Community, sustainability, and unforgettable flavors await.
summary: Experience the soul of specialty coffee at North Star. Ethically sourced, expertly roasted, and served with a commitment to community and sustainability.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, ethical sourcing, brunch, B Corp, local vibe
about:
North Star Coffee Shop: The Heartbeat of Leeds’ Specialty Coffee Scene

North Star Coffee Shop & General Store at Leeds Dock pulses with a unique soul—born from a love for specialty coffee and a deep commitment to sustainability. As Yorkshire’s only B Corp certified coffee shop, North Star has earned regional acclaim, including Yorkshire Life’s Coffee Shop/Tea Room of the Year 2018, standing out for both quality and community impact. Founded by trailblazers in ethical sourcing, the North Star team is renowned for forging direct relationships with producers in regions like El Salvador and Rwanda, ensuring every cup supports both taste and positive change.

The café’s vibe is contemporary yet welcoming, cultivating a brunch universe where expertly roasted, seasonal single origins and their house blend, The Docks, shine whether in hand-brewed filter or classic espresso drinks. Baristas champion both innovative brews and approachable favorites, while the kitchen serves locally-sourced brunch classics—think fluffy poached pear pancakes and fresh bakes from the award-winning Nova Bakehouse. Families and dogs are greeted with open arms, making North Star the perfect gathering spot for coffee aficionados and casual visitors alike. House specialty tip: try their latest single origin on Aeropress for a truly memorable, apple-forward taste experience.

name: Feel Good Club
excerpt_short: Manchester's Feel Good Club: Specialty coffee, community, and good vibes.
excerpt_long: Manchester's Feel Good Club: Specialty coffee meets community. A welcoming space championing wellbeing, inclusivity, and exceptional brews.
summary: Manchester's Feel Good Club: Specialty coffee meets community. A welcoming space championing wellbeing, inclusivity, and expertly crafted brews.
flavour_profile: Community-focused, wellness, inclusive, local roasts, positive vibes
about:
Feel Good Club: Manchester’s Community-Focused Specialty Coffee Retreat

Nestled in the vibrant heart of Manchester’s Northern Quarter, Feel Good Club isn’t your average coffee shop—it’s a soul-soothing hub where positive energy and community values brew side by side with expertly crafted coffee. Founded by wives Aimie and Kiera Lawlor-Skillen, this flagship café was born from a mission to champion mental wellbeing and inclusivity, particularly for the LGBTQIA+ community. With its minimalist, welcoming interior and affirming messages in every corner, the space radiates warmth and authenticity. Guests are greeted not just with exceptional service but with a genuine spirit of support, from Mental Health First Aid-trained staff to a calendar packed with wellbeing events, yoga, and art showcases.

Coffee here is more than a beverage; it’s an expression of Manchester’s independent roasting culture. The house blend, roasted in collaboration with local maestros Heart & Graft, delivers a cup that’s balanced and lively—perfect for everything from a morning flat white to a leisurely afternoon pour-over. Feel Good Club’s menu features standout all-day brunches, vegan delights, homemade cakes, and an impressive array of craft cocktails and local beers for the evening crowd. Don’t miss their specialty drinks or the signature super greens bowl, and be sure to check their events for a truly feel-good experience that celebrates both coffee and community.
hours_weekday: Mon - Wed: 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Thu - Fri: 9:00 AM - 11:00 PM

name: Coffee Lab Withington
excerpt_short: Greek soul meets Manchester. Award-winning coffee, baklava, and a warm, inviting vibe.
excerpt_long: Greek soul meets Manchester's heart. Award-winning coffee, Greek treats, and a warm vibe. Your new favorite spot for sips and meetups.
summary: Experience Greek warmth in Manchester! Coffee Lab Withington, a top-rated Greek coffee spot, offers award-winning brews, delicious pastries, and a welcoming vibe.
flavour_profile: Greek-inspired, third-wave coffee, community hub, award-winning
about:
The Soul of Greece Meets Manchester at Coffee Lab Withington

Coffee Lab Withington infuses the vibrant spirit of Greek café culture into Manchester’s coffee landscape, establishing itself as the first and only Greek franchise 3rd Wave Coffee spot in the city. Celebrated for its warm, community-driven atmosphere and friendly staff, Coffee Lab has quickly become a local favorite, earning the prestigious Coffee Business Award in 2023 and securing a Travellers’ Choice accolade by consistently ranking within the top 10% of Manchester venues. Guests regularly praise the shop’s inviting vibe, making it a standout destination for casual sips, focused work, or cozy meetups.

The coffee menu at Coffee Lab Withington is a direct testament to third-wave values, with expertly crafted options like rich Flat Whites, smooth Americanos, and classic Greek coffees that honor the franchise’s heritage. Their curated offerings extend beyond the cup, with standout pastries—especially the baklava and other Greek treats—drawing rave reviews alongside fresh, locally-sourced cakes and popular breakfast choices. Whether you’re indulging in an award-winning specialty blend, savoring a signature mocha, or exploring their rotating single-origin features, Coffee Lab Withington’s commitment to quality and hospitality ensures an authentic experience with every visit.

name: Passion Fruit Coffee Roastery
excerpt_short: Manchester's boldest coffee. Ethically sourced, expertly roasted, community-centered.
excerpt_long: Manchester's boldest coffee. Ethically sourced, expertly roasted. A community hub where quality meets character. Experience the soul of coffee.
summary: Manchester's boldest coffee. Passion Fruit Roastery: ethically sourced, expertly roasted. A community hub with vibrant energy, serving exceptional single-origins.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasts, ethical sourcing, community hub, vibrant vibe
about:
Passion Fruit Coffee Roastery: Manchester’s Boldest Sip of Soul

Nestled in the heart of Old Trafford, Passion Fruit Coffee Roastery stands out as a beacon for specialty coffee lovers seeking both quality and community spirit. This Manchester roastery is deeply committed to sustainability and ethical sourcing, curating a dynamic, seasonal selection that champions traceability and freshness. Their portfolio spans organic single-origins like Flor de Selva and Cojín Seasonal Espresso, with regular new arrivals such as harvests from Finca Bella Elizabeth and Guatemalan micro-lots, crafted in partnership with renowned green coffee collaborators. Whether you favor filter, espresso, or decaf, every offering is roasted with a balance of passion and precision that has earned them enthusiastic acclaim amongst locals and connoisseurs alike.

What truly sets Passion Fruit apart is its neighborhood warmth—it's more than a coffee stop; it’s a gathering place pulsing with vibrant energy and inclusivity. Expect a bar brimming with expertly brewed black and white coffees, fresh pastries, and specialty tips (their seasonal subscriptions are a local favorite). While details about the founders remain low-key, their imprint is unmistakable in the roastery’s uncompromising commitment to quality and their reputation for open, friendly service. Visit for the coffee, stay for the character—Passion Fruit Coffee Roastery is a cornerstone of Manchester’s specialty scene.
address: 25 Ayres Rd, Old Trafford, Stretford, Manchester M16 9WA

name: Bold Street Coffee University Green
excerpt_short: Liverpool's coffee soul, now in Manchester. Specialty roasts, brunch, and good vibes.
excerpt_long: Liverpool's Bold Street Coffee, now at University Green. Specialty coffee, gourmet brunch, and a warm, urban vibe. Everyone's welcome!
summary: Liverpool's coffee soul in Manchester! Bold Street Coffee at University Green: specialty brews, gourmet brunch, and a warm, urban vibe.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, urban vibe, gourmet brunch, quality focus
about:
The Soul of Specialty: Bold Street Coffee University Green

Nestled at University Green, Bold Street Coffee brings the spirit of Liverpool’s beloved coffee culture to Manchester’s energetic Oxford Road corridor. With roots as one of Liverpool’s most acclaimed specialty cafes, Bold Street Coffee’s University Green outpost pulses with a warm, urban vibe—inviting everyone from students to local professionals seeking more than just a caffeine fix. Here, the focus is unwavering on quality: expect carefully chosen specialty coffee from their own BSC House blend alongside rotating guest roasts, each brewed to perfection whether you’re craving a creamy flat white, robust filter, or adventurous single-origin espresso.

The food menu elevates the classic brunch experience with gourmet sandwiches (the famed “Buoy” range in its many variations), vibrant egg bowls, granola, and super porridge. Baked goods and pastries are always fresh, while vegetarian, vegan, and high-protein options cater to every lifestyle. Awarded the prestigious Travelers’ Choice accolade for consistently glowing reviews, Bold Street Coffee is both family and dog friendly—don’t miss the playful Babaccino and Puppaccino options. The open, stylish space and welcoming ethos make every visit memorable, whether you’re popping in for an espresso or lingering over brunch and conversation.

name: Foundation Coffee House
excerpt_short: Design-led coffee haven. Ethically sourced beans, expertly roasted. Manchester's creative hub.
excerpt_long: Manchester's design-led coffee haven. Ethically sourced beans, expertly roasted. Industrial design meets community spirit. A soulful coffee experience.
summary: Manchester's design-led coffee haven. Ethically sourced beans, expertly roasted. Industrial design meets community spirit. A soulful specialty coffee experience.
flavour_profile: Design-led, industrial chic, specialty coffee, community hub
about:
Foundation Coffee House: Manchester’s Design-Led Coffee Institution

Foundation Coffee House is much more than a place to grab your flat white—it's a Manchester institution that celebrates world-class coffee, iconic industrial design, and a genuine community spirit. Founded by the acclaimed local design studio NoChintz, Foundation’s spaces in the Northern Quarter and Whitworth Street are thoughtfully crafted tributes to Manchester’s industrial legacy, with brass ruler details, bolt head tiling, and modern, design-led interiors that strike a perfect balance between heritage and innovation. Each visit feels like stepping into a creative hub—full of light, energy, and the comforting aroma of expertly roasted coffee.

The coffee program is built on a foundation of quality and precision. Foundation sources and roasts its own beans, focusing on ethically sourced single origins and balanced blends, brewed to perfection by baristas who care about every extraction. You’ll find freshly ground Origin coffee alongside a rotating filter menu, pour-overs, and all the classics. Their all-day food menu is packed with nourishing breakfasts, healthy brunches, pastries, and vegan-friendly options, all prepared fresh to order. Regulars rave about the brunch offerings and the signature Espresso Martinis. Whether you’re after a quiet corner, an event space, or lively community gatherings like film nights and brew clubs, Foundation Coffee House delivers a soulful specialty coffee experience that’s authentically Manchester.
address: Sevendale House, Lever St, Manchester M1 1JB

name: Just Between Friends
excerpt_short: Northern Quarter gem. Cozy, intimate, serving Assembly coffee. Exceptional quality, authentic vibe.
excerpt_long: Northern Quarter's gem: Just Between Friends. Cozy hideaway serving Assembly's finest beans. Exceptional coffee, authentic vibe, and simple, delicious treats.
summary: Northern Quarter gem. Cozy hideaway serving Assembly roastery's finest. Intimate, authentic vibe. Exceptional coffee, simple menu, and genuine warmth. A true specialty experience.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, cozy atmosphere, quality focus, indie gem
about:
Just Between Friends: Manchester’s Northern Quarter Gem

Tucked away on Tib Street in Manchester’s vibrant Northern Quarter, Just Between Friends exudes genuine specialty coffee soul. The warm, minimalist interior and inviting outside tables create the perfect hideaway for both quick catch-ups and slow mornings. This independent coffee shop is known for its cozy, intimate atmosphere—complete with friendly service and a curated menu that draws coffee lovers from across the city. Just Between Friends proudly serves meticulously selected beans from London’s renowned Assembly roastery, offering a refined espresso menu and daily batch brew that spotlight origin character and roast precision. Their focus on quality is unmistakable, a fact reflected in rave reviews touting exceptional coffee and a truly authentic vibe.

The coffee portfolio here is intentionally tight, prioritizing depth over breadth—think seasonally rotated single origins and sophisticated, well-balanced blends, always brewed with care via espresso or batch methods. Complementing the stellar coffee is a select offering of breakfast classics, toasted sandwiches, and homemade cakes, all served with genuine warmth. Though not a hotspot for pastry overload, the menu’s simplicity highlights their dedication to doing the basics perfectly. While Just Between Friends doesn’t tout major awards or industry fame, its reputation for integrity, atmosphere, and coffee excellence has quickly marked it as a must-visit for those seeking a true specialty experience in Manchester.
address: Beehive Mill, Jersey St, Ancoats, Manchester M4 6JG

name: Mancoco Coffee Bar, Roastery & Barista Training School
excerpt_short: Hidden under arches, ManCoCo roasts specialty coffee with soul. Authentic brews, a true Manchester haven.
excerpt_long: Beneath Manchester's arches, ManCoCo roasts ethically sourced, small-batch coffee. A haven for authentic brews and a creative vibe.
summary: Beneath Manchester's arches, ManCoCo roasts ethically sourced, small-batch coffee. A decade of passion, blending industrial charm with unforgettable brews.
flavour_profile: Artisan roasts, railway arch charm, expert brews, cozy vibe
about:
ManCoCo: Manchester’s Hidden Coffee Haven Under the Arches

Tucked away beneath the iconic railway arches of Hewitt Street, ManCoCo is Manchester’s true specialty coffee sanctuary for those seeking soul, authenticity, and expertly crafted brews. This independent coffee roaster and artisan bar has been a quiet force in the city for over a decade, blending industrial charm with a warm, inviting atmosphere. Beyond its distinctive location, ManCoCo’s team of seasoned coffee specialists passionately hand roast small-batch, ethically sourced Arabica coffees from traceable single-origin farms and cooperatives worldwide. The venue’s laid-back, creative vibe makes it a beloved escape from the bustle, where regulars and newcomers alike are greeted by baristas who genuinely care about making every cup memorable.

ManCoCo’s portfolio is rich and ever-evolving, featuring both signature blends—like the lively Manchester ManCoCo Blend, perfect for all-day sipping—and a rotating selection of single-origin coffees, ensuring there’s always something new for the adventurous palate. Coffee highlights range from classic espresso drinks to unique house specialties, such as their trailblazing iced coffee with tonic, orange, and double espresso. Pair your cup with freshly baked pastries or tempting cakes; the decadent brownies and toasted paninis are particularly popular. ManCoCo’s commitment to quality and transparency has earned it consistently top reviews and Travelers’ Choice recognition, cementing its reputation as a must-visit for coffee lovers in Manchester. Whether you’re after a flawless flat white, interested in beans to take home, or keen to brush up on your barista skills, ManCoCo’s genuine character and expertise make it an essential stop on the UK specialty coffee map.

name: Idle Hands Coffee
excerpt_short: Specialty coffee sanctuary. Indie soul.Meticulously sourced beans, legendary pies. Manchester's coffee heartbeat.
excerpt_long: Manchester's indie coffee haven. Sourcing top roasters, serving exceptional brews & legendary pies. A true specialty coffee sanctuary.
summary: Manchester's indie coffee haven. Idle Hands: specialty coffee sanctuary with top-tier roasters. Discover unique brews, legendary pies, and a creative vibe.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, artisan roasters, homemade pies, indie vibe
about:
Idle Hands: Manchester’s Heartbeat of Specialty Coffee & Pie

Idle Hands, nestled in Manchester’s dynamic Northern Quarter, pulses with genuine indie soul—an inviting hub for both coffee devotees and casual sippers. Established after a series of beloved pop-ups and setbacks, Idle Hands finally found its permanent home on Dale Street, cultivating a loyal following with its relaxed, creative vibe and minimalist, quirky interior. This spot is much more than a café; it’s a true specialty coffee sanctuary, celebrated for championing the third-wave movement and welcoming guests to experience meticulously sourced beans from some of the world’s top artisan roasters. The ever-changing coffee portfolio features names like Hard Lines, La Cabra, Tim Wendelboe, Square Mile, and more, meaning there’s always something new to discover in the cup—whether you’re craving a nuanced single origin espresso, an adventurous filter brew, or a pitch-perfect flat white known to be one of Manchester’s best.

Idle Hands doesn’t just shine for its stellar coffee; its legendary homemade sweet pies are a destination in themselves, beckoning with options like vegan cherry, chocolate cream, pecan, and salted caramel apple—all ready to pair with your brew. The kitchen also delivers some of the city’s most raved-about brunches, including what fans call Manchester’s best avo toast and a range of vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free offerings. Regularly recognized in customer reviews and local best-of lists, Idle Hands’ commitment to quality and craft is unwavering. Whether you’re working remotely, meeting friends, or simply searching for inspiration, this café’s easy atmosphere and dedication to coffee excellence ensure every visit feels both special and authentically Mancunian.

name: Ancoats Coffee Co.
excerpt_short: Manchester's coffee heartbeat. Award-winning roastery, ethically sourced, community-focused.
excerpt_long: Manchester's coffee heartbeat. Award-winning roastery in historic Royal Mills. Ethically sourced beans, expertly roasted. A true coffee experience.
summary: Manchester's coffee heartbeat. Award-winning roastery in historic Royal Mills. Ethically sourced beans, expertly roasted. A community hub for serious coffee lovers.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasts, industrial chic, community hub, coffee craft
about:
Ancoats Coffee Co: Manchester’s Roasted Heartbeat

Tucked inside the historic Royal Mills, Ancoats Coffee Co. pulses with the vibrant soul of Manchester’s specialty coffee scene. Founded by Jamie Bolands after his immersion in Melbourne’s café culture, this independent roastery is a local institution—a hub where coffee craft, heritage, and community intertwine. Ancoats’ reputation goes far beyond its rich industrial past: nominated for Coffee Shop of the Year at the Manchester Food and Drink Awards and lauded with industry accolades like the Coffee Spot Best Roaster/Retailer Award, it’s a destination for serious coffee lovers and casual sippers alike. The exposed brick, natural light, and inviting vibe create a warm stage for a coffee program that’s as innovative as it is approachable.

The heart of Ancoats is its meticulously roasted portfolio: from their signature Warehouse espresso blend to a rotating cast of up to nine single origins sourced from ethical growers worldwide, every cup tells a story of provenance and care. Enjoy espresso, decaf, or a featured single origin on bar, with three single-origin filters changing daily—curious guests can often sample anything in stock. Wholesome food, cakes, and artisan pastries round out the offering, with standout vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free options. Whether attending a public cupping, a home brewing class, or simply soaking up the cozy atmosphere with a perfectly pulled shot, Ancoats Coffee Co. delivers an immersive, award-winning coffee experience rooted in Manchester’s revivalist spirit.
address: Unit 9, Royal Mill, 17 Redhill St, Ancoats, Manchester M4 5BA

name: Party At Pavilion by The Roasting Party
excerpt_short: Aussie coffee spirit in London. Bold flavors, good vibes, ethically sourced, and expertly brewed.
excerpt_long: Aussie coffee culture in Chelsea! The Roasting Party: bold flavors, ethical sourcing, and good vibes. Enjoy award-winning coffee and fresh pastries.
summary: Aussie coffee culture meets London charm. Ethically sourced, bold flavors, and good vibes. Enjoy award-winning coffee and fresh pastries.
flavour_profile: Aussie vibes, bold flavors, ethical sourcing, laid-back cafe
about:
Party At Pavilion by The Roasting Party: Where Aussie Vibes Meet London’s Specialty Scene

Nestled in Chelsea, Party At Pavilion by The Roasting Party brings the spirit of Australia’s specialty coffee culture to the heart of London. Founded by seasoned coffee experts Kirby Sinclair, Wesley Cusick, and Ed Cutcliffe—all veterans of Sydney’s renowned Little Marionette—the Roasting Party roastery is celebrated for its dynamic, bold flavors and a welcoming “good vibes, great friends” ethos. The team is deeply invested in ethical sourcing, forging strong relationships with traders and curating coffees from South and Central America, East Africa, and South East Asia. Their line-up excels with both vibrant single origins and robust blends like the Drake Espresso, lauded for smooth honey, roasted hazelnut, and cocoa notes. Sustainability is a core value, reflected in their eco-friendly packaging and support for reusable solutions.

The café’s laid-back, easy-going atmosphere invites both regulars and newcomers to linger over expertly pulled espresso or a meticulously crafted filter brew. Alongside their award-winning specialty coffees, you’ll find a tempting spread of fresh pastries, including croissants, pain au chocolat, and their signature banana bread—best enjoyed toasted (if you catch the grill at the right time). Whether you’re after a morning pick-me-up or a leisurely afternoon treat, Party At Pavilion stands out for its character, community spirit, and uncompromising dedication to the coffee craft.

name: Heart & Graft Coffee Roastery
excerpt_short: Manchester's coffee haven. Bold flavours, community spirit, and a playground for coffee lovers.
excerpt_long: Manchester's coffee haven. Bold flavours, community spirit. James & Sean's roastery: a playground for coffee lovers. Cupping sessions & delicious treats.
summary: Manchester's Heart & Graft: Where coffee passion thrives. Bold flavours, community spirit, and expert guidance await. A must-visit for coffee lovers.
flavour_profile: Specialty roasts, community hub, education, bold flavors
about:
Heart & Graft Coffee Roastery: Manchester’s Playground for the Coffee Curious

Tucked away in Manchester, Heart & Graft Coffee Roastery champions the true spirit of specialty coffee and community. Founded by James, a pioneer with roots in Manchester’s early specialty coffee scene, and Sean, once the UK’s youngest Q Grader and a seasoned green coffee buyer, this roastery has become the go-to haven for coffee obsessives seeking bold flavours and a place to share their passion. Inside, expect reclaimed wood and glass, a vibe that’s as welcoming as it is distinctive, and a direct line into Manchester’s evolving specialty coffee culture. Heart & Graft isn’t just a roastery; it’s an education hub, offering cupping sessions and guidance for home brewers eager to master their craft.

The coffee line-up dazzles: single origins like Colombia El Jaragual, Guatemala Yinta Witz, Kenya Rukira AB, and Rwanda Akegera Ikizere showcase the team’s sourcing chops and commitment to cup quality. Their portfolio highlights archetypal flavour profiles and includes subscriber favourites for those who crave discovery. While the main draw is clearly the coffee—both espresso and batch brew—Heart & Graft also serves up unlimited toast and cakes, making it ideal for a laid-back morning or a deep-dive into new origins. Whether you’re new to specialty or a seasoned grafter, Heart & Graft’s blend of expertise, warmth, and community spirit makes it a must-visit in the North’s coffee landscape.
address: Heart & Graft Coffee Ltd, 30 Holyoak St, Manchester M40 1HB

name: Ozone Coffee - Shoreditch
excerpt_short: Shoreditch's Ozone: Two decades of roasting excellence. Ethical, sustainable, and community-focused coffee.
excerpt_long: Ozone Coffee: Shoreditch's heart of specialty coffee. Two decades of roasting excellence, ethical sourcing, and a vibrant community hub.
summary: Ozone Coffee: Two decades of roasting excellence. Ethically sourced beans, roasted on-site. A vibrant hub for coffee lovers, serving exceptional brews and brunch.
flavour_profile: Specialty roasts, farm-to-cup ethics, vibrant atmosphere
about:
Ozone Coffee Shoreditch: Where Specialty Coffee Finds Its Soul

Stepping into Ozone Coffee in Shoreditch is like entering the beating heart of specialty coffee culture—where every detail reflects over two decades of roasting excellence and a fierce commitment to ethics and sustainability. Founded by New Zealand coffee pioneers, Ozone has become a fixture in London’s vibrant coffee scene since opening on Leonard Street in 2012. Here, coffee is not just a beverage but a craft: beans are roasted on-site, and each cup is a testament to their relationship-driven sourcing model. Ozone’s dedication has earned them the Most Sustainable Coffee Brand title at the European Coffee & Hospitality Awards and recognition as a leading independent café and specialty roaster. The vibe is warm and bustling, with baristas who make even the most complex pour-over feel approachable.

The coffee portfolio is a playground for enthusiasts and curious sippers alike, featuring a rotating menu of standout single origins—think Peru’s organic El Cedro and Bolivia’s Volcán Del Tigre—as well as expertly curated house blends. Alongside top-tier espresso and filter offerings, Ozone’s all-day kitchen serves up brunch classics, from rich eggs Benedict to French toast, catering to all dietary needs with vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free options. House specialties like their signature blends and limited edition reserves shine best when enjoyed with their artisanal pastries. For a deeper coffee experience, ask the team for brewing tips or the story behind your beans—they’re passionate about sharing the journey from farm to cup, making Ozone not just a café, but a true community hub for coffee lovers.

name: Kobean Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Rare micro-lots, ethically sourced. Manchester's coffee sanctuary, celebrating producers and community.
excerpt_long: Discover rare micro-lots at Kobean Coffee Roasters. Ethically sourced, experimental coffee, crafted with passion in Manchester. Community, flavor, and sustainability.
summary: Discover rare, experimental coffee at Kobean. We champion ethical sourcing, showcasing award-winning producers. Experience a progressive, community-focused coffee haven in Manchester.
flavour_profile: Rare microlots, experimental roasts, local treats, community
about:
Kobean Coffee Roasters: Manchester’s Sanctuary for Rare & Experimental Coffee

Tucked away in Heaton Moor, Kobean Coffee Roasters breathes new life into Manchester’s coffee scene with a soul rooted in collaboration, sustainability, and the celebration of rare micro-lots. Founded by Kobe, a passionate coffee aficionado inspired by European coffee cultures, Kobean’s ethos is all about honoring the stories behind the beans. The shop’s focus is on showcasing young, innovative, and legendary producers—think award-winning names like Diego Bermudez, Nestor Lasso, and emerging stars such as Alfredo Castaño—whose meticulously crafted microlots shine thanks to pioneering methods like anaerobic and controlled fermentations, thermal shock, and fruit infusions. Each cup highlights Kobean’s commitment to ethical sourcing, transparency, and environmentally conscious, biodiverse farming techniques, ensuring both amazing flavour and real traceability.

The vibrant, take-away focused space is as much about building community as it is about coffee, partnering with top local bakeries like Pollen, Longbois, and Half Dozen Other for a stellar rotation of pastries and baked treats. Alongside a revolving menu of experimental coffees, guests can enjoy in-house sandwiches, brioche, and premium grab-and-go snacks, all curated with the same eye for quality and sustainability as the coffee itself. Whether you’re a devoted specialty coffee enthusiast or a curious newcomer, expect a progressive, ever-changing experience that celebrates both the producer in the cup and the local flavor on the counter.
address: 3 Hawthorn Grove, Heaton Moor, Stockport SK4 4JA

name: Salford Roasters
excerpt_short: Salford Roasters: Bold coffee, industrial soul. Award-winning beans, ethically sourced. Proper coffee.
excerpt_long: Salford Roasters: Where industrial grit meets specialty coffee. Ethically sourced, award-winning beans, roasted with passion. Experience 'Proper Coffee'.
summary: Salford Roasters: Where industrial grit meets specialty coffee. Ethically sourced, award-winning beans, roasted with passion. Experience coffee excellence.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasts, ethical sourcing, local vibe, award-winning
about:
Salford Roasters: The Heartbeat of Salford’s Specialty Coffee Scene

Salford Roasters radiates the spirit of its city, blending industrial heritage with a bold, modern vision for specialty coffee. Founded in 2016 by Nik, whose coffee journey spans the bustling cafés of Athens to Manchester’s Northern Quarter, this independent roastery is built on inclusivity, transparency, and a genuine love for ‘Proper Coffee’. With a roastery that champions social value and ethical sourcing, Salford Roasters partners directly with top-tier producers and importers, ensuring fairness and excellence for growers and customers alike. Their house blends and single origins are a true celebration of global terroirs—each roast thoughtfully profiled to draw out unique flavors and crafted to suit every palate.

Salford Roasters’ coffees have earned prestigious recognitions, including Great Taste Awards and Cup of Excellence distinctions, reflecting their relentless pursuit of quality. Their portfolio features a curated range of award-winning coffees, each marked by traceability and depth. As a Specialty Coffee Association certified operation at the Professional level, they serve up everything from filter and espresso classics to creative specialty brews. Beyond coffee, expect tasty pastries and locally-inspired treats, all served with warm Northern hospitality. Whether you’re chasing the next competition-winning cup or seeking a soulful caffeine fix, Salford Roasters stands out as a beacon of craft, community, and coffee excellence.
address: Unit E, Protector Lamp Business Park, Salford Roasters, Lansdowne Rd, Eccles, Manchester M30 9PH
hours_weekday: Mon - Thu: 9:00 AM - 3:45 PM, Fri: 9:15 AM - 4:00 PM

name: FWD:Coffee
excerpt_short: London's vibrant hub for specialty coffee. Distinctive, connected, and always exceptional.
excerpt_long: FWD:Coffee: London's vibrant hub for specialty coffee. Expertly crafted espresso, inspiring spaces, and a community where creativity thrives.
summary: FWD:Coffee: London's vibrant hub for specialty coffee. Expertly crafted espresso, inspiring spaces, and a community where creativity and connection thrive.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, vibrant atmosphere, craft expertise, London hub
about:
FWD:Coffee – London’s Living Room for Specialty Coffee Lovers

Step into FWD:Coffee and you’ll instantly feel the magnetic energy that makes this specialty coffee destination a local favorite across Central London. With four locations woven into the cultural fabric of the city, FWD:Coffee is more than a café—it’s a vibrant social hub where creativity and conversation flow as smoothly as their expertly crafted espresso. The eclectic decor and curated soundtracks set an inspiring tone, while the friendly, deeply trained baristas serve thousands of meticulously prepared cups each week, welcoming everyone from creative professionals to curious regulars. FWD:Coffee’s identity is unmistakable: always distinctive, always connected to its neighbourhood’s unique soul.

At the heart of the experience is a commitment to true specialty coffee, anchored by their exclusive FWD:Coffee blend—delicate, balanced, and perfectly tuned for espresso or your favorite milky drink. Their lineup features standouts like the ethereal Brew, FWD:Espresso, and Phantom Brew decaf, available both in the shop and for home enthusiasts via subscriptions. Every barista undergoes rigorous sensory and technical training, ensuring an unparalleled cup every time. While their fame lies in the cup, you’ll also find a tempting selection of pastries and even carefully curated wines at their Kingsland Road site. FWD:Coffee isn’t just a place to drink coffee—it’s where London comes to think, create, and connect, one exceptional shot at a time.

name: The Gentlemen Baristas
excerpt_short: London's finest: Tradition meets modern craft. Award-winning coffee, crafted with care.
excerpt_long: London's coffee heritage meets modern craft. Award-winning roastery, known for its commitment to sustainability and traceable, stellar coffee.
summary: Experience London's finest. The Gentlemen Baristas blend tradition with modern craft. Award-winning coffee, sustainable sourcing, and a warm welcome await.
flavour_profile: London heritage, craft roasting, diverse blends, hospitality
about:
The Gentlemen Baristas: Where London’s Coffee Heritage Meets Modern Craft

Step inside The Gentlemen Baristas and you’ll instantly feel the character that sets this specialty coffee roastery apart. Founded in 2014 by Henry Ayers and Ed Parkes—well-known figures in London’s specialty scene—the brand has built its reputation on a blend of tradition, hospitality, and award-winning coffee. Their spacious East London roastery anchors the operation, housing both a cupping room and an acclaimed barista training school. With a legacy of accolades and a robust wholesale network that supports Michelin-starred kitchens, The Gentlemen Baristas are renowned for their commitment to sustainability and traceability, crafting each “hat”—their term for coffees—with precision and care.

The coffee portfolio is a true celebration of diversity, from the vibrant and clean Top Hat single origin, to signature blends like the smooth Deerstalker and the dark, powerful Gatsby. Seasonal exclusives, such as the rare Brazilian Pink Bourbon, highlight their forward-thinking sourcing philosophy. Espresso, filter, and V60 methods are all meticulously executed, ensuring a stellar cup whatever your brew style. Their cafes, each with a distinct ambience—whether cozy or expansive—offer not just coffee, but an array of fresh pastries and British-inspired food. For an insider’s tip, don’t miss their Racer from Rwanda for something punchy and distinct, or join their subscription for curated rotating selections. This is a haven for coffee lovers seeking substance, soul, and the refined warmth of true London hospitality.

name: Roasting Plant Coffee
excerpt_short: Fresh-roasted coffee, brewed to order. Experience the Javabot™ difference. A sensory coffee playground.
excerpt_long: Experience coffee like never before. Fresh-roasted, brewed-to-order, and bursting with flavor. A sensory playground for coffee lovers.
summary: Experience coffee like never before. Fresh-roasted, brewed-to-order, in under a minute. Discover unique beans, from Jamaican Blue Mountain to Ethiopian Yirgacheffe.
flavour_profile: Fresh-roasted, Javabot, artisan, innovative, sensory experience
about:
Roasting Plant Coffee: A Fresh-Roasted Revolution in the Heart of London

Step into Roasting Plant Coffee and you’re instantly immersed in a sensory playground where the aroma of just-roasted beans fills the air and the whoosh of the patented Javabot™ system signals something truly special. Founded by coffee visionary Mike Caswell—whose deep industry roots span from pioneering work at Coffee Connection to a senior executive role at Starbucks—Roasting Plant fuses artisan passion with cutting-edge engineering. Every store features the Javabot™, an on-site micro-batch roasting system that roasts and brews each cup to order in under a minute, ensuring unmatched freshness and flavor clarity. You’ll find a portfolio that ranges from the world-renowned Jamaican Blue Mountain and Hawaii Kona to single-origin gems like Ethiopian Yirgacheffe and Sumatra Royal Badger. Each bean is sourced by Director of Coffee Genevieve Kappler, a leading expert renowned for her meticulous palate and commitment to quality.

This is not your typical café. The vibe is energetic, open, and interactive—beans travel through pneumatic tubes overhead, inviting guests to witness the roasting process firsthand. The menu offers a range of custom blends and single origins, all available as espresso, filter, or cold brew. House specialties include limited-reserve pours and carefully crafted pastries to complement the nuanced profiles of their coffees. Whether you’re grabbing a quick cup or lingering to watch the roasting magic, Roasting Plant promises a uniquely flavorful journey in a space that’s both innovative and authentically passionate about specialty coffee.

name: The Fleet Street Press
excerpt_short: Historic London café. Ethically sourced, in-house roasted specialty coffee. Award-winning beans.
excerpt_long: Fleet Street Press: London's iconic specialty coffee hub. Ethically sourced, in-house roasted beans. Historic space, rich espresso, and a taste of London's legacy.
summary: Fleet Street Press: London's coffee icon. Housed in a former tobacconist, serving ethically sourced, in-house roasted specialty coffee. Experience award-winning beans and London's coffee culture.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, historic charm, press heritage, ethical sourcing
about:
Fleet Street Press: London’s Iconic Specialty Coffee Hub

Stepping into Fleet Street Press, you’re immediately immersed in the unique soul of a café deeply connected to London’s storied press heritage. Housed in a former tobacconist, the space reveals hidden rooms, locked safes, and charming period features, all beneath a sweeping curved glass frontage and historic staircase. Founded in 2013, Press Coffee & Co set out to redefine the specialty café experience, focusing on ethically sourced, high-quality coffees roasted in-house. The Fleet Street Press, now a flagship location, showcases a house Press Blend—perfect for milk drinks—alongside refined single origin espressos and filter coffees that reflect the most progressive coffee origins worldwide. Notably, they are a proud member of the Specialty Coffee Association, recognised for both consistency and standout flavor.

At the bar, skilled baristas extract rich, nuanced espresso on their La Marzocco machine, with batch-brewed filters and guest roasts regularly on offer. The menu is complemented by homemade baked goods and sought-after pastries, while their hot chocolate from Kokoa Collection and premium loose leaf teas round out the experience. Sustainability runs deep, with zero-emission delivery and compostable packaging. Don’t miss the rotating gallery of London press photography, a nod to the area’s legacy, or the chance to pick up beans and brewing gear for home. Whether you’re seeking a flawless flat white, award-winning beans, or simply a cozy landmark to savor a slice of London’s coffee culture, Fleet Street Press delivers at every sip.

name: Alchemy Café - The City
excerpt_short: London's Alchemy Café: Specialty coffee, meticulously roasted. Soulful space, exceptional brews since 2013.
excerpt_long: City of London's specialty coffee haven. Since 2013, Alchemy Café offers exceptional, hand-roasted brews in an intimate, creative space.
summary: Alchemy Café: City of London's specialty coffee haven. Since 2013, serving meticulously sourced, hand-roasted beans. Exceptional brews, warm welcome, and a touch of magic.
flavour_profile: Artisanal coffee, vibrant flavors, cozy ambiance, alchemy-inspired
about:
The Alchemy Café: City of London’s Modern Classic

Tucked away on a quiet stretch of Ludgate Broadway, just steps from St Paul’s Cathedral, Alchemy Café is the City’s specialty coffee institution with unmistakable soul. Since 2013, this bright, intimate space—distinguished by its symbolic wooden counter etched with the alchemical signs for Earth, Air, Fire, and Water—has welcomed professionals, locals, and wanderers alike with an exceptional brew and a warm, knowledgeable greeting. The vibe is one of understated, creative energy, supported by passionate baristas eager to talk through Alchemy’s ever-rotating lineup of direct trade, small-batch coffees, each cup reflecting meticulous sourcing and hand roasting that’s made them a mainstay in London’s specialty scene.

Coffee lovers can expect two espresso options, a single-origin pour-over, and batch brew, with past offerings spanning vibrant Kenyan beans—think fruity acidity and lively notes—to rich Costa Rican cold brews served in vintage medicine bottles for a playful twist. Aeropress, cold brew, and a full retail bean range ensure every palate is covered. The pastry counter is equally tempting, offering buttery croissants and inventive savory muffins to pair with your cup. The window seats are perfect spots for people-watching, while takeaway regulars enjoy quick, crafted service. Whether you’re diving into coffee’s nuances with the barista or grabbing a perfectly executed flat white, Alchemy Café embodies the city’s specialty spirit—refined, welcoming, and always a bit magical.
address: 8 Ludgate Broadway, City of London, London EC4V 6DX
hours_weekday: Mon & Fri: 8:00 AM - 3:00 PM, Tue - Thu: 8:00 AM - 4:00 PM

name: Electric Coffee Co
excerpt_short: Ealing's coffee pioneer. Freshly roasted beans, warm vibes, and exceptional coffee since 2008.
excerpt_long: Ealing's coffee pioneer. Electric Coffee Co: exceptional coffee, warm hospitality. Freshly roasted beans, vibrant blends, and a relaxed vibe since 2008.
summary: Ealing's coffee pioneer. Electric Coffee Co: exceptional coffee, warm hospitality. Freshly roasted beans, vibrant single origins. A beloved mainstay since 2008.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, local roasting, vibrant atmosphere, Ealing gem
about:
Electric Coffee Co: Ealing’s Pioneer of Specialty Coffee Culture

Step into Electric Coffee Co, and you’ll sense a rare energy—part sunlit neighborhood retreat, part specialty coffee institution. Founded by Simon and Oksana, frustrated by the lack of quality coffee in West London, Electric has become a beloved Ealing mainstay since 2008, holding fast to its original vision of exceptional coffee and warm hospitality. Their freshly roasted beans, once produced under Automaton Precision Roasters and now proudly carrying the Electric Coffee Co name, are featured in every cup, with guest blends and rotating single origins (like their acclaimed Kenyan) keeping the selection vibrant and ever-evolving. The café’s prominent presence has earned consistent acclaim: inclusion in The Independent’s Top 50 Coffee Shops, listings in London’s Best Coffee guides, and the coveted Travelers’ Choice distinction with Tripadvisor, plus a “Best Coffee Spot near a Railway Station” award in 2015.

The vibe is effortlessly relaxed—think expansive glass windows, window-bar seating for prime people-watching, and a spacious interior spilling out onto Haven Green. Coffee is crafted on a Mirage Veloce espresso machine, one of the few in the UK, underscoring Electric’s commitment to both heritage and innovation. Beyond espresso, guests can enjoy filter, decaf, cold brew, and loose leaf teas, with all beans available as whole or ground for various brew methods. Food offerings shine, from breakfast and brunch to lunch and pastries; the sourdough toast is a local favorite. Whether you’re a daily commuter or a casual visitor, the staff’s warmth and the unmistakable pulse of quality and care make Electric Coffee Co a destination for true coffee lovers and a cornerstone of Ealing’s evolving food scene.

name: Matthew Algie | London Coffee School
excerpt_short: London's coffee haven. Matthew Algie: Crafting excellence since 1864. Taste, learn, and master coffee.
excerpt_long: London's heart of coffee mastery. Matthew Algie, since 1864, blends tradition with innovation. SCA-accredited, empowering every coffee lover.
summary: London's heart of coffee mastery. Matthew Algie, since 1864, blends tradition with innovation. SCA-accredited school, ethical sourcing, and award-winning roasts.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, barista training, ethical sourcing, craft roasting
about:
Matthew Algie | London Coffee School: Where Coffee Craftsmanship Comes Alive

Nestled in the heart of London, Matthew Algie isn’t just a coffee stop—it’s a vibrant hub for specialty coffee mastery. With roots tracing back to 1864, this iconic roaster has shaped the UK’s specialty coffee scene, blending tradition with innovation. The London Coffee School buzzes with energy, hosting barista training in a state-of-the-art campus that’s SCA-accredited—a mark of coffee excellence recognized globally. Their team of passionate coffee gurus invites both professionals and enthusiasts to dive deep into the art of extraction, from espresso perfection to flawless milk steaming. The vibe here is inclusive and empowering, with every cup echoing the motto: “We are more than coffee roasters, we are coffee masters.”

Matthew Algie stands out with a diverse coffee portfolio, featuring filter and espresso blends, single origins, and three acclaimed triple-certified coffees (Fairtrade, Rainforest Alliance, Organic) that highlight their commitment to ethical sourcing and sustainability. The range includes award-winning blends roasted on traditional Probat drum roasters, with a promise of freshness—delivering within seven days of roasting. Beyond the beans, you can expect an enticing café experience with expertly crafted drinks and thoughtful accompaniments. Whether you’re here to refine your barista skills or savor a perfectly balanced cup, the London Coffee School is a cornerstone of specialty coffee culture, guided by a legacy that empowers every coffee lover to savor and succeed.
address: Business Design Centre, Suite, 233 52 Upper St, London N1 0QH
hours_weekday: Mon - Thu: 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM, Fri: 8:30 AM - 4:30 PM

name: Camden Coffee Roastery
excerpt_short: Camden Coffee Roastery: Where passion brews. Specialty coffee, community, and warmth in every cup.
excerpt_long: Camden Coffee Roastery: Where tradition meets innovation. Savor global beans, expertly brewed. A vibrant hub for coffee lovers.
summary: Camden Coffee Roastery: Where tradition meets innovation. Founded by two Algerian fathers, serving specialty coffee, pastries, and community spirit in Camden Market.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasts, cozy ambiance, global flavors, community hub
about:
Camden Coffee Roastery: Heart & Soul of Camden’s Specialty Coffee Scene

Step into Camden Coffee Roastery and you’ll feel the pulse of London’s independent coffee culture. Founded by Jamal and Smail—two Algerian fathers whose devotion to coffee, pastry, and hospitality is matched only by their commitment to community—this roastery is more than a café. It’s a vibrant meeting place where tradition meets innovation. Nestled in Camden Market, their cozy shop radiates warmth with comfortable seating, inviting lighting, and an atmosphere that welcomes both locals and travelers. Every detail, from sourcing sustainable beans to eco-friendly practices, reflects the founders’ ethos of authenticity and generosity. Camden Coffee Roastery stands out as Central London’s first specialty coffee roastery, dedicated to making top-tier coffee accessible to all.

The coffee portfolio is a global journey—expect single-origin discoveries and signature house blends, all roasted in small batches to showcase each bean’s character. Whether you’re craving a rich espresso, creamy latte, or precise pour-over, every cup is meticulously brewed by passionate baristas. Alongside exquisite coffee, the menu features hand-crafted pastries, making it an ideal spot for both quick stops and leisurely moments. Don’t miss house specialties or ask for a custom brew—the team loves to share their expertise. Camden Coffee Roastery’s reputation for quality, sustainability, and genuine hospitality makes it a must-visit for coffee lovers seeking an authentic London experience.
hours_weekday: Mon - Thu: 7:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Fri: 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM

name: La Tostadora Roastery + Coffee
excerpt_short: Authentic specialty coffee. Ethically sourced, traceable beans. Crafted with passion.
excerpt_long: La Tostadora: Specialty coffee, ethically sourced. Dora, "the roaster girl," connects farmers to London. Discover traceable, soulful coffee.
summary: La Tostadora: Specialty coffee, ethically sourced. Founded by "the roaster girl," Dora. Discover unique origins, traceable beans, and a community-driven vibe.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasts, farm-to-cup transparency, cozy community hub
about:
La Tostadora Roastery + Coffee: Where Coffee’s Story Comes Alive

Step into La Tostadora Roastery + Coffee and discover a specialty cafe that vibrates with authenticity, transparency, and heartfelt connection to coffee’s roots. Founded by Dora—affectionately known as “the roaster girl”—La Tostadora is more than a coffee shop; it’s a bridge between passionate farmers and London’s thriving specialty coffee scene. Dora’s hands-on approach, forged by time on coffee farms, infuses the space with a rare sincerity. The café’s mission centers on making the journey from farmer to cup visible, ensuring every sip is traceable and every bean ethically sourced. Inside, the ambiance is warm and welcoming, favored by locals and digital nomads alike for its open, community-driven spirit and laptop-friendly vibe.

The coffee portfolio at La Tostadora is a treasure map of distinct origins and pioneering processes. Expect to find micro-lots and limited releases like Rwanda Musasa Dukunde Kawa Red Bourbon, Colombia’s “Funky & Fermented” Anibal Diaz Thermal Shock Typica, and rare Ecuadorian espresso roasts—all quality-checked by certified Q Graders. Meticulously crafted flat whites and V60 pour-overs have earned them top ratings with specialty enthusiasts. La Tostadora also supports women-led initiatives (like their decaf from Colombia’s El Trebol Women Association) and offers a thoughtful pairing of artisan pastries and snacks to complement each cup. Whether chasing a fruity, juicy filter or a bold, expertly-pulled espresso, this is a destination for those who crave coffee with soul, story, and a sense of discovery.

name: Guillam Coffee House
excerpt_short: London's gem. Globally sourced, expertly roasted specialty coffee. Unique experience.
excerpt_long: London's Guillam Coffee House: Globally sourced, expertly roasted specialty coffee. Experience unique single-origin beans in vibrant Notting Hill, Bayswater, and more.
summary: Discover Guillam Coffee House: London's specialty coffee haven. Globally sourced beans, expertly crafted. Experience unique flavors in Notting Hill, Bayswater & Gloucester Road.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, global origins, vibrant atmosphere, expert craft
about:
Guillam Coffee House: London’s Specialty Coffee Gem with Global Roots

Guillam Coffee House stands out in London’s specialty coffee scene, celebrating the art of single-origin coffee at its vibrant locations in Notting Hill, Bayswater, and Gloucester Road. Recognised as one of London’s Notable New Cafés at the London Coffee Festival 2022 and featured at the World Coffee Summit 2023, Guillam has earned a place among the city’s best according to guides like Best Coffee Guide and European Coffee Trip. Each Guillam space is infused with the unique energy of its neighbourhood, offering an experience as distinctive as their globally sourced beans. The team includes authorised Specialty Coffee Association trainers, ensuring each cup is crafted with care and knowledge.

Guillam’s coffee portfolio is a journey through the world’s finest origins: from the chocolate-laced Brazil House Bean and the fruit-forward Yirgacheffe (Ethiopia), to the bold notes of Sumatra’s Gajah Mountain and the vibrant Kainamui (Kenya). The ever-evolving selection is matched with a curated array of pastries, cakes, artisanal breads, and sandwiches, making Guillam a go-to for both coffee purists and brunch enthusiasts. Guests rave about the calm atmosphere, attentive service, and a menu that champions sustainability and traceability—ensuring every visit is a celebration of global coffee culture at its finest.
address: 24 Gloucester Rd, South Kensington, London SW7 4RB

name: Meletius Speciality Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: London's heart of specialty coffee. Craft, community, and exceptional brews await.
excerpt_long: London's heart of specialty coffee. Meletius: Crafting exceptional blends & single origins. Community, quality, and innovation in every cup.
summary: London's heart of specialty coffee. Meletius: where craft meets community. Savor expertly roasted beans, from the "Spread the Love" blend to single origins. A welcoming space for all.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasts, vibrant flavors, cozy urban oasis
about:
Meletius Speciality Coffee Roasters: Where Craft Meets Community in Central London

Tucked away in the heart of High Holborn, Meletius Speciality Coffee Roasters is where London’s specialty coffee scene finds both innovation and heart. With a roasting plant in Wembley and a beautifully designed cafe on Warwick Court, Meletius combines years of expertise with a warm, contemporary vibe. The passionate team pours their dedication into every batch, treating coffee roasting as a meticulous craft—resulting in blends and single origins that highlight the world’s finest beans. Signature offerings like the “Spread the Love” blend (caramel, milk chocolate, Brazil nut, and stone fruit notes) and standout single origins from Costa Rica (with layers of caramel, black tea, grapefruit, and raspberry) show Meletius’s commitment to both approachable and adventurous palates. Their Swiss water–processed decaf boasts full flavour with none of the caffeine, winning converts among discerning drinkers.

Meletius isn’t just about top-tier coffee; the café delivers a holistic experience with its selection of sweet and savoury treats thoughtfully paired to accentuate each brew. The atmosphere is welcoming—ideal for both quiet reflection and lively conversation. Their champion-led barista courses and commitment to sustainability underscore a drive to uplift London’s coffee culture from bean to cup. Whether you’re a seasoned aficionado or a curious newcomer, Meletius invites you to discover specialty coffee in a space that values quality, innovation, and genuine connection.
hours_weekday: Mon - Fri: 7:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Tue - Thu: 7:00 AM - 6:00 PM

name: Hermanos Colombian Coffee Roasters – Angel Lane
excerpt_short: Colombian soul under the arches. Specialty coffee, roasted with passion. Experience the Hermanos spirit.
