Recognized by local media as one of Chicago’s hottest new cafés, Tasa’s inviting, woman-forward atmosphere and social responsibility ethos set it apart—making every cup an experience worth savoring and supporting.

name: Sip of Hope Coffee
excerpt_short: Specialty coffee, crafted with purpose. Every sip supports mental health. Logan Square's heart.
excerpt_long: Logan Square's Sip of Hope: Specialty coffee with a purpose. Every cup funds suicide prevention & mental health. Expertly crafted, deeply meaningful.
summary: Sip of Hope: Logan Square's specialty coffee haven. Every cup fuels suicide prevention & mental health. Expertly crafted coffee, community, and purpose.
flavour_profile: Specialty coffee, mental health, community, purpose-driven
about:
Sip of Hope: Specialty Coffee with a Purpose in Logan Square

Step into Sip of Hope and discover a specialty coffee experience unlike any other in Chicago. Nestled in Logan Square, this café isn’t just serving up expertly crafted espresso and pour-overs—it’s igniting a movement. Founded by Jonny Boucher, also known for launching the acclaimed nonprofit Hope for the Day, Sip of Hope is the world’s first coffee shop where 100% of the proceeds fund proactive suicide prevention and mental health education. Every barista is trained in Mental Health First Aid, ensuring genuine support and resources are as much a part of your visit as your cup of coffee. Walls are lined with mental health resources, and the environment is equal parts cozy, welcoming, and deeply purposeful, making it a hub for community connection and conversation.

Sip of Hope’s coffee program is powered by a close partnership with renowned local roaster Dark Matter Coffee. You’ll find their signature “Sip of Hope” blend alongside rotating offerings and fan favorites like the Chocolate City iced coffee blend on tap. The menu features classic coffee drinks, specialty beverages, and an array of baked goods and pastries. For those seeking more than just caffeine, house specialties like expertly prepared pour-overs or an iced chocolate coffee are sure to impress. Whether you’re grabbing a cup to go or settling in for a heartfelt conversation, Sip of Hope invites you to enjoy excellent coffee while supporting a vital cause—one cup at a time.

name: Kapéj Coffee Roasters
excerpt_short: Old Town's Kapéj: Hand-roasted specialty coffee, crafted with heart. Cozy, delicious, and welcoming.
excerpt_long: Old Town's Kapéj: Where specialty coffee meets heartfelt craftsmanship. Cozy vibes, single-origin beans, and a warm welcome await.
summary: Old Town's Kapéj: where specialty coffee meets heartfelt craftsmanship. Savor meticulously roasted beans, cozy vibes, and warm hospitality.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasts, cozy ambiance, craft beverages, community
about:
Kapéj Coffee Roasters: Heartfelt Craftsmanship in the Old Town

Kapéj Coffee Roasters, tucked away at 1447 N Sedgwick St in Chicago’s Old Town, is a haven for specialty coffee enthusiasts seeking both quality and comfort. Each coffee is meticulously evaluated and roasted on-site with a dedication that shines through every cup, offering a curated rotation of single-origin beans such as Burundi and Bravo. The moment you step in, you’re greeted by eclectic, cozy interiors—think leather sofas, brick counters, and a homey vibe that invites you to linger. The owner and staff are regularly praised for their warm, attentive hospitality, making both regulars and newcomers feel welcome.

Beyond the coffee, Kapéj delights with a menu of generously sized sandwiches and creative beverages like the Iced Dulce de Leche Latte or Vanilla Lavender Latte, each crafted to balance flavor and artistry. Whether you’re settling in with a book by the fireplace or enjoying a chat with the barista, you’ll find Kapéj’s passion for specialty roasting and community creates an experience that’s as memorable as it is delicious.

name: Dark Matter Coffee - The Mothership
excerpt_short: Chicago's Dark Matter: Art, science, and subcultural coffee. Bold roasts, unique flavors, and cosmic vibes.
excerpt_long: Dark Matter Coffee: Chicago's subcultural coffee icon. Art, science, and individuality blend in every cup. Bold flavors, innovative brews, and community spirit.
summary: Dark Matter Coffee: Chicago's subcultural coffee icon. A cosmic blend of art, science, and individuality. Bold coffee, inventive specialties, and a community-driven philosophy.
flavour_profile: Cosmic vibe, bold blends, artisan roasts, boundary-pushing
about:
Dark Matter Coffee – The Mothership: Chicago’s Subcultural Coffee Icon

Step into Dark Matter Coffee – The Mothership and you’ll find yourself immersed in a cosmic blend of art, science, and unapologetic individuality. Founded by Jesse Diaz, a pioneer in Chicago’s specialty coffee scene, the Mothership isn’t just a coffee shop—it’s the pulsating heart of the Dark Matter universe. The space, inspired by the intrigue of submarines and adorned with vibrant local murals, doubles as a roastery and artistic haven, channeling the city’s creative spirit into every cup. Their commitment to traceability, innovation, and social responsibility shines through both their coffee and their community-driven philosophy, making them a go-to for seekers of both quality and character.

The coffee lineup here is bold and boundary-pushing, led by signature blends like Unicorn Blood—crafted for rich, Northern Italian-style shots—and their mind-bending Barrel Mocha, made with barrel-aged beans for an unforgettable flavor journey. You’ll find inventive specialties like the Agave Latte, sweetened with agave nectar and cinnamon, and the Cafe Miel, spotlighting honey sourced directly from Guatemalan partner farms. Regulars rave about their imaginative single origins, heat-extracted iced coffee, and a full menu of espresso classics served with precision. The Mothership’s reputation as one of the premier artisan roasters in the country is well-earned, earning accolades including the Travelers’ Choice award for its consistently stellar reviews, bold menu, and fierce dedication to coffee excellence.

name: Metric Coffee
excerpt_short: Chicago's Metric Coffee: Flavor, craft, and community, roasted with radical transparency.
excerpt_long: Experience Chicago's Metric Coffee: Flavor, craft, and community converge. Witness roasting, savor ethically sourced beans, and embrace the neighborhood vibe.
summary: Experience Chicago's Metric Coffee: Flavor, craft, and community converge. Witness roasting, savor ethically sourced beans, and embrace a welcoming atmosphere.
flavour_profile: Artisanal roasting, transparent sourcing, community hub
about:
Metric Coffee: Chicago’s Hub for Flavor, Craft, and Community

Nestled in the vibrant Kinzie Corridor, Metric Coffee’s West Fulton flagship café encapsulates both the artistry of roasting and the heart of community. The space seamlessly blends modern industrial and Scandinavian-inspired design, with blonde and pink slats, walnut countertops, and exposed brick creating a warm, welcoming atmosphere. Visitors are treated to a unique experience—a thoughtfully designed glass divider lets guests watch the roasting process in action, highlighting Metric’s commitment to craftsmanship and transparency. Open daily, the café offers a full menu of freshly roasted single-origin and blended coffees, cold brew, espresso, and baked goods from neighboring Brite Donuts and Bakedgoods, making it a true neighborhood destination.

Metric stands out for its dedication to quality and radical transparency. Sourcing seasonally from small producers and paying fair, above-market prices, they ensure each cup is a testament to ethical sourcing and exceptional flavor. Every batch is roasted with care—on antique Probat machines—and meticulously quality-checked before being served or shipped nationwide. More than just a coffee shop, Metric’s commitment to traceability, sustainability, and community shines through in every detail, making it a must-visit for both coffee aficionados and casual sippers alike.

name: Sputnik Coffee Company
excerpt_short: Back of the Yards: Bold, simple coffee. Community-focused, unforgettable flavor.
excerpt_long: Back of the Yards' gem: Sputnik Coffee. Bold, approachable medium roast. Community-focused, serving exceptional coffee & Belgian waffles.
summary: Back of the Yards' gem: Sputnik Coffee. Bold, approachable simplicity in every cup. Single, meticulously crafted medium roast blend. A true neighborhood gathering spot.
flavour_profile: Neighborhood gem, bold simplicity, balanced blend, community focus
about:
Soulful Simplicity in Chicago’s Back of the Yards: Sputnik Coffee Company

Sputnik Coffee Company radiates authenticity from its roots in Chicago’s historic Back of the Yards neighborhood. Founded in 2017 by brothers Vova and Greesha Kagan, Sputnik’s story is one of restless curiosity—what started as home roasting experiments quickly transformed into a beloved local institution. Eschewing specialty coffee dogmas, Sputnik pursues bold, approachable simplicity, offering a single, meticulously crafted medium roast blend. Each component—Colombian, Brazilian, and Sumatran beans—is roasted separately and post-blended to create a balanced, accessible cup. This dedication to consistency and flavor has earned Sputnik wide acclaim, including the prestigious title of “Best Coffee Shop” in Time Out Chicago’s Best of the City awards.

Sputnik’s vibrant cafe hums with the warmth of a true neighborhood gathering spot. Visitors are greeted by generous seating, views of a gleaming, state-of-the-art roastery, and the irresistible aroma of fresh coffee. Beyond the signature blend—available as espresso and decaf—customers can indulge in house-made Belgian waffles, an unexpected delight crafted in an imported waffle maker. Sputnik remains deeply connected to its diverse community, with coffee bags labeled in multiple languages and pricing that defies specialty coffee norms. Whether you’re a regular or first-time visitor, Sputnik’s soul lies in genuine service, an inviting space, and coffee that’s as memorable as its origin story.
